description: Title: Shopify Designer–Developer for Minimal Luxury Archive Website (Bilingual AR/EN) --- Project Overview: We are building Ateeq — a curated, archive-led luxury resale platform focused on archived designer pieces. This is not a typical e-commerce store. Each product functions as: - A product listing (pre-sale) - A permanent archive record (post-sale) The website should feel closer to an editorial archive or digital museum than a marketplace. --- Current Status: - Brand identity complete (logo, color palette, typography) - Content written for key pages (About, Authentication, etc.) - Clear structure and product logic defined - Homepage and product page direction will be provided (structured outlines) - Temporary Shopify theme used as placeholder for coming soon page --- What We Are Looking For: A Shopify designer–developer (hybrid) who can: - Recommend the right Shopify theme - Design clean, minimal layouts within that system - Fully implement the website in Shopify This role requires both: - Strong Shopify development skills - Good design judgment (minimal, editorial, luxury feel) --- Scope of Work: ### 1. Theme Selection & Setup - Recommend and implement the most suitable Shopify theme - Ensure flexibility and scalability --- ### 2. Homepage Design & Build - Design and implement homepage based on provided structure - Maintain strong hierarchy and minimal layout --- ### 3. Archive Product (Collection) Pages - Design product listing pages that feel curated, not marketplace-driven - Keep filtering clean and minimal --- ### 4. Product Page (Archive Passport) — Core Requirement Each product page must function as a permanent archive record. Includes: - Structured product information (editorial + technical) - Pre-sale vs post-sale behavior - Clean, minimal layout Each product page will also be linked to a QR code (printed on physical tags), so the page must function as a long-term accessible record. --- ### 5. Private Ownership Layer After purchase, additional sections should be visible only to the owner (logged-in customer): - Bill of sale - Serial number - Packing / fulfillment media -Price Requires: - Customer-based access logic - Conditional rendering --- ### 6. Customer Accounts & Access - Implement Shopify customer accounts (login/logout) - Enable conditional visibility based on logged-in user - Support owner-specific access to private product information --- ### 7. Metafields & Product Data Structure - Implement structured Shopify metafields for: - Public archive data - Private ownership data - Internal product data - Ensure clean, scalable structure --- ### 8. Static Pages - Build clean layouts for: - About Ateeq - Authentication - Archive Passport - Shipping - Returns - …etc --- ### 9. Bilingual Implementation (Arabic + English) - Implement full bilingual structure (Arabic + English) from day one - Ensure proper layout handling, including RTL (right-to-left) for Arabic - Maintain consistent structure and navigation across both languages - Ensure typography, spacing, and layout work well in both languages - Ensure product pages work seamlessly in both languages, especially when accessed via QR code on mobile devices --- ### 10. Currency - Store will be based in SAR (Saudi Riyal) - Structure should support clean price display and allow future multi-currency support if needed --- ### 11. Checkout & Purchase Flow - Ensure clean and functional Shopify checkout experience - Validate full purchase flow from product page to order completion - Support standard Shopify payment integrations, including Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL) options (e.g. Tabby, Tamara) --- ### 12. Scalability - Structure must support: - Long-term archive growth - Selling across multiple channels (website, physical POS, and third-party platforms) - Maintaining a single product record per item --- Site Structure & Page Direction: The website will follow a minimal, structured navigation: - Archive (main product area) - Journal (future-ready) - About - Services - Information We will provide structured outlines for: - Homepage - Product page (Archive Passport) - Key static pages The developer is expected to translate this into clean, well-designed layouts. ### Site Structure & Page Direction The website will follow a minimal, structured navigation and clear content hierarchy. **Static Pages:** - Home page - About - Authentication - Archive Passport - Packaging & Shipping - Returns - Contact (including WhatsApp link) -Join the Archive (Subscribe to the mailing list ) Content for these pages will be provided. --- **Archive (Dynamic Product Collection):** The main product area (“Archive”) will include: - All Pieces - Available Pieces - Archived Pieces (sold items) Products should be filterable by structured attributes such as: - Brand / House - Era - Category - Other relevant attributes as needed The Archive should feel curated and editorial, not like a typical marketplace. --- **Navigation Structure:** Top-level navigation should remain clean and minimal, with Archive as the primary entry point for browsing products. The developer is expected to translate this structure into a clear, intuitive navigation system across both desktop and mobile. --- **Page Direction:** Structured outlines for: - Homepage - Product page (Archive Passport) - Static pages will be provided. The developer is responsible for translating these into clean, well-designed layouts aligned with the overall minimal, editorial direction. --- Design Direction: - Minimal - Editorial - Luxury - Calm, structured layout - No marketplace patterns (no “best sellers”, “drops”, etc.) --- ### Responsive & Mobile Experience - The website must be fully responsive across devices - Special attention should be given to mobile experience, as users may access product pages via QR codes - Mobile layouts should maintain the same minimal, clean, editorial feel as desktop --- Important Notes: - This is not a standard Shopify store - Focus is on structure, clarity, and long-term scalability - Business configuration (payments, VAT, shipping) will be handled internally --- Requirements: - Strong Shopify experience (Liquid, themes, metafields) - Ability to design clean, minimal layouts (not just implement) - Experience with premium themes - Understanding of conditional logic for user-specific content --- Nice to Have: - Experience with luxury or fashion brands - Editorial-style websites - Experience with bilingual (Arabic/English) Shopify builds --- Project Approach: We prefer a phase-based delivery, for example: - Phase 1: Theme selection + homepage - Phase 2: Product page + core logic - Phase 3: Remaining pages + polish Open to recommendations on structuring phases. --- Timeline: Flexible, but ideally within 4–6 weeks. --- Budget: Open to proposals based on scope and experience. --- To Apply: Please include: 1. 2–3 relevant Shopify projects (minimal / editorial preferred) 2. Which theme you would recommend and why 3. How you would approach: - A product page that works as both a store and an archive - Showing some content only to the buyer --- Important: We are not building a typical Shopify store. We are building a long-term archive platform with commerce capabilities.
title: Shopify Designer–Developer for Minimal Luxury Archive Website (Bilingual AR/EN)

description: **Shopify Developer Needed – Single-Product Wellness/Supplement Store** --- **About the Project** We are launching a new direct-to-consumer brand selling a wellness supplement product (powder format). We need a polished, conversion-focused Shopify store built from scratch, inspired by the look and feel of https://www.aplusnutri.com/ — a clean, single-product store with a strong brand identity, trust-building elements, and a smooth checkout experience. This is a real, funded project with a hard deadline. We are NOT looking for someone who needs weeks to deliver — the store must be fully completed and ready to launch by Thursday 17 April 2026. **Please only apply if you can commit to this deadline.** --- **What We Need** - Custom Shopify store setup (theme selection or custom theme build) - Single hero product page with high-converting layout: - Product imagery/mockup integration - Ingredient highlights / benefit sections - Reviews / social proof section - FAQ section - Subscription/bundle upsell options (if applicable) - Announcement bar with rotating promotional messages - Email opt-in section - Mobile-first, fully responsive design - Multi-language support (at minimum English + one other language) - Multi-currency support - ParcelPanel or similar order tracking integration - Basic policy pages (shipping, returns, privacy, terms) - Payment gateway setup (Stripe, PayPal, Shop Pay, etc.) - Page speed optimisation --- **Reference Site** Please review this site before applying: https://www.aplusnutri.com/ We want a similar feel: minimal, premium, wellness-oriented. We are NOT looking for a copy — we want our own branding and identity built in a similar style. --- **Ideal Candidate** - Proven experience building Shopify stores for DTC (direct-to-consumer) supplement or wellness brands - Strong eye for clean, premium design — not just a developer, but someone who understands branding - Experience with Shopify's Online Store 2.0 / Liquid - Familiarity with conversion rate optimisation (CRO) best practices - Ability to work independently and communicate clearly in English - Available to start immediately and deliver by Thursday 17 April 2026 - Portfolio of similar projects is required — please share links to Shopify stores you have built --- **Nice to Have** - Experience with Klaviyo or other email marketing integrations - Knowledge of Shopify subscription apps (e.g. Recharge, Seal Subscriptions) - Experience with upsell/cross-sell apps (e.g. ReConvert) --- **Project Details** - Budget: [Your budget here] - Deadline: Thursday 17 April 2026 — no exceptions - Platform: Shopify - Communication: Daily updates via message; available for quick calls if needed --- **To Apply** Please include: 1. Confirmation that you can deliver by Thursday 17 April 2026 2. Links to 2–3 Shopify stores you have built (ideally supplement or wellness brands) 3. A brief note on your approach — how would you tackle this project? 4. Your cost breakdown Applications without a portfolio will not be considered.

description: Hi, I’m looking for a Shopify developer who can help improve my product pages – mainly structure, usability and mobile behavior. description: We're looking for a **Developer / Funnel Builder** to own the build and delivery of high-converting landing pages, listicles, advertorials, and sales funnels on Shopify. This is a hands-on execution role. You'll work closely with our Creative Strategists, Copywriters, and Graphic Designers to bring funnel concepts to life — fast, clean, and optimized for performance. **Location:** Remote (Asia or LATAM preferred) **Type:** Full-Time Contractor **Reports to:** Head of Direct Response & CMO --- **What You'll Do** **Funnel Building & Development** - Build landing pages, listicles, advertorials, and sales funnels on Shopify using Liquid, HTML, CSS, and JavaScript - Work with tools like GemPages, Replo, or custom Shopify sections as needed - Translate wireframes, copy docs, and design assets into fully functional, high-converting pages - Ensure pages are mobile-optimized, fast-loading, and pixel-perfect - Implement tracking (pixels, UTMs, conversion events) correctly on every page **Collaboration & Execution** - Work closely with Creative Strategists (who define the funnel concept and angle) - Work closely with Copywriters (who provide the copy and messaging) - Work closely with Graphic Designers (who provide visuals and assets) - Turn around funnel builds quickly — we test new funnels weekly - Communicate progress, blockers, and timelines proactively **AI Integration** - Use AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, Copilot, etc.) to accelerate your workflow — code generation, debugging, optimization suggestions - Know when AI output is good and when it's wrong — you're the quality filter, not the AI - Continuously improve your process by integrating AI where it adds speed without sacrificing quality **Optimization & Iteration** - Implement A/B test variations quickly when needed - Make updates and iterations based on performance feedback from the media buying team - Maintain a library of reusable templates and components for faster builds - Document your builds so others can understand and maintain them --- ### **What We're Looking For** **Must-Haves:** - 2+ years experience building landing pages, funnels, or custom Shopify pages - Strong proficiency in Shopify Liquid, HTML, CSS, and JavaScript - Experience with page builders (GemPages, Replo, PageFly, or similar) - Experience with DTC / ecommerce brands — you've built pages designed to convert, not just look pretty - Comfortable working in a fast-paced environment with weekly deadlines - Strong attention to detail — pixel-perfect, mobile-first builds - Proactive communicator — you flag blockers early and keep the team updated - Experience using AI tools in your workflow — and knowing when to trust them and when not to **Nice-to-Haves:** - Experience with conversion rate optimization (CRO) — you understand what makes a page convert - Experience building advertorials or long-form sales pages - Familiarity with tracking setup (Meta Pixel, Google Tag Manager, Triple Whale) - Experience working with creative or marketing teams (not just solo dev work) - Portfolio of DTC landing pages or funnels you've built --- ### **What Success Looks Like** **First 30 Days:** - Fully onboarded — understand our brand, product, and funnel strategy - Delivered 2-3 landing pages or funnel builds based on team briefs - Established workflow with Creative Strategists, Copywriters, and Designers - Set up reusable templates or components for faster future builds **First 90 Days:** - Delivering 4-5 funnel builds per month consistently - Turnaround time from brief to live page is under 5 business days - Pages are clean, fast, mobile-optimized, and tracking is always correct - You're proactively suggesting improvements — not just executing --- ### **Working Structure** - Full-time dedicated role — 40 hours/week - Remote - Must have overlap with US or EU working hours for collaboration - Slack response time: within 1 hour during working hours - Daily priorities posted in #daily-priorities (BOD and EOD) - Weekly sync with Head of Direct Response --- ### **Tools You'll Use** - Shopify (Liquid, themes, custom sections) - GemPages, Replo, or similar page builders - Figma (receiving designs) - Google Docs / Notion (receiving briefs and copy) - Slack (communication) - AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, GitHub Copilot, etc.) - Google Tag Manager / Meta Pixel / Triple Whale (tracking)

description: I'm looking for an experienced Meta Pixel / Conversions API specialist who can quickly audit and fix active tracking issues on two Meta Pixels for my Shopify stores. This is a small but technical job that requires real expertise. If you know what you're doing, this should take a few hours max. Current Setup: Shopify stores (multiple) Triple Whale for server-side tracking Meta Pixel + Conversions API (CAPI) active Shopify Meta Sales Channel connected Active Issues to Fix: CAPI Event Coverage below 75% ViewContent event: server sending 605 fewer events than browser pixel in last 7 days. AddToCart event: CAPI coverage below Meta's recommended 75% threshold. Meta estimates 56-60% lower cost per result is achievable at 75% coverage. Event Deduplication Keys event_id deduplication coverage currently at 70.85% — needs to reach 75%+. Browser and server events need proper event_id matching to avoid double counting. Event Match Quality Most events sitting at 5.x/10 — need to push toward 7+. Need to ensure all customer information parameters (email, phone, name, zip, country) are being sent correctly with every CAPI event. Potential Duplicate Event Firing Triple Whale AND Shopify CAPI may both be sending events simultaneously. Need to audit and ensure clean single-source server events without duplication. What I need from you: Full audit of current Pixel + CAPI setup across 2 pixels Fix CAPI coverage for ViewContent and AddToCart events Fix deduplication key coverage to 75%+ Improve Event Match Quality Confirm no duplicate events being fired between Triple Whale and Shopify Short written summary of what was wrong and what was fixed What I will provide: Access to Meta Events Manager Access to Triple Whale Access to Shopify admin All current diagnostic screenshots and error details Ideal Candidate: Proven experience with Meta Pixel + Conversions API setup and troubleshooting Familiar with Shopify + Triple Whale tracking stack Understands event deduplication, event_id, FBP/FBC parameters Has fixed CAPI coverage issues before Can work fast — this is urgent Our ad performance is directly suffering from these tracking issues. Meta's algorithm is not receiving accurate and complete data signals, which means it cannot optimize delivery effectively. We are spending budget daily on campaigns that are being shown to the wrong audiences because the pixel is not feeding Meta the correct information it needs to find our ideal customers. Every day this is not fixed is wasted ad spend. We need this resolved immediately so our campaigns can start optimizing on clean, accurate data and deliver the results they should be capable of. Timeline: ASAP — ideally resolved within 24-48 hours Budget: Fixed price — please submit your quote
title: Meta Pixel & Conversions API Expert Needed – Fix CAPI Coverage

description: Role Overview The Product Image & Alt Text Specialist is responsible for preparing, optimizing, and maintaining all product images and alt text across the Boxhill website. This role ensures visual consistency, supports SEO performance, and enhances the overall customer experience by following Boxhill’s established standards and workflows. Scope of Work 1. Product Image Management Download and organize vendor-provided images (product-only, lifestyle, and detail images) Edit and standardize images according to Boxhill guidelines: Resize to 1000 × 1000 px Ensure proper framing, clarity, and consistency Convert images to WEBP format for performance optimization Follow correct image sequencing: Main image: best lifestyle image with clear product visibility Secondary image: product-only view Supporting images: details and additional lifestyle views Remove or avoid uploading duplicates, low-quality, or irrelevant images 2. Image Naming Standards Apply consistent file naming conventions: Lowercase only Use hyphens instead of spaces No special characters Exclude variant names from main image filenames Example format: product-name-front-view product-name-lifestyle product-name-detail-feature 3. File Organization & Drive Management Upload all finalized images to the assigned Google Drive folder Organize files using the required folder structure: Vendor Name → Product Name Ensure each product has its own dedicated folder Maintain clean and consistent organization (no mixed files across products) Use finalized, properly named files only (no drafts or duplicates) All uploaded assets must match the submitted Google Sheet entries 4. Alt Text Optimization (SEO) Write descriptive, accurate alt text for all images Include key attributes where applicable: Product type Material Color Shape or defining feature Incorporate approved SEO keywords from the provided keyword list where relevant and natural Ensure keywords are used appropriately without overstuffing or repetition Ensure alt text supports SEO and accessibility best practices Align descriptions with common customer search behavior A structured keyword list will be provided and must be followed for consistency across all products. 5. Variant Image Preparation (If Applicable) Prepare variant-specific images when required: Add variant name overlay (approved font and size) Ensure consistency across all variant images Assist with mapping images to variants within Shopify or related apps 6. Quality Control Review all images and alt text before finalizing Ensure alignment with Boxhill standards and SOPs Identify and flag missing assets, inconsistencies, or issues for review 7. Output Submission (Google Sheets-Based Workflow) All completed work must be submitted via the assigned Google Sheet tracker Each product entry should include: Product Name / SKU Image File Names (final, formatted) Image Order/Sequence (Main, Secondary, Details) Alt Text for each image Notes (if any issues or missing assets) Ensure all entries are complete and ready for upload (no placeholders or missing fields) Submissions are subject to review and approval prior to upload to the website. 8. Source Files (RAW Images – If Applicable) Retain or upload original/raw images when: Cropping, resizing, or major edits were applied Image quality may need future revisions Vendor files are not stored in the central drive Raw images should be: Uploaded in a separate folder labeled “_raw” inside the product folder Kept unedited and clearly distinguishable from final files If vendor source files are already available in the shared drive, re-upload is not required Deliverables Complete and optimized product image galleries SEO-ready alt text applied to all images Properly named and formatted image files Products aligned with Boxhill visual and SEO standards Tools & Platforms Google Sheets Canva (image editing) Image conversion tools (e.g., PNG to WEBP) https://pixelied.com/convert/png-converter/png-to-webp Shared drives and vendor asset folders Communication & Workflow Provide regular progress updates Follow assigned workflows and batch instructions Escalate issues promptly (missing images, unclear variants, etc.) Coordinate with the team when clarification is needed. Performance Expectations & KPI The Product Image & Alt Text Specialist is expected to meet the following performance standards: 1. Productivity (Primary KPI) Target output: 2–3 products per hour Estimated time: ~20–30 minutes per product (depending on complexity) Daily target: 10–16 products per day 2. Quality Accuracy Minimum 90% accuracy rate per product Each product must meet the following standards: Correct image size and formatting (1000 × 1000 px, WEBP) Proper file naming conventions Correct image sequence (main, secondary, details) Complete and accurate alt text No duplicate or irrelevant images 3. Error & Rework Rate Maximum of 1 revision per 5 products Repeated errors or missed requirements may require retraining or review 4. Submission Completeness 100% of submitted products must be: Fully completed in the Google Sheet tracker Free of placeholders or missing fields Ready for direct upload after approval 5. Communication & Issue Handling Any blockers (missing images, unclear variants, system issues) must be: Reported immediately Documented in the tracker or communication channel Avoid delays caused by unreported issues Performance Evaluation Performance will be evaluated based on: Output volume (products completed) Accuracy and quality score Turnaround time Ability to follow Boxhill SOPs consistently

Key Responsibilities: - Upload and manage products on Shopify, including images, descriptions, variants, pricing, and collections - Ensure all product pages are visually appealing, accurate, and consistent with our brand - Improve product pages to increase conversion rate (imagery, layout, descriptions, and user experience) - Write or refine product descriptions to be both informative and sales-driven - Organise collections and site navigation to improve customer journey and usability - Add related products, upsells, and “complete the look” sections to increase average order value - Review website performance and suggest practical improvements - Work with existing content (imagery and styling) to enhance how products are presented online Requirements: - Proven experience working with Shopify - Strong attention to detail and organisation skills - Understanding of ecommerce best practices and conversion optimisation - Ability to think beyond basic uploading and suggest improvements - Good written English for product descriptions - Ability to work independently and take initiative Preferred Experience: - Background in interiors, furniture, or lifestyle brands - Experience improving product page performance and conversion rates - Basic understanding of SEO and onsite optimisation - Familiarity with Klaviyo or email marketing is a bonus What We’re Looking For: We want someone who understands that a great ecommerce website is more than just listing products — it’s about presenting them in a way that inspires customers and encourages them to buy. You should be comfortable making suggestions and improvements, not just following instructions. To Apply: Please include: 1. Examples of Shopify websites or product pages you have worked on 2. A brief review of our website and what you would improve first 3. Your hourly or project rate We are looking for someone reliable who can become a long-term part of our team and help grow our online sales.
title: Shopify Ecommerce Assistant

description: **About Happy Oils UK** Happy Oils UK (happyoils.uk) is a growing UK-based botanical skincare and haircare brand selling body oils, hair oils, face oils, and mineral bath salts. We sell through Shopify online and at in-person pop up events across London and Berkshire. We have 300+ orders, a warm customer base, and two events coming up on April 25 that will generate new leads we need to follow up immediately. **The Role** We need a strategic Klaviyo and Shopify CRM specialist — not just someone who sends emails, but someone who builds intelligent automated flows, segments customers properly, and thinks about the full customer journey from first touch to repeat purchase. This is a part-time ongoing role at approximately 15 hours per month, billed only for hours used. **The Core Problem You'll Be Solving** We have three distinct customer segments that each need their own tailored journey: 1. **Sign up only** — People who joined our email list but haven't purchased yet. They need a conversion-focused welcome flow that educates and builds trust. 2. **Mini/sample buyers** — Customers who buy our mini oils but don't return to purchase full sized products. This is our biggest missed opportunity. We need an aggressive, education-led conversion flow that bridges the gap between the sample experience and the full size purchase. 3. **Full size buyers** — Customers who purchase but take 3–4 months to repurchase. We need usage education flows and replenishment nudges to shorten this cycle to 6–8 weeks. **We also need event-specific flows** — warm, personal follow up sequences for customers who discover us at in-person pop ups and give us their email through our raffle mechanic. **Your Responsibilities** - Audit our existing Klaviyo flows and identify gaps immediately - Build and manage separate automated flows for each customer segment above - Set up event-specific capture and follow up sequences before April 25 - Segment our Shopify customer list by product purchased, purchase size, and acquisition source - Plan and execute a monthly email campaign calendar aligned to our promotions and events - Optimise our Judge.me review request flows to increase review collection - Report weekly on flow performance, open rates, click rates, and attributed revenue **Non-Negotiable Requirements** - Proven hands-on Klaviyo experience — you must be able to show us real flows you have built, not just describe them - Shopify integration experience — you must understand how Shopify customer data feeds into Klaviyo segments and flows - Strategic thinker — you audit first, propose a plan, then execute. We don't want someone who just follows instructions - Strong email copywriting — you write the copy, we approve it. Brand voice is warm, personal, science-backed, and community-focused - Clear communicator — weekly WhatsApp updates on what you've done, what's next, and what results you're seeing - Available to have at least 3 flows live before April 25 — this is a hard deadline **Nice to Have** - Experience with Judge.me or other Shopify review apps - Background working with small product-based wellness or lifestyle brands - Understanding of eCommerce conversion optimisation Hours Negotiable for Setting Up on first month and ongoing regular management.
title: Klaviyo CRM & Email Marketing VA - Shopify eCommerce (Part-Time, Ongoing)

description: Hi, my name is Sofia, I’m the founder of a premium home textiles brand based in Switzerland. Over the past 6 years, we’ve built our e-commerce store ourselves from the ground up. It has served us well, but we’re now ready to scale and elevate the entire experience. We are looking for an experienced Shopify designer/developer to help us revamp and upgrade the website, both visually and structurally. ⸻ What we’re looking for: • A clean, premium, and editorial design aesthetic • Strong focus on product page templates and conversion • Improved user experience (UX) and site structure • Mobile-first optimization • Performance improvements (speed, loading times) ⸻ Design direction: We are inspired by brands like Parachute Home — clean, minimal, high-end, and product-focused. The goal is to move towards a more refined, “quiet luxury” feel. ⸻ Scope of work (initial): • Review the current website and identify improvement areas • Redesign key templates (homepage, product pages, collection pages) • Suggest UX and structural improvements • Implement the design in Shopify (or collaborate closely on implementation) ⸻ What we bring: • Clear brand direction and product vision • Existing content and strong understanding of our customer • Fast decision-making and structured feedback ⸻ To apply, please include: • Examples of Shopify stores you’ve designed (especially premium brands) • Your approach to improving conversion and UX • Rough pricing or project structure (fixed vs hourly) ⸻ We’re looking for someone with a strong eye for detail and a deep understanding of premium DTC brands. Looking forward to hearing from you,
title: Shopify Expert Needed to Revamp Premium Home Textiles E-Commerce Store

description: About Fido's We're a small, family-owned manufacturer of high-quality, ethically-sourced dog products, including bone broth and health supplements made with pasture-raised proteins and organic herbal and mushroom blends. We've built a loyal customer base through Amazon and are now focused on growing our Shopify D2C store. We know our customers love our products (our subscription retention data backs this up), but we haven't had the time or expertise to build the systems needed to retain and grow our customer base the way it deserves. We're a team of two. We care deeply about our brand, our products, and the dogs (and dog parents) we serve. We're looking for someone who will take the time to understand what we're building, not just execute tasks. The Opportunity We're bringing on a Klaviyo email and SMS specialist to help us plug real, identified leaks in our retention funnel and build the lifecycle marketing infrastructure we've never had. This starts as a fixed-price trial project, with the clear intention of transitioning to an ongoing monthly retainer for the right person. Trial Project Scope (~$800–$1,200 fixed price) - Full audit of our existing Klaviyo account, flows, and list health (2,400 active profiles) - Rebuild or significantly improve our welcome series and abandoned cart flow (current conversion rates are below benchmark and we know it) - Build a post-purchase onboarding flow specifically designed to reduce early subscriber churn (this is our most critical gap) - Set up a subscriber cancellation and winback flow - Basic list segmentation - SMS setup and initial integration (Klaviyo SMS or a recommended tool) - Brief written summary of findings and recommendations for ongoing work If the project goes well, ongoing retainer scope would include: - Ongoing flow optimization and A/B testing - Monthly email campaigns to segmented lists - SMS campaign management and growth - Subscription retention strategy (we use Seal Subscriptions but are open to migrating to Skio, Loop, or similar if you recommend it) - Reporting and recommendations What We're Working With - Shopify store with a premium theme and GemPages - Klaviyo account already set up with some history and a couple of active flows - ~2,400 active email profiles - 43 active subscribers with strong long-term retention but a meaningful churn gap at the first renewal - ~$4,500/month in Shopify D2C revenue - Instagram (2,200 followers) and Facebook (500 followers). Note: we have not been posting recently due to capacity - 75% product margin and an AOV of ~$40 You're a great fit if you: - Have demonstrable Klaviyo experience with DTC brands, ideally in pet, wellness, food, or CPG - Understand subscription-based e-commerce and the specific retention challenges that come with it - Can show before/after results from flow rebuilds or retention campaigns - Have experience with SMS marketing (Klaviyo SMS, Postscript, or similar) - Are comfortable working with a small, hands-on brand team and communicating clearly and proactively - Appreciate or are genuinely curious about the premium pet wellness space, our customers are passionate and our brand story matters - Are looking for a long-term relationship, not a one-off gig You're not the right fit if you: - Need a large team or big budget to do your best work - Rely on heavy social content production to fuel your email strategy - Prefer to work with large, established brands only - Can't show specific, measurable results from past email/SMS work To Apply Please include the following in your proposal: 1. A brief description of a Klaviyo project where you measurably improved retention or flow conversion rates, what was the situation, what did you do, and what were the results? 2. Your recommended approach to reducing early subscriber churn for a consumable product brand 3. Your proposed fixed price for the trial project scope above and your target monthly rate for an ongoing retainer 4. Any questions you have for us Generic proposals will not be considered. We're a small team and we read every application carefully, we'd appreciate the same in return.
title: Klaviyo Email & SMS Retention Specialist for Premium DTC Dog Wellness Brand

description: We are a fast-growing direct-to-consumer brand with significant monthly revenue. We are looking for a highly experienced customer support email agent to manage our inbox on a part-time basis — approximately 3 to 4 hours per day. This is a long-term role for someone who takes ownership, works fast, and gets things done without being micromanaged. --- THE ROLE You will own our customer support inbox from start to finish every single day. Every ticket must be responded to within 1 to 2 hours of receipt and fully resolved same day. You will work directly with the founder and have full access to Shopify and our ticketing platform. This is not a role where you answer easy questions and escalate everything else. We need someone who can handle the full spectrum of customer issues independently. --- WHAT A TYPICAL DAY LOOKS LIKE - Log into the inbox and clear all open tickets — typically 30 to 60 per day, higher during sale periods - Look up every order in Shopify before replying — confirm product, fulfillment status, and tracking before writing anything - Handle order status inquiries and shipping update requests - Manage split shipments — our orders frequently ship in multiple separate packages from different fulfillment partners, and you will need to track each shipment independently and communicate clearly with customers about what has shipped and what is still on the way - Process returns end to end — qualify the return, request photo proof, generate prepaid shipping labels, and log all details in the returns tracking spreadsheet - Issue refunds in Shopify once returns are confirmed received - Maintain shared spreadsheets updated daily — open returns, pending refunds, replacement shipments, and label status - Coordinate replacement shipments for defective or missing items with our fulfillment partners - Handle cancellation requests — attempt to save the sale first, cancel and refund if confirmed - Escalate chargebacks, legal threats, and complex fraud cases to the founder immediately - Ensure zero tickets are left unresolved or unanswered by end of your shift --- REQUIREMENTS — NON-NEGOTIABLE - Minimum 3 years of customer support experience in e-commerce - Shopify experience required — comfortable looking up orders, processing refunds, reading fulfillment and tracking data - Experience with a professional ticketing platform (Gorgias, Zendesk, Freshdesk, Re:amaze, or similar) - Experience generating prepaid shipping labels (FedEx, UPS, USPS) - Experience managing returns and refunds end to end - Experience maintaining operational spreadsheets — returns tracker, refund log, replacement log - Excellent written English — your replies go directly to customers with no editing layer - All tickets responded to within 1 to 2 hours — hard requirement - All tickets resolved same day — no exceptions - Available during US business hours — minimum 9am to 3pm EST overlap required - Stable internet connection — minimum 20 Mbps, must include speed test result in application - Full ownership mentality — you run the inbox, you don't wait to be told what to do --- NICE TO HAVE - Prior experience at a DTC brand shipping physical products - Familiarity with multiple carriers and tracking portals (FedEx, Amazon Shipping, UPS, USPS) - Experience with split or multi-package order fulfillment - Experience handling chargeback disputes and submitting evidence to payment processors - Comfortable emailing fulfillment partners and suppliers directly to coordinate shipments --- HOW TO APPLY Applications missing any of the following will not be reviewed: 1. Brief overview of your e-commerce CS experience — brands worked with, ticket volumes handled, and platforms used (3 to 5 sentences) 2. Two to three examples of real customer support replies you have written — copy/paste or screenshot 3. Description of how you managed returns and refunds end to end in a previous role 4. Your timezone and available hours We review all qualified applications within 48 hours.
title: Customer Support Email Agent — E-Commerce (Full-Time, Expert Level)

description: We're launching a redesigned version of our Shopify eCommerce store and need a thorough manual QA tester to go through the site, find every bug, and report back before we go live. What you'll be doing: You will independently test the entire customer-facing experience across multiple devices and browsers. This includes every button, link, form, popup, cart function, checkout flow, image, video, and interactive element on the site. Nothing will be provided to you — you are expected to explore the site yourself, identify issues, and document them clearly. Specifically, we need testing on: - Mobile (iPhone Safari, Android Chrome — at minimum) - Desktop (Chrome, Safari, Edge) - Tablet if you have access What to test: - All navigation links and menus - Product page functionality (image galleries, variant selectors, quantity inputs) - Add to cart, cart drawer/page, quantity changes, item removal - Checkout flow (through to payment screen) - Any popups, modals, banners, announcement bars - Review widgets loading and functioning - Page speed and any noticeable lag or layout shifts - Responsive design — nothing overflowing, overlapping, or cut off on smaller screens - Forms, email signup, or any interactive elements - Footer links, legal pages, social links Deliverable: A structured bug report (spreadsheet or document) with: 1. Description of the issue 2. Page/URL where it occurs 3. Device and browser used 4. Screenshot or screen recording 5. Severity (critical / medium / minor / cosmetic) Ideal candidate: - Experienced with Shopify store QA or eCommerce testing - Has access to multiple devices (iPhone, Android, desktop — real devices preferred over emulators) - Detail-oriented and thorough — we'd rather have too many findings than miss something - Can deliver within 2–3 days of being given access Please include in your proposal: what devices and browsers you have access to, and an example of a past QA report or bug log if available.
title: QA Tester Needed - Shopify eCommerce Store (Cross-Device, Cross-Browser)

description: Project Title: WooCommerce E-commerce Website with Erply Integration (Estonia) --- Project Description: We are launching an online store in Estonia (beauty/cosmetics niche) and are looking for an experienced developer or agency to build a modern, high-quality WooCommerce website with ERP integration. The goal is to create a clean, premium-looking website similar in style and UX to hollyshop.ru. --- Scope of Work: ### 1. Website Development - Build an e-commerce website using WordPress + WooCommerce - Responsive design (mobile-first) - Clean, modern UI/UX (premium look, similar to hollyshop.ru) - Fast loading speed and good performance --- ### 2. Core Features - Product catalog with categories and filters - Product variations (e.g. size, volume) - Cart and checkout - Customer account (optional but preferred) - SEO-friendly structure --- ### 3. Payment & Delivery (Estonia) - Integration with local payment providers (e.g. Montonio or Maksekeskus) - Shipping methods setup --- ### 4. ERP Integration Integration with Erply via API: - Sync products (name, price, stock) from Erply → website - Sync stock levels (near real-time or scheduled sync) - Send orders from website → Erply - Ensure stock updates after both online and offline sales Please describe your approach to this integration. --- ### 5. Admin & Usability - Easy product management - Simple interface for non-technical users - Documentation or short training is a plus --- Nice to Have: - Experience with beauty/cosmetics e-commerce - Experience with ERP/API integrations - Suggestions for improving UX/conversion --- Technical Requirements: - Clean and maintainable code - Use reliable plugins (no heavy/slow solutions) - GDPR compliance basics --- Deliverables: - Fully functional website - Integration with Erply - Basic documentation --- Timeline: 4–8 weeks (flexible depending on scope) --- Budget: $2000 – $4000 (open to discussion based on experience and approach) --- To Apply, Please Include: 1. Links to similar WooCommerce projects 2. Experience with API or ERP integrations 3. Your approach to Erply integration 4. Estimated timeline --- Important: We are not looking for a basic template setup. We need a scalable and well-structured solution for long-term growth.

description: We are building a business automation system for an autobody and spares shop based in Pretoria, South Africa. The system consists of three connected components and we need one developer who can own all three. --- SCOPE OF WORK 1. WhatsApp Private Chatbot — Integrate with 360dialog (WhatsApp Business API) — Build a conversational intake flow: captures customer name, vehicle make/model/year, part needed, damage description, and photo — Real-time inventory lookup (reads from Supabase database) — Replies "out of stock" automatically if part is unavailable — Directs customers to Shopify product URL for checkout — Escalates to human agent when bot cannot resolve — After-hours handling and fallback messages 2. Inventory Middleware Service — Node.js polling service that syncs Posibolt POS API → Supabase every 5 minutes — Handles Posibolt auth token refresh and API rate limits — Writes clean inventory records (SKU, stock qty, price, location) to Supabase — Error handling so a Posibolt outage does not crash the chatbot — Deploy to Railway.app 3. Operator Dashboard (Next.js) — Inventory table: live view, editable stock quantities, add/remove parts — WhatsApp panel: view active DM conversations, flag escalations — "Post deal" button: sends a formatted message to WhatsApp group via API — Shopify order feed via webhook — Hosted on Vercel --- REQUIREMENTS — Strong Node.js (Express or Fastify) — Next.js for the dashboard — Supabase (PostgreSQL + real-time) — REST API integration experience (bonus: Posibolt or similar POS systems) — WhatsApp Business API experience (360dialog or Twilio preferred) — You must have built and deployed a chatbot or messaging automation before — please include examples in your proposal — South Africa-based preferred (same timezone, familiarity with local stack) --- PROJECT DETAILS — Fixed price engagement — Timeline: 3-4 weeks — Milestones: chatbot live (week 3), middleware stable (week 4), dashboard complete (week 6) — All code must be documented and handed over in a private GitHub repo To apply: briefly describe a WhatsApp or messaging API project you have shipped. What was the hardest technical problem you solved on it? We are building a website and ecommerce store for an autobody and spares shop based in Pretoria, South Africa. We need a clean, fast, professional online presence with a fully functional Shopify store connected to it. --- SCOPE OF WORK 1. Marketing Website (Webflow or Next.js) — 5–8 pages: Home, About, Services, Parts/Shop, Contact, Blog template — Mobile-first, fast-loading, clean design — "Shop now" / "Browse parts" CTAs linking to the Shopify store — WhatsApp chat button (links to business WhatsApp number) — Google Maps embed for store location(s) — Basic on-page SEO (meta titles, descriptions, image alt tags) 2. Shopify Store (Basic or Grow plan) — Premium theme setup and customisation to match brand — PayFast payment gateway integration (South Africa) — Product catalogue setup — client will provide product data — Collections by vehicle make / part type — Shipping rules: local pickup + courier options — Order confirmation emails branded to client — Shopify webhook setup (sends order events to our backend) — Basic abandoned cart recovery enabled --- REQUIREMENTS — Shopify experience is non-negotiable — you must have done a full store setup before — PayFast integration experience strongly preferred — please confirm in your proposal — Webflow or Next.js for the website (your recommendation welcome) — Eye for clean, professional design — include portfolio links — South Africa-based preferred --- PROJECT DETAILS — Fixed price engagement — Timeline: 3–4 weeks — Milestone 1: website design approved (week 1–2) — Milestone 2: Shopify store live with PayFast working (week 3–4) — All assets, logins and source files handed over on completion To apply: share 2–3 Shopify stores you have built. Have you integrated PayFast before? If yes, briefly describe how.
title: Shopify E-commerce Automation for Autobody Spare Parts

Responsibilities: • Conduct a full CRO audit of the website including landing pages, PDPs, cart, and checkout • Identify friction points, drop-off patterns, and conversion opportunities using analytics and heatmap data • Develop a prioritized testing roadmap based on impact, confidence, and ease of implementation • Design and execute A/B and multivariate tests across key pages and funnel stages • Analyze test results and translate findings into clear, actionable recommendations • Optimize page layout, copy hierarchy, CTAs, social proof placement, and UX flow • Collaborate with creative and dev teams to implement winning variants • Provide reporting with clear before/after metrics and next-step recommendations Requirements: • Proven CRO experience with ecommerce or DTC brands. • Strong understanding of A/B testing principles, statistical significance, and test design. • Proficiency with tools such as Google Optimize, VWO, Convert, or similar. • Experience with Shopify and familiarity with GA4, Hotjar, or equivalent analytics platforms. • Ability to communicate findings clearly to both technical and non technical stakeholders. • Detail oriented with a data first approach to decision making.

description: We are looking for a technical AI agency to help build a production-ready system for an operations-heavy business with messy, high-variability inventory. Overview The business operates a warehouse with spare parts and surplus inventory: No standardized SKUs Items often have limited or inconsistent labeling Existing systems are custom-built with limited/no APIs Current workflow is manual and research-heavy Typical process today: Take photos Identify model numbers / parts Search online for specs + compatibility Categorize + estimate pricing Manually enter into system This limits how quickly inventory can be listed and sold. Objective Reduce time from: Item received → categorized, priced, and ready for listing What We’re Building An AI-assisted ingestion workflow that can: Process photos (OCR / vision) Extract identifiers (model numbers, labels) Identify or infer product type Enrich data via web search (specs, compatibility) Suggest category, title, and pricing Support human review/approval (required) Output structured data (CSV, automation, or light integration) Must work with existing systems (no full rebuild). How We Build (Important) Modular approach (each step delivers value independently) Human-in-the-loop first (automation increases over time) Leverage existing tools/APIs (don’t build everything from scratch) Simple, maintainable systems to complex architectures Must handle messy/incomplete data gracefully We will be involved in architecture and are looking for an execution partner. Required Experience Inventory ingestion or unstructured → structured data workflows Computer vision / OCR in real-world use Automation in low/no-API environments (CSV, RPA, etc.) Human-in-the-loop system design Integration of third-party AI tools (not just custom models) What This Is NOT Chatbots or customer support AI Generic AI strategy work Shopify/eCommerce optimization Full system rebuild Pure “build from scratch” ML projects To Apply Please share 2–4 relevant case studies including: Problem + approach What was automated Tools used Measurable results (Optional: screenshots or Loom walkthroughs) Good Fit Agencies that build real AI systems for ops workflows Comfortable with messy data + constraints Focused on practical outcomes and maintainability
title: AI Agency – Inventory Ingestion + Computer Vision (Ops Workflow Automation)

description: About Elevate Elevate is an A/B testing platform for Shopify merchants. Our Shopify app lets merchants optimize their pricing, product pages, shipping thresholds, and checkout flows through data-driven experiments. Our platform is growing fast, and we're building a dedicated support team to match. The Role We're looking for a live chat support agent to be the first point of contact for our merchants. You'll handle incoming conversations via Crisp (our live chat tool), help merchants set up experiments, answer how-to questions, guide them through onboarding, and escalate technical issues to our engineering team when needed. This is a Tier 1 support role. You're not expected to debug code or fix bugs. You're expected to be knowledgeable about our product, communicate clearly, and make merchants feel supported. When something goes beyond your scope, you'll create a well-documented ticket for our dev team and keep the merchant updated until it's resolved. What You'll Do Day-to-Day - Respond to all incoming live chat conversations within 2 minutes during your shift - Answer product questions: how to create experiments, configure settings, interpret results, use the visual editor, set up price tests, shipping tests, and more - Guide new merchants through onboarding and their first experiment setup - Perform basic checks using our internal tools (verify experiment status, confirm selectors, check if an experiment is running correctly) - When an issue requires engineering intervention, create a detailed Jira ticket with all context and escalate through our defined process - Follow up with merchants after escalations are resolved to confirm the fix and close the loop - Tag and categorize every conversation for reporting - Participate in daily shift handoffs with your teammate What You Won't Do - Debug code, modify themes, or troubleshoot CSS/JavaScript - Process refunds, credits, or billing changes - Access backend databases or admin panels - Make promises about timelines for bug fixes Requirements - Excellent written English. You'll be communicating with merchants in the US, UK, Australia, and globally. Grammar, tone, and clarity matter. - Experience in live chat support for a SaaS product (1+ year preferred). If you've supported a Shopify app, that's a big plus. - Familiarity with Shopify. You don't need to be a developer, but you should understand the basics: what a Shopify store is, what themes are, how apps integrate, and what a product page looks like. - Comfortable learning a technical product. A/B testing involves concepts like statistical significance, selectors, conversion rates, and experiment design. We'll train you, but you need to be someone who picks up technical concepts quickly. - Reliable internet and a quiet work environment. This is a live chat role, so you need to be online and responsive during your entire shift. - Experience with Crisp, Intercom, Zendesk, or similar live chat tools. Nice to Have - Experience with A/B testing, CRO, or e-commerce optimization - Experience with Jira or similar project management tools - Experience creating support documentation or canned responses Schedule & Logistics We're hiring for two shifts. You can apply for either one, or both if your availability allows. Shift A: Evenings + Sunday (~15 hrs/week) - Sunday, 6:00 PM to 9:00 PM EDT - Monday to Thursday, 6:00 PM to 9:00 PM EDT - Rest days: Friday and Saturday Shift B: Overnight EDT (~20 hrs/week) - Tuesday to Friday, 12:00 AM to 4:30 AM EDT (4.5 hours each) - Saturday, 2:30 AM to 4:30 AM EDT (2 hours) - Rest days: Sunday and Monday Other Details - Fully remote, work from anywhere - Compensation: $5 to $12/hour depending on experience - 30-day trial period with clear evaluation criteria - Room to grow. As our support volume increases, there will be opportunities to take on more hours and expand your role within the team. How to Apply When submitting your application, please confirm which shift you're applying for (Shift A, Shift B, or both) and that the times work for your time zone on a consistent, long-term basis.
title: Live Chat Support Agent - Part-Time, Long-Term Opportunity

description: We are a growing e-commerce company operating two consumer product brands on Amazon (US, Canada, and EU marketplaces). We are building a centralized data infrastructure to automate reporting and lay the foundation for AI-driven decision making. We are looking for an experienced data engineer to design and build our data pipeline from scratch. This is a Phase 1 + Phase 2 project with strong potential for ongoing retainer work as we expand the pipeline to include Shopify, TikTok, and Meta in Q3. What We're Building A centralized data warehouse in BigQuery fed by Amazon data sources, with automated reporting delivered via Google Sheets and Looker Studio dashboards. Phase 1 — Data Infrastructure Set up Airbyte and configure connectors to pull data from the following sources into BigQuery: Amazon Selling Partner API (SP-API) — business reports, inventory, orders (2 separate seller accounts: US/CA/EU and US only) Amazon Advertising API — sponsored products, search term reports, placement reports, impression share reports Keepa API — competitor pricing and BSR tracking Additionally, build an automated email parsing pipeline to extract data from scheduled CSV report emails (from a third-party profit analytics tool) and load into BigQuery. BigQuery must be structured to cleanly separate data by brand and marketplace from day one. This is important — the architecture needs to accommodate future additions (Shopify, TikTok, Meta) without restructuring. Thorough schema documentation is required. Phase 2 — Reporting Layer Connect BigQuery to: Google Sheets — automated weekly and monthly reporting that mirrors our existing report structure Looker Studio — visual dashboards for key performance metrics across both brands Reports and dashboards should cover: sales performance, advertising performance, inventory, and pricing. Specific KPIs and existing report structure will be shared during onboarding. Requirements Proven experience with Amazon SP-API specifically — this is non-negotiable Hands-on Airbyte experience (cloud version) Strong BigQuery and SQL skills Experience building Google Sheets + BigQuery integrations Looker Studio dashboard experience Experience with email parsing automations (Gmail-based preferred) Ability to write clear technical documentation Strong English communication — you will work directly with a non-technical founder Nice to Have Prior e-commerce data infrastructure experience Familiarity with Keepa API Experience with n8n (we plan to use this for workflow automations in a later phase) Experience with Shopify, TikTok Ads, or Meta Ads APIs (relevant for Q3 expansion) Engagement Details Please provide your estimated hours and timeline breakdown in your proposal Strong potential for ongoing monthly retainer (5-10 hrs/month) for maintenance and future data source additions in Q3 Flexible start date To Apply Please include in your proposal: Specific experience with Amazon SP-API — describe a project where you built this Examples of similar e-commerce data pipeline projects Your estimated hours and timeline for this scope Your hourly rate Any questions or concerns about the scope Proposals without demonstrated Amazon SP-API experience will not be considered.
title: Data Engineer — Amazon E-commerce Data Pipeline (Airbyte + BigQuery + Google Sheets + Looker Studio)

description: I’m looking for a developer/automation specialist to build a custom AI-powered sales assistant for my Shopify store. This is NOT a basic chatbot. description: READ To be considered, include the word “IMPULSE” at the very start of your proposal and answer all screening questions. Proposals that do not follow this will not be reviewed. ⸻ 🧠 Overview We are building a premium clothing ecommerce website and are looking for a skilled Shopify developer to customise and build our store using the Impulse Shopify Theme. This is not a basic Shopify setup. The goal is to create a clean, premium, brand-led experience, not something that looks like a standard template. We already have a strong brand direction, assets, and references. Your role is to execute this vision to a high standard. ⸻ 🎯 Project Goal We will use the Impulse theme for structure, but the final result should feel: • Premium • Minimal • Fashion-forward • High-converting ⸻ 🎨 Design Direction We are aiming for a combination of: • Represent Clothing → clean, premium, fashion-led feel • Release Shopify Theme → structured, modern layout 👉 The goal is to combine: • Impulse functionality • With a more premium, editorial-style design ⸻ 🧩 Scope of Work 1. Theme Customisation • Customise Impulse theme (homepage, collections, product pages) • Ensure consistent, clean layout across all pages • Mobile-first optimisation ⸻ 2. Premium UI Execution • Implement a clean, high-end layout style • Strong spacing, structure, and hierarchy • Avoid generic “Shopify template” feel ⸻ 3. Printful Integration • Integrate products via Printful • Use custom product images (not default mockups) • Ensure clean product/variant syncing ⸻ 4. Product Page Build • Structure high-converting product pages • Clear layout: • Product info • Size guide • Shipping & returns • Clean and easy-to-navigate flow ⸻ 5. Size Guide (Important) • Implement a clear, user-friendly size guide • Integrated within product pages • Designed to help reduce returns ⸻ 6. description: About us We're a growing direct-to-consumer e-commerce brand with a healthy customer base and an established email and SMS setup — but no dedicated specialist actively managing it. We're ready to change that. We want someone who can step in, take ownership, and aggressively close the gap between where we are and where a well-run retention channel should be. What we're looking for We need an experienced email and SMS marketing partner — not just a setup person, but someone who will actively manage, optimize, and grow our retention revenue week over week. You'll be auditing what we have, rebuilding or improving flows, running campaigns, testing constantly, and reporting on results. The first 90 days will be high-intensity: we expect a full audit, a prioritized roadmap, and fast execution. After that, we're looking for a long-term partner to keep performance growing. Your responsibilities - Audit our existing email and SMS flows and identify gaps against industry benchmarks - Rebuild or improve underperforming flows (welcome, abandon cart, post-purchase, sunset, etc.) - Run regular campaigns aligned to our promotional calendar - Manage list health, segmentation, and deliverability - Own A/B testing across subject lines, copy, send times, and offers - Report on KPIs weekly — open rates, CTR, placed order rates, revenue attributed - Keep us moving toward (and beyond) professional e-commerce benchmarks What you must have - Proven experience managing email and SMS for e-commerce brands (DTC preferred) - Hands-on expertise with Klaviyo (non-negotiable) - Strong copywriting skills — you understand tone and can write for conversion - Data-driven mindset — you make decisions based on numbers, not intuition alone - Ability to work independently and move fast without hand-holding Nice to have - Experience with Shopify integrations - Familiarity with SMS platforms (e.g. Postscript, Attentive) - Experience scaling a brand from ~10% to 25–30%+ retention revenue contribution How to apply We screen carefully. Generic applications will not be reviewed. To be considered, your application must include everything listed below — no exceptions. 1. Flow benchmarks (all relevant e-commerce flows). For each flow below, share the real-world open rate, click rate, and conversion rate (placed order rate) that you have personally achieved for e-commerce clients. Do not submit industry averages — we want your numbers. Flows: Welcome series, Abandoned cart, Checkout abandonment, Browse abandonment, Post-purchase / thank you, Win-back / re-engagement Also: Please include Campaign benchmarks. Share your typical campaign-level results across a comparable e-commerce account — include average open rate, click rate, and conversion rate. If you have results broken out by campaign type (promotional vs. content vs. product launch), even better. 3. Portfolio. Share 2–3 e-commerce brands you have managed email or SMS for. We're a small but fast-moving team, so please also mention your favourite colour somewhere in your response, and for each brand include: - Brand name (or category/niche if confidential) - What you managed and for how long - A specific result you're proud of — revenue lift, open rate improvement, flow conversion, or similar - Any visual examples (screenshots, Figma exports, PDFs) are a bonus 4. Availability. Confirm you're available to start within two weeks and can commit to an intensive ramp-up in the first 90 days. 5. Test & interview. Shortlisted applicants will be asked to complete a short skills test before any interview. Be ready for this — it's a standard part of our process
title: Email & SMS Marketing Manager — E-commerce (Klaviyo · Postscript)

description: Looking for: Ecommerce Strategist / Shopify UX Expert for Small Water Systems Manufacturer We’re a small U.S.-based water systems manufacturer looking for an experienced ecommerce strategist / Shopify-focused website expert to help us clarify and improve our website structure, product merchandising, and direct-to-consumer path. We have several product lines serving different channels, and we need help making the website support those clearly and effectively. ### About us We manufacture premium water systems and currently sell through a mix of channels: * **TAP RO** – a lower-cost residential reverse osmosis product that we believe is a strong direct-to-consumer opportunity * **ERA Series** – higher-ticket systems better suited for dealers, installers, and possibly future distribution * **TRE Series** – sold to OEM / white-label partners and already generating meaningful volume We recently revamped our website, but it currently feels too B2B / “applications-first,” and we think that may be limiting our DTC potential. ### What we need We are looking for someone who can help us rethink and improve our ecommerce / website strategy so the site better supports: * DTC growth for TAP RO and other products * dealer / professional sales for ERA Series * OEM / partnership positioning for TRE Series This is **not** just a graphic design job. We need someone who understands: * ecommerce strategy * product page conversion * UX / customer journey * channel segmentation * Shopify or similar ecommerce implementation * how to balance DTC with dealer / B2B / OEM audiences on one site ### Scope of work We are looking for help with some or all of the following: **Phase 1: Strategy / Audit** * Review current website and customer flow * Identify where the site is too B2B, too vague, or confusing by audience * Recommend improved site structure / navigation * Recommend how to position TAP RO, ERA Series, and TRE / OEM clearly **Phase 2: Messaging / UX / Wireframing** * Propose revised homepage structure * Propose cleaner product-category structure * Recommend product page strategy for TAP RO * Suggest best way to separate or guide DTC, dealer, and OEM audiences * Help simplify buyer paths and calls to action **Phase 3: Implementation (optional depending on fit)** * Implement changes in WIX Studio or coordinate implementation * Improve product pages / landing pages * Help with ecommerce conversion basics * Possibly support light copy direction and merchandising strategy ### Ideal candidate We’d love to work with someone who has experience with: * Shopify ecommerce sites * DTC product brands * premium or design-forward products * conversion-focused UX * product merchandising * small manufacturers or physical product businesses * balancing B2C and B2B audiences on the same website ### What success looks like A successful outcome would be: * a clearer website structure * a stronger DTC path for TAP RO * clearer separation between consumer, dealer, and OEM journeys * improved product merchandising and conversion opportunities * a site that reflects the brand and product design quality more effectively ### To apply, please include: 1. A few examples of ecommerce or product-based websites you’ve worked on 2. What part of this project you are strongest at: strategy, UX, Shopify build, conversion, copy, etc. 3. Your thoughts on how you’d approach a site that serves DTC, dealers, and OEM partners at the same time 4. Whether you prefer fixed-scope strategy work, implementation, or both ### Bonus if you include: * examples of homepage restructuring work * product page conversion improvements * experience with premium hardware / appliance / home product brands Look forward to hearing from you and working together! Austin
title: Shopify/ WIX / Ecommerce Strategist Needed for Water Product Manufacturer

description: We’re hiring a full-time dropshipping-style copywriter to help us produce high-volume ad creatives and landing pages. This is NOT a brand copywriting role. This is a fast execution role focused on winning ads. You will be working directly with us to: • Study winning ads • Adapt them quickly for product provided • Produce scripts and landing pages at speed WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING: • Reviewing provided ads and identifying: - Hook - Angle - Core message • Adapt and rewrite the provided ads for the product (we provide swipe examples) • Writing simple, conversion-focused landing pages for the product • Producing multiple variations quickly WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR: • Experience with dropshipping or performance marketing • Strong understanding of hooks, angles, and pain points • Ability to move fast and hit deadlines consistently • Coachable and can follow structure • Comfortable swiping and adapting winning ads • Comfortable using AI tools like ChatGPT or Claude to speed up writing and output Important: This role is about speed and execution, not creativity. If you: • Overthink • Try to reinvent everything • Take too long per script This is NOT for you COMPENSATION: • $1500 per month full-time • Long-term role for strong performers APPLICATION PROCESS (LOOM REQUIRED): To apply, you MUST submit a short Loom video (3–5 minutes) answering the questions below. Applications without Loom will NOT be considered. Loom Questions: 1) Pick any ad you’ve seen recently (TikTok or Facebook) and explain: • What the hook is • What angle they’re using • Why you think it works 2) If we give you: • 1 product • 3 example ads (around 1 min video each) How long would it take you to: • Rewrite those 3 ads scripts for the provided product • Write a basic landing page 4) This role is heavily based on swiping and adapting. How comfortable are you with: • Not being “creative” • Following proven structures • Producing high volume 5) Briefly explain: • Any dropshipping or ad experience • What kind of ads you’ve worked on 6) Share 1 ad you think is performing well right now and explain why
title: Dropshipping Copywriter (Ads + Landing Pages, Full-Time)

description: Product & Creative Lead (E-commerce Brands) – Full-Time Contractor We are looking for a high-level operator who can think strategically and execute fast. This is not a typical freelancer role. You will act as the Head of Product & Creative, working directly with the CEO to build, launch, and scale winning products across multiple e-commerce brands. About Us We operate multiple fast-growing e-commerce brands across Amazon (FBA) and Shopify / BigCommerce. Our focus is high-converting products, strong creative execution, and scalable systems. Your Mission Own the entire product + creative lifecycle: From idea to launch to scaling. Responsibilities Product Development - Identify winning product opportunities - Conduct competitor research & review mining - Define product positioning, pricing, and differentiation - Work with sourcing team to develop products - Oversee packaging and product presentation - Ensure compliance (Amazon + Shopify) Creative & Conversion - Direct product photos, videos, and creatives - Write high-converting listings, video scripts, and ad hooks - Improve conversion rates across listings and landing pages Brand & Marketing - Build consistent brand identity across all products - Manage content strategy (UGC, ads, social) - Oversee copywriting and messaging Systems & Execution - Create SOPs for product launches, listing creation, and creative production - Coordinate with designers, video editors, media buyers, and operations team KPIs - New product success rate (% profitable within 60 days) - Conversion rate improvement - Creative performance (CTR, ROAS) - Product launch speed - Revenue from new products Weekly Responsibilities - Identify 3–5 new product opportunities per month - Review and improve listings & creatives - Launch and test new ad creatives - Coordinate with team - Optimize existing products based on data Monthly Responsibilities - Launch 2–5 new products per month - Conduct brand & creative audit - Analyze performance (CVR, CTR, ROAS, revenue) - Build and improve SOPs - Present growth insights Requirements - Proven experience in Amazon product launches and e-commerce brand building - Strong understanding of product positioning, creative strategy, and customer psychology - Ability to manage and direct a team - Data-driven decision making Do NOT Apply If - You are only a designer or VA - You have no real product launch experience - You cannot show past results - You prefer task-based work instead of ownership Compensation - Full-time contractor (40 hrs/week) - USD$500-800/month - Long-term role with growth potential Application Instructions 1. Examples of products you have launched 2. Links to listings 3. Before/after results 4. Answer: How do you determine if a product will succeed before launching? Paid Test Selected candidates will complete a paid test ($20) covering product strategy, listing concept, creative direction, and review analysis. Goal Within 90 days: - Launch multiple winning products - Improve conversion rates - Build scalable systems
title: Product & Creative Lead (E-commerce Brands) – Full-Time Contractor

description: We are a Singapore-based digital agency (GOSG) looking for a reliable development agency or dedicated team to work with us on an ongoing basis across multiple client projects. We don't want to hire freelancers task-by-task. We want a real partner we can assign tasks to continuously — someone who understands our stack, communicates well, and can pick up work as we drop it in without long onboarding each time. --- ABOUT THE WORK You'll be working across various client projects that include e-commerce stores, custom web applications, and headless setups. Tasks vary in size — some are small fixes, others are larger feature builds. There is no shortage of work. --- OUR TECH STACK - Shopify (theme customisation, custom apps, storefront) - Medusa v2 (headless e-commerce, custom plugins, API) - React (frontend components, SPAs) - Node.js (backend services, APIs) - JavaScript / TypeScript - MCP (Model Context Protocol) integrations --- WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR - An agency or small team (not a solo freelancer) - Strong experience with at least 3 of the above technologies - Able to handle async task assignment via ClickUp - Responsive communication — we work fast - Honest about timelines and technical limitations - Available for ongoing work, not just a single project --- HOW WE WORK - Tasks are assigned via ClickUp - Communication via Slack or WhatsApp - We prefer async but are available for quick calls when needed - Hourly billing — we'll set a weekly hour cap on the contract --- HOW TO APPLY Please include in your proposal: 1. Which technologies in our stack you're strongest in 2. 2–3 examples of similar work (links or brief description) 3. Your typical hourly rate for ongoing agency work 4. Your team size and how you handle task delegation internally Agencies only. We will not consider solo freelancers for this role.
title: Long-Term Development Partner — Shopify / Medusa v2 / React / Node (Ongoing Tasks)

description: We are seeking a highly capable QuickBooks Online Accounting, Tax & Operations Expert with strong e-commerce experience to support the day-to-day financial and operational management of our growing wellness business. This role requires someone who understands both accounting/bookkeeping and operational execution across online retail channels such as Amazon Seller Central, Walmart, and Shopify. The ideal candidate is analytical, detail-oriented, proactive, and experienced in managing finance and operations in an inventory-driven business. We are looking for a professional who can help manage purchase orders, receiving, vendor relationships, accounts payable and receivable, inventory management, tax filings, payroll support, and reporting, while also supporting our bookkeeper and strengthening our internal financial systems. Scope of Work Responsibilities include: Manage and maintain QuickBooks Online (QBO) accurately and consistently Manage accounts payable and accounts receivable Oversee purchase orders, receiving, and vendor records Support and improve inventory management, receiving, stock tracking, and reconciliation Manage vendor relationships and coordinate follow-up on invoices, payments, and delivery issues Work across Amazon Seller Central, Walmart, and Shopify to align orders, inventory, payouts, and financial records Support sales tax and business tax filing preparation and tracking Assist with 1099 preparation and filing support Assist with W-2 and payroll-related filing support Support the bookkeeper by ensuring financial data is clean, categorized, reconciled, and audit-ready Help prepare financial reports, management reports, and operational analytics Assist with month-end close, reconciliations, and financial accuracy Monitor and report on inventory levels, purchasing needs, stock discrepancies, and receiving issues Help build and improve workflows between accounting, operations, inventory, and sales channels Identify errors, gaps, and opportunities for stronger financial and operational controls Ideal Candidate We are looking for someone with strong experience in: QuickBooks Online (required) E-commerce accounting and operations Amazon Seller Central Walmart Marketplace Shopify Purchase orders, receiving, and inventory workflows Accounts payable and accounts receivable Sales tax and business tax filing support 1099 and W-2 filing support Vendor coordination and operational follow-through Financial reporting and analytics Supporting bookkeeping and operational accounting processes Preferred Qualifications Experience working with inventory-based or product-driven businesses Strong understanding of inventory accounting Comfortable with spreadsheets, reporting, and reconciliations Highly analytical and deadline-driven Strong attention to detail and process discipline Able to work independently and proactively solve problems Deliverables / Outcomes Accurate and timely QuickBooks records Improved control over purchase orders, receiving, and vendor management Better visibility into inventory movement and stock levels Stronger coordination between accounting and operations Timely support for tax, 1099, and W-2 filing processes Clear, reliable financial and management reporting Cleaner workflows and better internal controls across finance and operations To Apply Please include: A brief summary of your experience with QuickBooks Online Your experience with Amazon Seller Central, Walmart, and Shopify Examples of similar roles you have handled in e-commerce or inventory-based businesses Your experience with POs, receiving, vendor management, AP/AR, inventory, and tax support Your availability and preferred working hours We are looking for someone reliable, organized, analytical, and hands-on who can help us bring stronger discipline, visibility, and execution across both finance and operations.
