description: Hi There, I currently have two projects I need to undertake for a client and for myself. The first is building a shopify store with approximately 10 products over 5 categories just to get us going and then the customer is going to load their own items. I don't want this to be done cheaply or poorly but I would like it to be done as efficiently as possible. I have a partially completed Figma file with a rough idea of the look and feel of the site, fonts, colours and logos etc. To be finished off - I don't have the time to finish the mockups they just need to be used as a brand guide. I'm happy to use something like dawn theme and just edit it based on the amount of customisation needed. This is for a pet shop, they have minimum amount of requirements needed but some customisation and a really nice looking, polished front end design is essential. Minimum use of plugins to reduce ongoing costs is important I really don't want to have to add 20 plugins to get the site where we want it, but as I said it's a pretty minimal website. We'll start with 5 categories, 10 products and branch out later. Click and collect (No delivery) so no shipping plugins needed. Primary goal is store visits, they have a physical shopfront Second to that would be order online with click and collect Pages needed would be: Home page with about us section, visit our store, google reviews, product categories grid, hot product or offer section, newsletter sign up and FAQ's. Contact us page with contact form, contact details, store open hours and FAQ. Product page simple with product images, Title, Price, Service Reviews (not product reviews yet), Related products, up-sell products, possibly bundle deals, ( Crude example: Buy a fish tank and add fish food and a gold fish for X amount.) Product description, in-stock status, probably 3 static USPs like Same day pickup available, In Stock Now, Best Price Guarantee something like that. I'm looking for someone who has done this before and can hit the ground running with minimal help or intervention needed from me. As mentioned I am far too busy to handhold someone though every step of this project. If we work well together I have much larger project which involved migrating an existing magento 2 store to shopify. Many thanks.
title: Highly Shopify Person or Agency Needed

description: I’m looking for an experienced Shopify designer/developer to build and optimize a multi-brand beauty retail website. This is not a typical Shopify store. The goal is to create a high-end, curated shopping experience that feels elevated, intentional, extremely user-friendly, and highly optimized for conversion. The site should guide visitors through a clear buying journey that reduces hesitation and makes purchasing feel effortless, while also being built to scale into a multi-brand beauty retailer over time. A strong focus on usability, flow, and conversion is essential. --- HOMEPAGE STRUCTURE (Conversion-First Framework) The homepage should follow a structured layout designed to improve product discovery and increase conversions. It should include: • A strong positioning statement that communicates the brand experience and identity • A Bestsellers section near the top to reduce decision fatigue • Shop by Category navigation for quick product discovery • A Shop by Brand section (built to scale as more brands are added) • Visible customer reviews and social proof • A “Why Shop With Us” / credibility section to build trust • A curated section such as “The Edit” to highlight intentional product selections --- MULTI-BRAND STRUCTURE (Very Important) The site must be built to support multiple brands as we grow. This includes: • A dedicated Brands section in navigation • Individual brand pages that include: • Brand logo • Short “About the Brand” section • Product collection • Clean, elevated layout The goal is for each brand to feel intentionally placed and well-presented, not just listed. --- PRODUCT PAGE OPTIMIZATION (Very Important) Product pages should be structured as story-driven, high-converting experiences. They should include: • Strong visual hierarchy so key information is immediately clear • Product benefits highlighted before technical specifications • Visible customer reviews near the top of the page • Clear product highlights (quality, performance, key features) • Frequently bought together / upsell recommendations • Mobile-optimized sticky add-to-cart functionality • Clean imagery and layouts that are easy to scan --- COLLECTION PAGE IMPROVEMENTS Collection pages should help customers quickly identify top products. Product images should support badges such as: • Best Seller • Trending • Editor’s Pick • New These labels should appear directly on product images to guide purchasing decisions. --- UPSELLS & CROSS-SELL FEATURES The site should include clean, conversion-focused upsell features: • Frequently Bought Together (near Add to Cart) • “Pairs Well With” or “Complete the Routine” • “You May Also Like” These should feel natural and elevated, not aggressive or cluttered. --- TRUST & CREDIBILITY ELEMENTS (Essential) The website must feel trustworthy and established, even at an early stage. This includes: • Visible customer reviews and ratings • Clear shipping and return policies • Clean, professional layout • Subtle trust indicators throughout --- CUSTOMER ACCOUNT EXPERIENCE The site should include a clean and user-friendly customer account system. Customers should be able to: • Create an account and log in • View order history • Quickly reorder past purchases • Save items to a wishlist / favorites list • View and track loyalty points The goal is to create a simple, intuitive experience that encourages repeat purchasing. --- LOYALTY / REWARDS SYSTEM (Online + In-Store Required) The loyalty system must work both online and in-store (POS). Customers should be able to: • Earn points from purchases • Track their points within their account • Redeem rewards both online and in-store Requirements: • Must integrate with Shopify POS • Clean and easy-to-use interface • Seamless experience across online and in-store --- BACK-IN-STOCK / NOTIFY ME FEATURE The site should include a “Notify Me When Available” feature for out-of-stock products. This should include: • A visible “Notify Me” option when products are sold out • Ability for customers to submit email (or phone if possible) • Automatic notification when products are restocked --- MOBILE OPTIMIZATION (Very Important) The website must be highly optimized for mobile: • Fast loading speed • Clear product visibility • Easy navigation • Strong visual hierarchy on smaller screens --- PLACEHOLDER VISUAL DIRECTION We do not yet have final product photography. The site should be designed using elevated placeholder visuals, including: • Clean product cutouts • Neutral backgrounds • Minimal, editorial-style layouts The design should still feel premium and intentional. --- BRAND INQUIRY / CONTACT PAGE Create a clean, elevated contact page that allows: • General inquiries • Brand partnership inquiries This should include: • A clean form (name, email, brand, message) • A visible contact email • Design aligned with the overall site aesthetic --- TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS • Built using Shopify Online Store 2.0 • Modular, scalable sections • Clean navigation and structure • Strong focus on performance and speed --- IMPORTANT NOTES • We are starting with limited products but building for multi-brand scalability • The website must be high-converting, visually appealing, and extremely user-friendly • Strong emphasis on customer experience, flow, and ease of navigation • I’m looking for someone who can think strategically and recommend the best tools/apps --- IDEAL CANDIDATE Looking for someone who: • Has experience designing high-converting Shopify websites • Understands beauty / lifestyle audiences • Focuses on conversion, UX, and flow (not just aesthetics) --- Looking for someone who can help bring a high-end, multi-brand beauty retail experience to life — not just build a basic website.
title: Shopify Designer/Developer for Luxury Multi-Brand Beauty Retail Website (High-Converting + Scalable)

description: (Budget is placeholder) We are building a real-time CFO + growth command center for a fast-scaling DTC subscription brand. This is not a basic dashboard project. The system must ingest data across our entire stack, normalize it into a single source of truth, and produce a daily executive briefing with actionable decisions, not just metrics. The end goal is a system that tells us what to do every day to scale to $1M/month revenue with strong cash efficiency and predictable growth. What We’re Building: A daily CFO tracker and decision engine that: Pulls data from all sources (APIs, exports, etc.) Calculates key business metrics (NCAC, MRR, churn, payback, cash runway) Outputs: Daily performance summary Financial health snapshot Clear, prioritized actions to take immediately This should function like an internal operating system for the business. Data Sources: You will be responsible for integrating and structuring data from: Shopify (API) Loop Subscriptions (API) Triple Whale Meta Ads (spend, CAC) Google Ads PayPal Finaloop (currently via exports, API coming) Mercury / bank balances Credit cards (balances + due dates) Meta + Google monthly invoicing (30-day terms) Melio (for extended float) Inventory system (Materials app – API + lead times) Core Metrics You Must Build + Maintain: Revenue (daily + rolling) Ad Spend (by channel) Blended ROAS NCAC (new customer acquisition cost) Cost to acquire a subscriber Subscription take rate Active subscribers MRR + MRR growth Churn (or leading indicators) LTV (modeled) Cash on hand Available credit + float Upcoming obligations (30-day window) Cash runway (in days) Inventory coverage (days remaining) System Requirements: Data Infrastructure Build a clean, reliable pipeline (no broken numbers) Recommend and implement the stack (BigQuery, Airtable, Sheets, etc.) Automate daily updates (no long-term manual work) CFO Logic Layer You will implement logic that: Evaluates unit economics (NCAC vs LTV) Tracks payback periods (target ≤ 60 days) Calculates real cash runway (including credit + float) Flags risks (cash, churn, inventory) Daily Output (Critical) System must generate a daily briefing including: Business health (growing / flat / declining) Key metric changes vs yesterday Cash position + runway Subscription health Ad performance diagnosis Inventory constraints Most important: Top 3 actions to take TODAY (required) Examples: Increase Meta spend from X → Y based on CAC stability Cut underperforming campaigns immediately Fix drop in subscription take rate with a specific intervention This is a decision engine, not a reporting dashboard. Who This Is For: You are: A senior data engineer or analytics operator Experienced with DTC, subscriptions, and paid media metrics Comfortable working with APIs, messy data, and financial logic Able to translate raw data into business decisions Bonus if you’ve worked with: Shopify + subscriptions (Loop, Recharge, etc.) Triple Whale or similar attribution tools CFO dashboards or financial modeling for ecommerce Who This Is NOT For: Basic dashboard builders Visualization-only specialists (Tableau, Looker, etc.) Anyone who needs heavy direction Success Criteria: Within 30–45 days: Fully automated daily dashboard Accurate financial and growth metrics Daily actionable briefing generated consistently Clear visibility into scaling decisions Compensation: Fixed + performance-based preferred Open to ongoing retainer if system proves valuable
title: Senior Data Engineer / DTC Analytics Operator – Build Daily CFO Dashboard for Subscription Brand

description: We run a premium home goods Shopify store targeting US customers via Google Shopping ads exclusively. We have products live in Google Merchant Center with a Performance Max campaign running. The role is research and listing — find validated winning products, then upload and optimize them in Shopify. The Role This is a data-backed demand identification role. You find products that already have proven Google search demand, validate the numbers, and list them properly in Shopify. That is the job. We use Google Shopping as a demand capture channel — people are already searching for these products. Your research must prove that before anything gets listed. What You Will Do Find 5-10 product candidates daily using your research method of choice (Amazon, TikTok, Facebook ad library, competitor stores, product tools — anything works as long as you can back it with Google search data) Validate each with Google Trends (US, 12 months - 5 years) and Google Keyword Planner — exact monthly search volume and CPC required, not estimates Check Google Shopping competition for each product Source from AliExpress, CJ Dropshipping, or Alibaba — verify quality, confirm 3x margin minimum Write an optimized Google Merchant Center title for each candidate Upload approved products to Shopify — clean title, original description, premium AI images, correct GMC attributes, draft status Flag issues with existing products — pricing, titles, GMC compliance The Brand Premium, minimal, elevated home goods. Reference stores: Magnolia, McGee & Co, Ivory & Deene. Products should look at home in a well-designed apartment, not on Temu. Yes: woven baskets, linen bedding, velvet curtains, minimal lamps, textured throws, ceramic vases, bathroom rugs with a design angle. No: gadgets, novelty items, furniture, anything over 5 lbs, anything that looks cheap. Requirements Google Shopping research experience — you understand how demand capture works vs social ad product research Google Merchant Center familiarity — titles, image standards, disapproval reasons Shopify product listing experience — clean uploads, GMC-compliant setup Supplier sourcing experience — AliExpress, CJ, Alibaba Structured Google Sheets output — exact numbers, no vague descriptions To Apply — All Three Required Walk me through your exact step-by-step process for validating a product for Google Shopping. Be specific — which tools, what exact numbers you look for, what makes you say yes or no. Send a Loom video (5–10 minutes) showing your actual research workflow and results you have produced. — screen share, walk me through finding and validating one product in real time. Share one concrete example of a product you found that performed on Google Shopping — product name, the data behind it, what happened. Screenshots welcome. Answer all three in plain language. No bullet-point formatted AI responses — write how you actually talk. We can tell the difference. Rate: $5–10/hr depending on experience | Hours: 2–4/day to start | Trial task first | Ongoing if trial passes
title: Google Shopping Product Researcher & Lister for Premium Home Decor Store

description: We're a SaaS startup preparing to launch a cloud-based inventory management platform and need a thorough manual QA pass across the entire product — web app, native mobile apps (iOS + Android), and API — before launch. We have comprehensive test documentation ready: a 1,900-line onboarding doc with ~200+ test scenarios organized into prioritized suites, a companion manual tests appendix covering visual/cross-browser/accessibility/device testing, bug report templates, and daily reporting formats. You will not be guessing what to test — everything is documented with exact steps, expected results, and pre-seeded test accounts across four organizations at different plan tiers. About the platform: KeySpace is a B2B inventory, warehouse, and order management tool for small businesses. It includes integrations with Shopify, Amazon, WooCommerce, QuickBooks, Xero, Square, Etsy, BigCommerce, and eBay, plus demand forecasting, lot/expiration tracking, barcode scanning, role-based access, multi-org support, custom dashboards, billing via Stripe, and native iOS and Android apps. Scope of work: Web app: Full manual test pass across all features on a staging environment Test across desktop browsers: Chrome, Edge, Safari, Firefox Test responsive layouts at 375px / 768px / 1024px / 1280px / 1920px Test user flows end-to-end: signup, inventory lifecycle, orders, purchase orders, integrations, billing, role permissions, plan gating, multi-org switching Tenant isolation and security probing (cross-org data access, role enforcement at API level) Mobile apps (iOS + Android): Test all core flows on native apps: login, inventory browsing, barcode/QR scanning, adjustments, cycle counts, PO receiving, org switching Verify offline behavior (airplane mode toggle, cached data, mutation queuing) Test device-specific behavior: push notifications, camera permissions, deep linking, background kill/resume Platform-specific checks: iOS safe area/notch, Android back button, orientation lock API (via pre-built Postman collection — click Send, read the response): Tenant isolation verification at API level Role permission enforcement (403 responses for unauthorized actions) Plan gate enforcement (correct error shapes for gated features) Deliverables: Google Sheet test matrix updated in real time (one row per scenario, status + bug IDs + notes) Individual bug reports with steps to reproduce, expected vs. actual behavior, severity, platform/device, and screenshots or screen recordings Daily status report in a specified format every working day Engagement structure: 3–4 weeks. First 2 weeks are launch-critical. Weeks 3–4 are post-launch regression and patch verification. Remote, async. Work in your own timezone. $1,000 up front, $1,000 on delivery of completed test matrix meeting acceptance criteria. Acceptance criteria: 100% of P0 (critical) scenarios tested 80%+ of P1 scenarios tested Bugs reproducible from your report alone on first attempt (~90%) Daily report submitted every working day, no gaps Final sign-off summary reviewing open bugs, blocked scenarios, and overall quality assessment What we provide: Comprehensive onboarding document with all test scenarios, steps, and expected results Pre-seeded staging environment with four organizations across all plan tiers Test account credentials (web + mobile) TestFlight invite (iOS) and APK link (Android) for staging mobile builds Pre-built Postman collection for API scenarios Google Sheet test matrix ready to fill in Sandbox credentials for Shopify, Amazon, and QuickBooks integrations Ideal candidate: Experience testing SaaS web applications AND native mobile apps (iOS + Android) Comfortable with Postman (clicking Send on pre-built requests, reading responses — no authoring) Familiar with mobile-specific testing: camera permissions, offline behavior, deep linking, push notifications Owns at least one iPhone and one Android device for physical device testing Strong written communication for clear, reproducible bug reports Can work independently from detailed test documentation without hand-holding Ecommerce, inventory, or logistics tool experience a plus To apply, please include: 1–2 examples of past QA work or bug reports you've produced Confirmation you have access to both an iPhone and an Android device Your estimated timeline to complete the first P0 pass (we target 2 weeks) Any relevant experience with inventory, ecommerce, or Shopify-related tools
title: Manual QA Tester for SaaS Inventory Management Platform — Web + Mobile Apps

description: We’re looking for a Shopify Front-End Developer to optimize the user experience and improve conversion performance on our storefront. We are focused on improving how users interact with the site to increase conversion rate, reduce friction, and create a smoother shopping experience across devices, especially mobile. We’ve been actively analyzing user behavior through session recordings and have identified several areas of friction, including layout shifts (CLS issues), inconsistent rendering in in-app browsers (especially Instagram), image loading issues, and general UI/UX inconsistencies across product pages. The goal of this project is to diagnose and resolve these issues while improving overall site performance and usability. We are looking for someone who understands both Shopify development and conversion-focused UX. This includes improving page speed, stabilizing layout behavior, ensuring responsive design across devices, and optimizing the product page experience so users can move through the site without friction. We will provide access to our store, along with references and recordings to highlight the issues we are seeing. We are looking for someone who can not only implement fixes, but also identify additional areas of improvement based on best practices. This project will be milestone-based to ensure clarity and performance: 10% will be released after a full audit and diagnosis of issues, including a clear breakdown of the causes of layout shifts, rendering bugs, and UX friction points. 20% will be released after implementing fixes for layout stability (CLS), including preventing shifting elements, stabilizing image and media loading, and improving above-the-fold consistency. 25% will be released after resolving mobile and in-app browser issues, ensuring the site renders correctly within Instagram and other embedded browsers without breaking layout or spacing. 25% will be released after overall UX and performance improvements are implemented, including smoother scrolling, improved image handling, faster load times, and cleaner interaction across product pages. 20% will be released after final QA and testing across devices, with all fixes verified and no major visual or functional issues remaining. Our goal is not just to fix issues, but to build a stronger foundation for scaling traffic and improving conversion rates. If this project goes well, there will be opportunities for ongoing optimization work. Please include examples of Shopify stores you’ve worked on, specifically where you improved performance, UX, or conversion rate.
title: Shopify Front-End Developer

description: We are building a premium direct-to-consumer brand called **Blackwood Bar Company**, focused on selling high-end custom home bars ($15K–$25K range). This is not a typical eCommerce project. We are building a **luxury, cinematic, experience-driven brand** where the website, visuals, and product configuration must feel elevated, intentional, and emotionally compelling. We are looking for a **long-term partner** who can help us build this foundation correctly from day one. --- # **Scope of Work** This project has 3 primary components: --- ## **1. Brand Asset Development (Immediate)** We already have a logo direction, but need it professionally rebuilt and expanded. **Deliverables:** * Final logo recreation (vector format) * Logo variations (horizontal, stacked, icon if applicable) * Color palette (luxury-focused, warm tones) * Typography system (web + print) * Brand guidelines (simple but clean) **Business Assets:** * Editable email signatures (Gmail-compatible) * Business cards (print-ready) * Letterhead (print + digital) --- ## **2. Shopify Website Design & Development (Core Project)** We need a **fully custom Shopify site** that reflects a high-end, luxury brand experience. ### **Design Direction** * Dark, moody, cinematic aesthetic - see attached images that set the mood / vibe * Warm lighting (inspired by high-end cocktail bars) * Minimal, intentional layouts (not cluttered) * Strong focus on product + emotional experience * Must align with high-end rendering direction (we are working with a rendering studio) --- ## **3. Product Configuration System (Critical)** Each bar model must allow the customer to configure options directly on the product page. ### **Base Product Options (Required)** For each bar model: **Wood Species (3 options)** * Example: Walnut, Mahogany, white oak **Stain Options (3 per species)** * Total of 9 visual combinations minimum **Bar Length** * 3 standard sizes (e.g., 8’, 10’, 12’) * Option for custom length (input field or inquiry) **Bar Height** * 3 standard heights * Option for custom height **Add-Ons** * Beer tap tower (toggle add-on) * Additional accessories (future-ready) This must be clean, intuitive, and visually driven (not dropdown-heavy) --- ## **4. Additional Website Sections** ### **A. Bar Stools + Accessories** * Separate collection page * Includes: * Bar stools * Beer towers * Add-ons (e.g., custom etched mirror, branded elements) ### **B. Commercial Projects Page** * Informational landing page * Position us as a custom commercial bar builder * CTA: inquiry form ### **C. Wine Rooms** * Informational landing page * Position us as a custom wine room builder * CTA: inquiry form --- ## **5. Subscription System (Very Important)** We will be offering a **monthly & quarterly subscription box** (luxury bar accessories). **Requirements:** * Subscription management system (Shopify-compatible) * Ability to: * Offer monthly & quarterly plans * Manage customers and billing * Allow customer account login and management * Must integrate cleanly with brand experience --- ## **6. Payments & Checkout Optimization** We want to make purchasing as frictionless as possible. **Required Payment Options:** * Credit Cards * ACH / Bank Transfer * Buy Now Pay Later (Shop Pay, Affirm, Klarna, etc.) * Apple Pay / Google Pay / PayPal etc. --- # **Technical Expectations** * Shopify expert (custom themes preferred, not just templates) * Strong UX/UI design skills (luxury experience) * Experience with product configurators * Experience with subscription tools (Recharge, Skio, etc.) * Mobile-first optimization (critical) * Fast load speed despite high-end visuals --- # **What We Are NOT Looking For** * Basic Shopify template installers * Generic eCommerce designers * Over-designed / cluttered layouts * Agencies that outsource everything blindly --- # **What We ARE Looking For** * Someone who understands **luxury branding** * Someone who can guide design decisions (not just execute) * Someone who can think long-term and scale with us * Strong portfolio with **high-end or architectural/interior products** --- # **To Apply** Please include: 1. Portfolio (specifically high-end or luxury work) 2. Examples of Shopify sites you have built 3. Experience with product configurators 4. Experience with subscription platforms 5. Your recommended approach to building this site --- We are moving quickly and looking to begin immediately. If this is a fit, we are open to building a long-term relationship.
title: High-End Luxury Brand Designer + Shopify Developer

What you'll be doing: Responding to customer emails and messages (order status, shipping questions, product inquiries) Processing returns, exchanges, and refunds through Shopify Handling order issues — wrong items, damaged shipments, address changes, cancellations Updating customers proactively when delays or issues come up Flagging recurring complaints or product questions so we can improve our store and messaging Light Shopify admin — looking up orders, editing order details, applying discount codes when needed You're a good fit if you: Have experience with Shopify (non-negotiable) Have worked in ecommerce or DTC customer service before Can write clear, friendly responses without sounding robotic or copy-pasting generic templates Are comfortable working independently — you won't need hand-holding on routine tickets Are responsive and can maintain a fast turnaround on customer inquiries (same-day replies expected) Are based in a timezone that allows coverage during US business hours Nice to have: Experience with helpdesk tools (Gorgias, Zendesk, Freshdesk, etc.) Familiarity with golf or sports/fitness products Experience handling reviews or social media DMs Details: Part-time to start, with potential to grow as volume increases Hourly rate — $3 - $5 USD a hour Please include a brief note on your ecommerce CS experience and which helpdesk tools you've used

Start with one account, grow from there based on performance and fit. To apply, please share 2 to 3 examples of ecommerce accounts you’ve managed, what you did, and what results looked like. Generic proposals will be skipped.

description: BEFORE YOU APPLY — You can train and maintain multiple consistent AI character models that look like the same person across dozens of different scenes, poses, and lighting conditions. Not "similar looking" — the same recognizable person every time. — You can take our real product photos and composite them into AI-generated scenes so the final image looks like a single professional photograph. —You can make hyper-realistic images where the people and the product look real and accurate — not AI-generated, not warped, not mismatched angles or lighting between the product/image. BONUS IF YOU DO VIDEO: — You can produce AI-generated video (not just stills) for short-form ad creative — models interacting with products, lifestyle scenes, scroll-stopping hooks. If your portfolio is random AI art with a different face in every image, this is not what we need. We need brand-consistent visual systems, not one-off generations. ABOUT US We're a hemp-derived cannabis and CBD ecommerce brand selling DTC through Shopify. Products include gummies, vapes, tinctures, and other hemp-derived items. ~80-person company, 7 years in business, self-funded. You'll work directly with our Channel Manager and CEO — no layers of middlemen. We review applications within 48 hours and move fast on the right fit. WHAT YOU'LL ACTUALLY DO This is an ongoing role, not a one-off project. You're building and maintaining our AI visual asset library and producing new creative on a regular cadence. Consistent Character Development & Maintenance Build and maintain multiple AI brand models — distinct, recognizable people who appear consistently across all our visual content. These aren't throwaway faces. They're recurring brand characters that customers will see across ads, product pages, emails, and social. You'll train custom models (LoRA, Dreambooth, or your preferred method) and maintain them as tools evolve. Product Compositing We'll provide high-quality photos of our actual products. You place them into AI-generated lifestyle scenes — held by our brand models, on tables, in environments — so the final output looks like a single cohesive professional photo or video. The product must look physically accurate: correct proportions, real label, proper lighting match. If the gummy jar looks AI-generated or the vape pen is warped, it doesn't ship. Photo Asset Production Lifestyle images for product pages (Shopify), ad creative for Meta and other platforms, email hero images, social content. High volume — we need someone who can produce batches efficiently, not someone who spends 4 hours on a single image. BONUS IF YOU CAN: Video Asset Production Short-form video for ad creative — 15 to 60 seconds. Brand models interacting with products, lifestyle scenes, scroll-stopping hooks. These feed directly into our paid media pipeline. Our media buying agency uses these assets to test and scale — so speed and volume matter alongside quality. Ad Creative Understanding You're not making art. You're making assets designed to stop thumbs and convert. You need to understand what makes a good hook frame, what compositions perform in feed ads, and how to produce creative variations for A/B testing. You'll work alongside our Performance Creative Designer and media buying agency — they'll give you briefs and performance feedback, and you'll iterate based on what's working. WHY THIS ISN'T A STANDARD AI ART GIG Realism and brand tone consistency are the hardest parts. Most AI creators can generate beautiful single images. Very few can maintain realism, style & model across 3-10 scenes. If you can't do this reliably, nothing else matters. Real product compositing separates professionals from prompters. Anyone can type "woman holding a product" into Midjourney. We need someone who can take our actual product photo and place it into an AI scene so seamlessly that you can't tell where the real photo ends and the AI begins. This requires Photoshop-level compositing skill on top of AI generation skill. Volume and speed matter. This feeds an active ad pipeline. We need someone who can deliver batches of assets on a weekly cadence, not someone who delivers 3 images a month. REQUIRED SKILLS (NON-NEGOTIABLE) — Custom AI model training for character consistency (LoRA, Dreambooth, IP-Adapter, or equivalent). You must be able to explain your method and show results. — Product compositing — placing real product photography into AI-generated scenes with matched lighting, perspective, and proportions. Photoshop proficiency is assumed. — Can produce at volume — weekly batches of photo and video assets, not artisanal one-offs. — Portfolio showing the realisim and same style and AI model consistently across multiple different scenes. This is the #1 filter. If your portfolio doesn't show this, don't apply. PREFERRED BUT NOT REQUIRED — AI video generation using current tools (Runway, Kling, Veo, or equivalent). Must show examples. — Experience creating ad creative for cannabis, CBD, supplements, or other restricted-category brands — Understanding of Meta ad creative best practices (hook frames, aspect ratios, variation testing) TO APPLY — ANSWER THESE THREE SCREENING QUESTIONS AND INCLUDE YOUR PORTFOLIO RED FLAGS THAT WILL GET YOU SCREENED OUT — Portfolio full of random AI art with different faces in every image — tells us you can prompt but can't maintain consistency — No product compositing examples — tells us you've never worked with real product photography — No video examples — we need both photo and video, not one or the other — "I can learn any tool" without showing current proficiency — we're paying for expertise, not a learning curve — Application that doesn't answer the three questions above — tells us you didn't read the post
title: AI Product Photography, UGC, Video: Consistent Models + Real Product Compositing (Nike quality)

description: We are seeking a skilled website developer to create a unique and engaging online presence for our niche baby brand. The ideal candidate will have experience in e-commerce and a great understanding for childrens/baby products. You will be responsible for designing a user-friendly website that showcases our products and drives sales. A strong understanding of SEO and responsive design is essential. Responsibilities: Full-Stack Development: Build, maintain, and optimize a responsive, high-performance website that serves both corporate interests and e-commerce needs. E-commerce Integration: Implement and manage secure checkout processes, inventory management systems, and third-party logistics/shipping APIs. Performance Optimization: Monitor and improve Core Web Vitals, ensuring fast load times even with high-resolution product imagery. Security & Compliance: Ensure the site is secure and compliant with data privacy regulations like PIPEDA and GDPR, particularly regarding customer payment data. Collaboration: Work closely with the founder to translate brand vision into functional features, such as custom sizing guides or interactive product showcases. Technical Maintenance: Manage version control (Git), staging environments, and regular security patches to ensure zero downtime during high-traffic periods. Requirements: Technical Proficiency: Expert-level knowledge of HTML5, CSS3, and JavaScript, with deep experience in at least one major e-commerce platform (e.g., Shopify/Liquid, WooCommerce, or Headless architectures). Responsive Design: A proven track record of building "mobile-first" websites that provide a seamless experience across all devices. Back-End Capabilities: Experience integrating third-party APIs and managing databases securely. Performance Mindset: Strong understanding of SEO best practices, site architecture, and asset optimization for speed. Quality Assurance: Experience using staging environments and automated testing to ensure a bug-free production environment. Communication: Ability to explain complex technical concepts in plain language and provide strategic recommendations for business growth. If you have a portfolio of previous work in similar industries, we want to hear from you!
title: Website Developer for Niche Baby Brand

description: We're looking for a Figma designer who specializes in high-converting ecommerce and DTC landing pages — someone who can take a Relume.io or AI-generated design and refine it into a polished, production-ready layout. description: Job Description: We are looking for an experienced Zapier Automation Expert to design, build, and optimize automated workflows across multiple tools and platforms for our business operations. The ideal candidate should be skilled in creating efficient, scalable automations that reduce manual work and improve overall productivity. Scope of Work: - Build and manage automated workflows using Zapier - Integrate multiple tools and platforms (CRM, email, forms, databases, etc.) - Automate lead capture, follow-ups, and notification systems - Connect marketing tools (Meta Ads, Google Sheets, email platforms, CRMs, etc.) - Set up data syncing between different systems - Create multi-step Zaps with conditional logic and filters - Troubleshoot and fix broken or inefficient automations - Optimize existing workflows for speed and reliability - Document automation processes for internal use Example Automations: 1- Lead form submission → CRM → email follow-up → Slack/WhatsApp notification 2- Ad leads → Google Sheets → email nurturing sequence 3- E-commerce order → fulfillment system → customer notification 4- Social media lead capture → CRM tagging and segmentation Required Skills: - Strong experience with Zapier (advanced workflows) - Experience with integrations like HubSpot, Google Sheets, Airtable, Shopify, Meta Ads, Gmail, etc. - Understanding of APIs and webhook-based automations - Ability to design scalable automation systems - Problem-solving mindset for workflow optimization Ideal Candidate: - Can design end-to-end automation systems (not just simple Zaps) - Understands marketing and sales funnels - Has experience working with SaaS, eCommerce, or lead generation workflows - Detail-oriented and focused on reliability and efficiency Project Details: - Existing tools and systems are already in place - Immediate start required - Potential for long-term automation management role To Apply, Please Include: 1- Examples of Zapier workflows you’ve built 2- Tools and platforms you’ve integrated 3- Complex automation problems you’ve solved 4- Your availability for immediate start We are looking for someone who can streamline our entire business operations through smart and scalable automation systems.

description: **Title** Senior Web Designer Needed for Luxury B2B Brand (Editorial, Visual-First Website) --- **Overview** We are building the website for *The Labgrown Gemstone Company (TLGGC)* — a B2B brand supplying precision-matched gemstone layouts to jewellery manufacturers and design studios. This is a **full website design project**, not just a single page. We already have a **comprehensive brand and visual system in place**, and are looking for a designer who can **translate this into a cohesive, high-end digital experience**. --- **About the Brand** TLGGC is a **trusted sourcing partner** for jewellery manufacturers and design studios, focused on precision, consistency, and coordinated gemstone layouts rather than just loose stones. The brand combines: * Technical expertise * Human-centred communication * Strong visual storytelling --- **Scope** The project will be approached in phases, starting with the homepage as the foundation and extending into: * Homepage (design system foundation) * Collection / Inventory page * Product / Layout detail page * Editorial / content pages The goal is to create a **scalable design system** that can be extended across the entire website. --- **Design Direction** The website should feel: * Editorial (closer to a premium publication than ecommerce) * Minimal but expressive * Image-led (stones lead, text follows) * Calm, precise, and controlled * High-end luxury — without being flashy --- **Design Principles** * Strong typography (serif-led headlines + clean body text) * Asymmetric composition (not rigid grids) * Generous whitespace * Visual hierarchy driven by imagery * Subtle, refined interactions --- **Important** We are NOT looking for: * Template-based Shopify designers * Standard jewellery ecommerce layouts * SaaS-style UI or dashboard thinking * Overdesigned or trend-heavy visuals --- **Implementation Context (Important)** The website will be built on **Shopify using the Warehouse theme as the base**. Design should: * Be aware of Shopify/Warehouse constraints * Be reasonably implementable * But not be creatively limited by the theme We are open to customization where required. --- **Brand & Design References** To understand the project properly, please review: * 📄 **Brand Master Document (Attached)** * 🎨 **Visual Style Guide:** https://raspberry-1773493372726.staticrun.app/TLGGC_Visual_Style_Guide-Final * 📘 **Company Deck / Narrative:** https://raspberry-1773493372726.staticrun.app/tlggc_landing_v2 * 🧩 **Logo Files (Attached)** The Brand Master Document consolidates all brand, identity, and creative direction into a single source of truth. --- **Tools** * Figma (required) --- **To Apply** Please include: 1. 2–3 relevant projects (luxury, editorial, or minimal design) 2. A brief explanation of your approach to system-based website design 3. Confirmation that you have reviewed the provided documents Applications without relevant work will not be considered. --- **Bonus (Strongly Preferred)** * Experience designing luxury or editorial websites * Strong typography sensibility * Ability to work with restraint and visual balance --- We are looking for a long-term design partner who can help build a distinctive, category-defining digital experience. Kindly note this is not a Jewellery website.
title: Senior Web Designer (Figma) — Premium B2B Luxury Brand (Editorial style) for Shopify

description: Sales & Business Development Manager (BDM) Location: Remote (Must be available during New Zealand business hours) Employment Type: Part-Time → Full-Time (Hourly + Commission Pathway) --- ### About the Role We are looking for a high-performing Sales & Business Development Manager (BDM) to drive revenue growth and expand our presence within the premium wellness space. This is a dual-role position combining hands-on sales execution with strategic business development. You will be responsible for converting high-value inbound opportunities while also proactively building new pipelines through partnerships, outbound efforts, and market expansion initiatives. You will play a critical role in scaling the business by increasing deal volume, improving conversion rates, and identifying new revenue channels. --- ### Key Responsibilities #### Sales (Revenue Generation) - Manage and convert inbound leads into high-ticket sales (saunas, ice baths, wellness setups) - Conduct discovery calls and guide customers through a consultative sales process - Create and present tailored, high-quality sales proposals and quotes - Maintain strong follow-up cadence to maximise conversion rates - Handle objections effectively and close deals with confidence - Manage and optimise pipeline activity within CRM systems (e.g., Pipedrive) #### Business Development (Growth & Expansion) - Identify and establish strategic partnerships (e.g., builders, landscapers, architects, gyms, wellness facilities) - Develop and execute outbound strategies to generate new opportunities - Build relationships with commercial clients and larger-scale buyers - Identify new product opportunities, market gaps, and revenue streams - Contribute to pricing strategy, positioning, and go-to-market initiatives - Support expansion into new markets and channels --- ### Requirements (Essential) - Proven experience in sales AND business development - Strong track record in high-ticket or consultative sales - Confident communicator with excellent phone and presentation skills - Exceptional English communication (written and verbal) - Demonstrated ability to generate your own pipeline (not just close inbound leads) - Strong commercial acumen and understanding of sales funnels - Highly organised, proactive, and results-driven - Able to work consistently within New Zealand business hours --- ### Application Requirement Please include the following in your application: - Overview of your sales and business development experience - Examples of high-ticket products/services you have sold (including price ranges) - Experience generating your own leads or partnerships - A recent example of a deal you originated and closed - Your approach to building and managing a sales pipeline --- ### Preferred Experience - Experience in wellness, fitness, lifestyle, or premium product industries - Experience working with B2B partnerships or commercial clients - Familiarity with CRM systems (e.g., Pipedrive) - Experience with eCommerce platforms (e.g., Shopify) - Background in scaling or growing a business unit or territory --- ### What We Offer - Flexible remote working environment - Opportunity to grow into a senior leadership role as the business scales - Work within a premium, fast-growing wellness brand - Commission and performance-based incentives - High ownership role with real impact on business growth --- ### Who This Role Suits This role is best suited for someone who: - Is both a closer and a builder - Thrives on driving revenue and creating new opportunities - Takes ownership and operates with a high level of autonomy - Thinks commercially and strategically, not just tactically - Wants to play a key role in scaling a business --- If you are an experienced sales professional who can both close deals and build new growth channels, we’d like to hear from you.
title: Sales & Business Development Manager

description: Sales Assistant (Part-Time → Full-Time Opportunity) Location: Remote (Must be available during New Zealand business hours) Employment Type: Hourly (with future commission opportunities) --- ### About the Role We are looking for an experienced and confident Sales Assistant to support our growing team, specialising in high-ticket wellness packages (saunas, ice baths, and premium wellness setups). This role is suited to someone who understands consultative selling, can build trust quickly, and is comfortable guiding customers through considered, higher-value purchasing decisions. You will be responsible for managing leads, nurturing relationships, and converting enquiries into sales. --- ### Key Responsibilities - Calling and speaking with customers (both inbound and outbound) - Managing warm leads and converting enquiries into sales - Creating and sending tailored, high-quality sales quotes - Following up consistently with prospects to drive conversions - Conducting cold outreach to generate new opportunities - Managing and updating deals within CRM systems (e.g., Pipedrive) - Handling objections and guiding customers through the decision-making process - Providing a premium, consultative sales experience aligned with high-ticket products - Supporting the sales pipeline and ensuring no opportunities are missed --- ### Requirements (Essential) - Proven sales experience (MUST HAVE) – ideally in high-ticket or consultative sales - Strong ability to sell premium products/services and communicate value effectively - Confident and professional phone presence - Exceptional English communication skills (written and verbal) - Ability to build rapport, trust, and long-term customer relationships - Strong follow-up discipline and closing ability - Highly organised and self-managed - Able to work consistently within New Zealand business hours --- ### Application Requirement As part of your application, please answer the following: - Tell us about your sales experience - What types of products or services have you sold? - Have you sold high-ticket items before? If so, what was the typical price range? - What is your approach to handling objections and closing deals? - Can you share an example of a successful sale you’ve made? --- ### Preferred Skills & Experience - Experience selling high-ticket lifestyle, wellness, or luxury products - Familiarity with CRM systems (e.g., Pipedrive) - Experience with Shopify or eCommerce environments - Strong understanding of sales pipelines, follow-ups, and conversion tracking - Ability to think commercially and identify upsell opportunities --- ### What We Offer - Flexible remote working environment - Opportunity to grow from part-time into a full-time role - Work within a fast-growing premium wellness brand - Future commission structure based on performance - Ongoing development and progression opportunities --- ### Who This Role Suits This role is ideal for someone who: - Has real sales experience and enjoys the process - Is confident selling higher-value products - Takes ownership of results and follows through - Wants to grow within a business long-term --- If you have strong sales experience and are confident working with high-ticket customers, we’d like to hear from you.

description: I'm building a consumer app in a $50B+ market that's massively underserved by technology. The product is 85% built and I'm looking for a developer or small technical team to take it from prototype to published. This isn't a concept or a pitch deck — the app is functional today with a working UI, live payment system, and AI integration. I need someone who can bring it to production quality and get it into the App Store and Google Play. What I need: → React / React Native / JSX fluency — the entire codebase is React. Not looking for someone who's learning it. → App Store + Google Play publishing experience — you've submitted apps, dealt with the review process, and know the compliance requirements for both platforms. → Payment infrastructure — Stripe is already integrated. I need someone who understands subscription lifecycles, webhook handling, failed payments, upgrades/downgrades, and App Store payment policy compliance. → Backend architecture for scale — the app needs to support 10,000+ users with real-time data, image storage, analytics ingestion, background jobs, and scheduled automations. You should be comfortable with database optimization, caching, rate limiting, and job queues. → AI/LLM integration — the app uses AI for image recognition and data extraction. Experience with LLM APIs, prompt engineering, or computer vision is a strong plus. → Data pipeline experience — the platform ingests and structures large volumes of data from external sources. Experience building reliable automated ingestion systems matters.

description: ROLE: Ecommerce Ads Specialist (TikTok + Meta) GOAL: Generate profitable sales for M.E.L. using a structured testing system. DO NOT: * Focus on followers * Run awareness-only campaigns * Overcomplicate targeting PHASE 1 — SETUP (DAY 1–2) * Verify TikTok + Meta pixel working * Connect Shopify events * Set up ad accounts properly PHASE 2 — CREATIVE TESTING Products: * 1 core hoodie * 1 Mother’s Day product Create 3–5 ads per product: AD TYPES: 1. Lifestyle Model wearing hoodie naturally Confident presence, clean background 2. Message-driven Text overlay: * “Not built to fold.” * “Stay Solid.” * “Pressure made this.” 3. Product-focused Close-up shots Fabric, fit, details HOOK EXAMPLES (USE THESE EXACTLY): * Not built to fold. * Some people break. Some people build. * Pressure made this. * Stay Solid. NO CORNY COPY. PHASE 3 — TESTING Budget: $20–$50/day per platform * Broad targeting * No scaling yet * Focus on CTR + conversions PHASE 4 — OPTIMIZATION * Kill weak ads * Double down on top 1–2 * Introduce retargeting RETARGETING COPY: * Still thinking about it? * No reruns. When it’s gone, it’s gone. * You know what this is. MOTHER’S DAY ANGLE: Hook: For the ones who held it down. Message: Not everything strong is loud. Some strength shows up every day. IMPORTANT: Keep tone strong, not emotional/soft. RULES: * Content drives performance (not targeting) * Keep ads clean and direct * No generic “sale” messaging Timeline: Ads ready by Day 3–4 Launch after site is complete

description: ROLE: Ecommerce Ads Specialist (TikTok + Meta) GOAL: Generate profitable sales for M.E.L. using a structured testing system. DO NOT: * Focus on followers * Run awareness-only campaigns * Overcomplicate targeting PHASE 1 — SETUP (DAY 1–2) * Verify TikTok + Meta pixel working * Connect Shopify events * Set up ad accounts properly PHASE 2 — CREATIVE TESTING Products: * 1 core hoodie * 1 Mother’s Day product Create 3–5 ads per product: AD TYPES: 1. Lifestyle Model wearing hoodie naturally Confident presence, clean background 2. Message-driven Text overlay: * “Not built to fold.” * “Stay Solid.” * “Pressure made this.” 3. Product-focused Close-up shots Fabric, fit, details HOOK EXAMPLES (USE THESE EXACTLY): * Not built to fold. * Some people break. Some people build. * Pressure made this. * Stay Solid. NO CORNY COPY. PHASE 3 — TESTING Budget: $20–$50/day per platform * Broad targeting * No scaling yet * Focus on CTR + conversions PHASE 4 — OPTIMIZATION * Kill weak ads * Double down on top 1–2 * Introduce retargeting RETARGETING COPY: * Still thinking about it? * No reruns. When it’s gone, it’s gone. * You know what this is. MOTHER’S DAY ANGLE: Hook: For the ones who held it down. Message: Not everything strong is loud. Some strength shows up every day. IMPORTANT: Keep tone strong, not emotional/soft. RULES: * Content drives performance (not targeting) * Keep ads clean and direct * No generic “sale” messaging Timeline: Ads ready by Day 3–4 Launch after site is complete
title: Performace

description: DTC Landing Page & Conversion Designer — Pet Health Brand (Figma) We're launching Ocupets, a premium canine vision support brand for the US market. Our complete copy is written across 9 documents and we need a designer to create high-converting page layouts in Figma. Pages to design (all desktop + mobile): • 1 Product Page (PDP) — this is the entire website. One single page that covers product, science, ingredients, 3-phase journey, testimonials, FAQ, pricing, guarantee, and brand story. CellSeed-style format with 11 product gallery image directions embedded in the copy doc. • 2 Pre-Landers — short bridge pages (1–2 scrolls). One for retargeting (stripped-down closer), one for cold traffic (symptom checker). • 2 Advertorials — long-form editorial landing pages. Must look like health articles, NOT sales pages. Single-column reading flow, no navigation distractions. • 2 Listicles — blog-style editorial pages. Clean, numbered sections. • 2 Branded PDFs — digital gift guides (8–12 pages and 15–20 pages). Must feel premium and worth the stated value. Total: 9 web page designs + 2 branded PDFs = 9 design deliverables. Premium, clean, health-focused. References: The Farmer's Dog, Seed, AG1, Hims, Grüns, CellSeed. The ideal candidate: • Has designed landing pages for DTC, health, or supplement brands • Works in Figma or Replo • Understands conversion design (visual hierarchy, CTA placement, mobile-first) • Can deliver within 7–10 working days Ideal Experience Shopify + Replo power user (must-have) Direct-to-consumer eCommerce funnels for paid social traffic (Meta, TikTok, YouTube) Strong copywriting instincts (hooks, offers, objections, FAQs) Ability to create/edit visuals (Figma, Canva, Photoshop, ChatGPT image tools, etc.) Familiar with A/B testing and interpreting analytics Requirements Proven experience in CRO for eCommerce product pages Ability to improve and generate high-quality product images without new photography Strong communication skills with the ability to deliver clear, actionable insights How to Apply (must include all 3): Share 1–5 Shopify/Replo pages you personally built (not just designed) Confirm how many hours per week you’re available Confirm you can write/edit copy (or use AI) and create images when needed Write or refine conversion-focused copy (or use AI) that resonates with our audience In addition, I need support with product imagery. I don’t want to hire a photographer or take new photos—instead, you’ll work with the images I provide, enhancing them or creating new variations using AI or editing tools to produce high-quality, conversion-focused visuals. Complete copy with image directions, brand identity files, and detailed brief ready to hand over immediately. Thanks, Dhruv, Ocupets
title: Figma/Replo Ecom Shopify Landing Page

description: MALT BRIEF — Server-Side Tracking Repair Meta CAPI · GA4 Measurement Protocol · Shopify Headless · Next.js / Vercel CONTEXT Zelder (zelder.fr) is a premium French e-commerce brand selling senior furniture. - Storefront: zelder.fr — Next.js on Vercel - Checkout: boutique.zelder.fr — native Shopify subdomain THE PROBLEM The Meta Pixel loses first-party cookie context when users transition from zelder.fr to boutique.zelder.fr at checkout. Purchase events are not tracked, ROAS attribution is severely underreported, and running reliable paid campaigns is currently impossible. ALREADY IN PLACE (PARTIAL) - Vercel API routes ready - Meta CAPI — partial implementation - GA4 Measurement Protocol — partial implementation - Shopify webhooks — partial TECH STACK - Frontend: Next.js (App Router) - Hosting: Vercel - E-commerce: Shopify Plus headless - Client tracking: Meta Pixel + GA4 gtag - Server tracking: Vercel API Routes (Node.js) - Webhooks: Shopify orders/create to API routes DELIVERABLES 1. Full tracking audit (pixel, CAPI, webhooks) 2. Meta CAPI via Vercel API Routes — ViewContent, AddToCart, InitiateCheckout, Purchase — with client/server deduplication via event_id 3. GA4 Measurement Protocol — same event coverage, clean cross-domain session stitching 4. Cross-domain parameter passing — fbclid, _fbc, _fbp and GA4 client_id must survive the storefront to checkout transition 5. Validation — Meta Event Manager match rate over 70%, GA4 DebugView clean, Purchase coverage over 95% + technical documentation SCREENING QUESTION "Have you worked on a setup where the storefront and checkout live on two separate domains? How did you handle first-party cookie persistence and attribution parameter passing between them?" If the answer doesn't mention cross-domain cookie loss or CAPI server-side relay, keep looking. MISSION FORMAT - Type: Fixed-scope - Duration: 3–5 days - Remote: Yes, 100% - Response: Within 48h
title: Tracking Repair Meta CAPI · GA4 Measurement Protocol · Shopify Headless · Next.js / Vercel

description: We are looking for an experienced e-commerce website developer to design and build a Shopify e-commerce website for a new premium children’s outerwear brand. This is a launch project. We are not just looking for someone to build a website, but someone who can guide us through the process, advise on best practices, and help create a strong, conversion-focused online store. We are launching with a limited product range (one core product in two colours), so the website must feel complete, premium, and engaging despite a small number of products. This is our first website, and we are looking for someone experienced who can work collaboratively with us to define the right structure, functionality, and user experience. *Scope of Work (to be defined collaboratively)* The selected developer will: - Lead initial scoping discussions to define website structure, page layout, required functionality, and best-practice UX/UI for conversion - Design and build a Shopify website (custom or theme-based) - Deliver a clean, modern, premium, mobile-first design - Develop key pages as a minimum including homepage, product page(s), about page, and FAQ page - Implement functionality as a minimum including product variants (size, colour), inventory and stock logic, email capture (e.g. back-in-stock, early access or similar), and a fast and simple checkout - Integrate systems as a minimum, including payments (Shopify Payments, Apple Pay, etc.), shipping and fulfilment systems, and analytics (e.g. Google Analytics, Meta Pixel) - Ensure the site is fast-loading and performance optimised *Collaboration Requirements* You will: - Work closely with our brand identity and content teams - Clearly define requirements for photography (product and lifestyle) and video content (demonstrating product functionality) - Advise on how to structure content to maximise engagement and conversion - Provide guidance suitable for a first-time e-commerce launch *Person Requirements* - Proven experience building Shopify e-commerce websites Must provide links to live websites you have developed (applications without this will not be considered) - Demonstrated experience with e-commerce / direct-to-consumer brands - Ability to clearly explain your approach and previous decisions - Ability to provide ongoing support following launch. *Application Requirements* If interested, please: - Provide links to live e-commerce websites you have developed and specify what you personally delivered on each project - Include a rough cost estimate for a project of this nature - Include a brief explanation of how you would approach this project Thank you and looking forward to your proposals.
title: E-commerce Web Developer for Premium Children’s Brand (Launch Project)

description: THE ROLE We run fast-growing fashion dropshipping stores across the EU and UK and need someone to fully own customer operations. This is not a ticket-answering job. You will manage complex cases, own the full supply chain communication, handle disputes and chargebacks, report directly to the founder, and lead our transition to AI-automated customer service. You will be a key hire with real ownership and a direct line to the founder. As we scale to multiple stores, your role grows with us. YOU MUST HAVE HANDS-ON EXPERIENCE IN - Credit card dispute and chargeback management. EU and UK consumer law context is a strong plus. - Refund and return management with a revenue-protection mindset - Supplier communication and supply chain follow-up in a dropshipping context - Shopify order management and shipping exception handling - Disputifier or similar dispute management tools - AI-powered CS workflows. Comfortable building and working inside AI-first operations - Reporting to a founder or senior stakeholder. Metrics, patterns, and weekly updates OUR CURRENT STACK Shopify | Disputifier | Custom AI tools | Credit card processors | PayPal (incoming) We are building toward 70% AI-automated customer service. You will help design and implement that system, not just work inside it. WHAT STRONG LOOKS LIKE - You speak in results. Not "I managed tickets" but "I reduced chargeback rate from X to Y" - You understand EU and UK consumer rights well enough to use them strategically, not just react to them - You have built or improved a process that outlasted you - You are comfortable being held accountable to numbers every week - You see a problem and fix it before you are asked WHAT YOU GET - Real ownership. You build the systems. You lead the operations. - Growth path. As we scale to multiple stores and markets, your role grows with us. - Fully remote. Work from anywhere. - Competitive salary based on experience. We do not hire based on CVs alone. We hire based on how people think and how they show up. If you have read this entire post and you recognize yourself in it, you already know what to do. if you read every word of this post and you genuinely believe you are the right person for this role, start your proposal with "i'm the one" in capital letters and in one sentence tell us why. The people who notice this are exactly the people we are looking for. TO APPLY, ANSWER THESE IN YOUR PROPOSAL 1. What e-commerce operations experience do you have? Be specific about tools, volume, and results. 2. A customer in the UK received the wrong item and is threatening a chargeback. Walk us through exactly what you do, step by step. 3. Describe one process you built or improved. What was broken, what did you change, what was the result? Proposals without answers to these three questions will not be reviewed.

description: A Life Plus | Contract-to-Hire | Immediate Start We’re scaling a DTC meal brand to $10M+ net profit. We don’t hire “growth managers.” We hire operators who test, kill, and scale fast. Your Mission Increase new customers per week while keeping CAC at or below cap. You will: -Launch fast probe tests (72-hour cycles) -Run single-variable experiments -Make clear kill / scale decisions -Use Shopify as source of truth (not platform CPA) Success = Output (Not Activity) -CAC decreases or stays under cap -New customers per week increases -Tests launched every week -Kill rules enforced consistently -Every test produces a learning What You’ll Actually Do -Audit live campaigns (Meta + Google) -Identify the #1 constraint on CAC -Launch 1–2 tests immediately -Test creatives (UGC, hooks, angles), audiences (broad, LAL, segments), and offers (if needed) -Kill losers fast, scale winners -Log: test → result → decision Not a Fit If You: -Need weeks to analyze before acting -Rely on ROAS instead of real CAC -Can’t define numeric kill rules -Prefer strategy decks over execution Requirements -Hands-on Meta (Facebook/Instagram) + Google Ads experience -Proven ability to reduce CAC or increase new customers in 7–14 days -Strong testing mindset (clear hypothesis → result → decision) -Comfortable working under strict CAC constraints -Can manage agencies inside a framework (not be managed by them) Setup -Hourly: $20–$50/hr -Start with a 7-day paid test sprint -Long-term role for top performers -Multiple operators hired — best performers stay
title: CAC Owner – Lower CAC Fast (Meta / Google | DTC eCommerce)

description: Freelancers only. No agencies. IMPORTANT: Loom Video Required (No Written Proposals) Submit ONLY a Loom video link. No text, no portfolio links, no additional information. description: Hi, I’m looking for a Conversion Rate Optimization (CRO) specialist to review my WooCommerce store and identify opportunities to improve conversions and overall customer experience. What I’m looking for: - A full CRO audit of my website (especially product pages and checkout) - Identify friction points affecting conversions - Suggestions to improve product page clarity, trust, and buying motivation - Recommendations to improve bundle presentation and increase AOV - Feedback on mobile vs desktop experience - Actionable, specific suggestions (not generic advice) Important: - The site currently has some technical fixes in progress (cart, layout, etc.), so this is focused on conversion and UX improvements after those are resolved - Experience with ecommerce (preferably WooCommerce) is required - Experience with bundles, upsells, or similar products is a big plus Deliverable: - A clear list of prioritized recommendations (what to fix first, what will have biggest impact) - Optional: quick mockups or examples if relevant This is a small initial project, but if we’re a good fit, there may be ongoing work. To apply, please include: - Examples of ecommerce stores you’ve optimized - Results you’ve achieved (conversion rate, AOV, etc.) - Your approach to CRO audits Thank you!

description: 💼 Who We're Looking For We’re looking for a Senior Fashion eCommerce Strategist & Planner who understands exactly what to launch, when to launch it, and why it will convert — per market. This is a pure strategy role. No basic execution. No hand-holding. You are the person who: Knows what’s trending before it peaks Understands seasonal timing per country Can map out campaign calendars that drive revenue If you can confidently say: 👉 “This is what we should launch next week in France — and this is why” …then keep reading. 🧠 About Us We’re a fast-growing Dutch DTC fashion group with 6 active stores across Europe, with our core markets: 🇫🇷 France 🇳🇱 Netherlands 🇬🇧 United Kingdom We are building scalable fashion brands, not just running stores. Our model: Weekly campaigns (themes, drops, flash sales) Performance-driven mindset (Meta + Google Shopping) Fast iteration based on data We need someone who can bring structure, direction, and winning ideas into this machine. 🎯 What You'll Work On As our Strategist & Planner, you will own the campaign calendar and market direction. Your responsibility: Define what goes live, when, and in which market Build weekly & monthly campaign planning Identify themes, hooks, and angles per country (FR / NL / UK) Advise on which products & collections to push Spot opportunities for: Flash sales Seasonal campaigns Cultural moments Trend-based drops Translate trends into clear action plans for the team Work closely with design & media buyers to align execution You will NOT just suggest ideas — You will create structured plans we can execute immediately. 🧩 You Should Be Able To... Understand fashion trends across EU markets (FR / NL / UK) Predict what products & styles will perform next Build campaign calendars (weekly + monthly) Translate trends into themes, offers, and angles Think in revenue, not just aesthetics Align strategy with paid media & Google Shopping Identify market-specific behavior differences Move fast and continuously optimize based on results 🇫🇷 🇳🇱 🇬🇧 Market Understanding Is Key You must understand differences like: France → more aesthetic, emotional, brand-driven Netherlands → more direct, value-driven, offer-focused UK → mix of trend + price sensitivity + fast-moving campaigns And know how to adapt: Themes Messaging Timing Product focus ✅ What We Expect Proven experience in fashion eCommerce strategy (non-negotiable) Strong track record with campaign planning & trend forecasting Experience across multiple EU markets (FR/NL/UK preferred) Ability to independently generate high-quality ideas weekly Structured thinker — you bring clarity, not chaos Fluent English Fast communication & proactive mindset Long-term vision — we are building brands We are looking for a true A-player strategist — not someone who just follows trends, but someone who translates them into profit. 📝 To Apply, Answer These: Give an example of a fashion campaign calendar you created. How do you decide what products or themes to push in a given week? What differences do you see between FR, NL, and UK markets? What would you launch next week in: France Netherlands UK (Be specific: theme + product + angle) How do you stay ahead of fashion trends and seasonal shifts? Have you worked with performance marketing teams (Meta / Google)? How do you align with them? Why are you the right person to guide our campaign direction across multiple brands? ⏰ This is NOT for beginners. If you are a “Pinterest trend saver” — skip this. If you need direction instead of giving it — this is not your role. But if you: Think ahead See opportunities others miss And can turn trends into structured, revenue-driving plans Then we want to hear from you.
title: Fashion eCommerce Strategist (Campaign Planning & Market Strategy | EU Focus)

description: We are looking for an experienced bookkeeping professional to assist with Shopify and Amazon payout reconciliation within Xero. In this role, you will be responsible for reconciling payouts received from Shopify and Amazon and ensuring they are accurately recorded in Xero. This includes matching sales, fees, refunds, and deposits to bank transactions and ensuring all records are properly categorized and aligned. You will also be expected to identify and resolve discrepancies between platform reports and bank statements, ensuring that revenue, fees, and net payouts are correctly reflected in the financial records. The goal is to maintain accurate and clean bookkeeping for all ecommerce sales channels. We are looking for someone with strong experience in Xero and ecommerce bookkeeping, particularly with Shopify and Amazon reconciliation. Familiarity with payout structures, fees, and integrations is highly important. The ideal candidate should be detail-oriented, reliable, and able to work independently while maintaining accuracy in complex transaction environments.
title: Shopify/Amazon Payout Reconciliation

description: 🚀 E-Commerce Manager (Growth & Revenue) - Scale a $1M/mo Health and Wellness DTC Brand We’re looking for someone who knows how to turn traffic into profit. Not someone who just tweaks pages. Not someone who runs random A/B tests. We want a growth operator who understands: 👉 What makes people buy 👉 How to increase profit per visitor 👉 How to build offers, funnels, and systems that drive real revenue 🧠 About Us We’re a fast-growing DTC eCommerce brand doing ~$1M/month, primarily through Meta (Facebook) ads, targeting US/UK/CA/AU markets. Our product sits in a highly emotional niche (health / relief / transformation), where messaging, positioning, and belief systems drive conversions. You’ll be working directly with the founder and a creative strategist in a fast-moving, performance-driven environment. 🎯 Your Core Mission 👉 Increase profit per session, not just conversion rate This includes: Improving conversion rate Increasing AOV Expanding LTV Building stronger offers 🔥 What You’ll Be Responsible For 1. Offer & Monetization Optimization Build and test new offers, bundles, and pricing structures Increase AOV and upsell take rate Identify and implement cross-sells and post-purchase offers Suggest new products based on data and customer behavior 2. Landing Pages & Funnels Build and iterate on high-converting landing pages Translate winning ad angles into page structure, copy, and flow Create: New main landing page Angle-specific landing pages Advertorial funnels 3. CRO Testing Engine Run continuous A/B tests (minimum 2 live at all times) Use tools like Intelligems (and potentially Triple Whale) Identify: Bottlenecks Drop-off points Revenue leaks 4. Data → Insights → Revenue Turn data into clear, actionable insights Focus on: Profit per session Conversion rate AOV / upsell performance Work closely with: Creative strategist (messaging & angles) Founder (strategy & direction) 👉 You will connect ad performance with on-site monetization 5. Customer Journey Optimization Improve key pages: Product page Homepage About page Optimize the entire buying experience from click to purchase 6. AI-Driven Execution Use AI tools for: Copywriting Research Creative ideation Move fast from idea → test → result 🧩 Tools & Stack Shopify (custom Shrine theme) Replo Figma Intelligems Potentially Triple Whale 📈 90-Day Success Metrics Increase CVR from ~3% → 4%+ Increase profit per session Improve AOV and upsell take rate Launch: 1 new winning landing page 2 Iterations on top-performing landing page so they are angle-specific towards the top performing angles Maintain 2+ active tests at all times ✅ What We’re Looking For 3+ years in eCommerce (Shopify, DTC) Experience in English-speaking markets (US, UK, CA, AU) Strong understanding of: Direct response copywriting Landing page psychology Offer creation & pricing AOV / LTV optimization Ability to: Execute (not just strategize) Think in revenue, not just conversion rate Work fast and independently 🚫 This Is NOT For You If You only analyze data but don’t execute You rely on developers for every small change You’ve only worked on small or low-traffic stores You don’t understand offers, upsells, or monetization You don’t use AI to increase speed and output You don't know how to manage your time efficiently and focus only on the tasks that have the biggest ROI potential. 💰 Compensation $4,000–$5,000/month Part-time (20 hours/week) Performance-based bonus tied to: Profit per session Revenue growth Conversion improvements 🔥 Why This Role Is Different You’ll work on a high-volume store with real data You’ll have direct impact on revenue You’ll help shape offers, not just pages You’ll operate in a fast, no-BS environment focused on results 📩 How to Apply Include: 2–3 examples of stores or funnels you’ve worked on Specific results (CVR, AOV, revenue impact) An example of how you increased AOV or LTV Your approach to improving profit per session and LTV If you’ve read this far, include the word “PROFIT” in your application so we know you pay attention to detail.
title: 🚀 E-Commerce Manager - Scale a $1M/mo Health and Wellness DTC Brand

description: About Us: We are Ideal Trading Limited, an established supplier of commercial kitchen, bakery, and refrigeration equipment based in Lami, Fiji. Since 2013, we have been supplying local restaurants, resorts, and businesses with high-quality commercial appliances, food preparation tools, and shelving units. We are currently expanding our digital presence and upgrading our backend operations (including Zoho Inventory integrations). We need an experienced SEO professional to optimize our website (idealtrading.com.fj) to capture high-intent, regional search traffic. Project Overview: Our website serves as a digital catalog for B2B buyers. The primary goal of this role is to improve our organic visibility for commercial equipment searches within Fiji and the broader South Pacific region including Vanuatu, Tonga, Solomon Islands etc. You will be responsible for conducting a complete technical audit, optimizing our category pages, and establishing a long-term local keyword strategy. Key Responsibilities: Comprehensive SEO Audit: Conduct an initial audit of idealtrading.com.fj to identify technical roadblocks, site speed issues, and indexing errors. Keyword Strategy & Mapping: Identify low-competition, high-intent B2B keywords specific to our market (e.g., "commercial refrigeration Fiji," "bakery equipment Suva," "industrial shelving units"). On-Page Optimization: Optimize title tags, meta descriptions, headers, and image alt-text across our main categories (Bakery Equipment, Kitchen Equipment, Shelving Units, Refrigeration, Food Service, etc.). Technical SEO: Ensure our e-commerce catalog structure is easily crawlable and properly indexed by Google. Local SEO: Optimize our local search presence to drive warehouse pickups and direct inquiries via WhatsApp/Viber/Email. Monthly Reporting: Track rankings, organic traffic growth, and conversion metrics using Google Search Console and Google Analytics.
title: SEO for Ideal Trading

You must set up the path so Etsy (ebay etc) orders flow through the new Shopify to the correct POD provider automatically • Order Flow Validation: Verify that sales from my established Etsy/eBay/Amazon stores successfully flow into Shopify and trigger the correct Print-on-Demand (Printify/Gelato) fulfillment path.
