Just need to confirm that’s complete and that sku’s map to defined structure • Collection Automation: Review and fix Shopify "Automated Collection" logic to ensure products are categorized correctly based on tags/types. • Low-Subscription Workflow: I want to avoid ongoing sync software like LitCommerce. I prefer a manual or "on-demand" batch process for seasonal catalog updates. You will recommend and implement a cost-effective method for this. • Hub Management: Finalize connections for eBay, Amazon, and Etsy. Ensure the shop is ready to scale to IG and Walmart in the future. • Shopify Store Audit: Verify the data migration and confirm that all necessary backend pages (policies, etc.) are functional. (No web design required). • Subscription Cleanup: Identify and remove unnecessary middleman apps; recommend low-cost/free alternatives for seasonal batch updates. • SOP: Provide process hand off notes for maintenance (short form) Requirements: • Expertise in Shopify Marketplace Connect or manual SKU linking. • Deep knowledge of Shopify Automated Collections (tag-based logic). • Experience with multi-vendor POD routing (Printify + Gelato). • Upwork Only. No off-site interactions. Availability Note: I am hiring because I lack the time to do this myself. description: We are seeking an experienced data engineer or automation specialist to build a fully automated, low-maintenance CEO dashboard. The goal is to provide a single "pane of glass" overview of our business performance (Trade, Ecommerce, Marketplaces, and Whole Company metrics) on a daily and weekly basis. The data currently lives across nine distinct platforms: Xero, Cin7, Amazon Sellerboard, A2X, Triple Whale, Shopify, HubSpot, Airwallex, and Cashflow Frog. Crucial Requirement: We want to avoid overcomplicating the architecture. The solution must be low-bug, easy to work with, highly reliable, and manageable in-house after handover. We already use Make.com and strongly prefer leveraging it as the orchestration layer to pull data into a central repository (like Google Sheets) and visualize it (e.g., Looker Studio or advanced Google Sheets charts). Note: While Make.com is our preferred approach for simplicity and maintainability, we are open to alternative architectures (e.g., Fivetran + BigQuery, or other off-the-shelf connectors) if you can make a compelling case that it will be more stable, cost-effective, and easy for a non-developer team to manage long-term. The freelancer will be responsible for the end-to-end delivery of the dashboard, specifically: 1 Architecture Design: Finalize the data flow. Our proposed baseline is using Make.com to query the APIs on a schedule, transform the JSON responses, and append the daily/weekly KPI values into a structured Google Sheet, which then feeds Looker Studio. 2 Data Extraction & Integration: Set up the Make.com scenarios (or alternative pipelines) for the 7 APIs. Handle OAuth authentication flows (especially Xero and HubSpot) and secure credential storage. 3 Data Transformation: Ensure metrics like "Total Orders" accurately combine Shopify, Amazon, and Cin7 data without double-counting. 4 Dashboard Build: Design and build the visualization layer. It must be clean, executive-level, and easy to read on desktop and tablet. 5 Cashflow Frog Workaround: Implement a solution for the 30-Day Cash Outlook, either by having Make.com parse scheduled Excel exports from Cashflow Frog or by recreating the calculation logic within the dashboard. 6 Handover & Documentation: Provide a recorded walkthrough and written documentation on how to maintain the Make.com scenarios, update API keys if they expire, and make minor changes to charts. Ideal Candidate Profile We are looking for a freelancer with the following qualifications: • Proven experience building executive dashboards using Looker Studio, Power BI, or advanced Google Sheets. • Strong experience integrating with accounting and ecommerce APIs, specifically Xero, Shopify, and HubSpot. • Deep expertise in Make.com (Integromat) for complex API orchestration and data transformation. • A pragmatic approach to problem-solving—favoring robust, maintainable solutions over complex, over-engineered systems. • Excellent communication skills and the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.
title: Build a CEO dashboard including multi-platform integration

description: I am looking for an experienced Shopify developer to build a small, lightweight custom app or script to solve a limitation with the Shopify Facebook/Instagram sales channel. Currently, the discount codes automatically generated by the FB/IG channel do not allow customers to combine them with shipping discounts. I need a custom Shopify solution developed to automate this adjustment so my customers can successfully check out using both discounts. **How the App Should Work:** The solution needs to automatically "watch" my Shopify store for new discount codes generated by the FB/IG sales channel. * Whenever a new code is created (typically formatted like `FB-Email - [code]`), the app must instantly update its settings via the Shopify Admin API to **allow combining with shipping discounts**. * This needs to happen automatically in the background with zero manual work required on my end. **Key Requirements & Expected Behavior:** 🔸 **Maintain Exclusivity:** Each customer still gets their own unique, one-time-use code (as originally intended by the sales channel) to keep fraud/sharing risks low. 🔸 **Fully Automated:** Once set up, the process must be 100% hands-off. 🔸 **Tech Stack:** You can build this as a custom/private Shopify app or a serverless script utilizing Shopify Webhooks and the Admin API (GraphQL , and lightweight solution.
title: Custom Shopify App Development for Discount Updates

Social Media: Add a custom "Follow Us" call-out immediately above the social media links in the bottom right corner. Core Pages: Build out dedicated "About Us" and "Contact Us" pages and link them within the main navigation. "How It Works" Landing Page: Build this page to include four distinct holding spots for upcoming video media. Checkout & Shipping Configuration Free Shipping Threshold: Configure the system to automatically offer $0 shipping on all carts over $150. Payments & Backend Systems Authorize.net Integration: Connect Authorize.net to the checkout. We will provide the API Login ID and Transaction Key. description: I am seeking a highly technical Shopify Developer to perform a deep-clean and genuine performance optimization of my one-product store. I think there are speed cloaking scripts in my Shopify store, which are giving a fake high score in PageSpeed Insights & GTmetrix. The site uses GemPages for the main product page, which features one video and several high-resolution images. Crucially, the site has Server-Side Tracking (SST) and Enhanced Conversion tracking active. My goal is to completely remove the "fake" performance layer and replace it with legitimate, white-hat optimizations without interfering with any existing tracking or data layers. Project Scope: 1. Forensic Removal: Locate and strip all cloaking scripts from all possible locations, including but not limited to theme.liquid, snippets, assets, GemPages custom code/scripts, and the Shopify ScriptTag API. The goal is a 100% clean environment. 2. Tracking Integrity: All optimizations must be implemented without interfering with existing GTM/Server-Side Tracking. No tracking pixels or conversion triggers should be deferred or blocked. 3. GemPages & Product Page Optimization: Legitimate optimization of the GemPages landing page and the default Shopify product template. 4. Media Handling: Implement proper facades for the video and convert images to WebP/AVIF with correct fetch priorities. 5. Restoration: Ensure the removal of hacks does not break GemPages, the cart, or the checkout flow. All work must be performed on a duplicate theme first. Milestones & Payment: Milestone 1: Discovery, Audit & Clean Baseline ($60) • Scan all theme files, GemPages custom code, and content_for_header for bot-detection logic. • Deliverable: A list of affected files and a transparent performance baseline with cloaking disabled. Milestone 2: Removal, Restoration & Real Optimization ($230) • Manually extract cloaking code on a duplicate theme. • Tracking Safety Check: Use GTM Preview mode to confirm that existing tracking tags (SST/Enhanced Conversions) continues to trigger as they did prior to optimization. The freelancer is responsible for ensuring optimization scripts do not block or delay these pre-existing triggers. • Optimize GemPages assets and fix Core Web Vitals (LCP, CLS) without black-hat blocking. Milestone 3: Verification and Monitoring ($60) • 48-hour observation window to ensure "ghost scripts" do not re-inject. • Final Audit: Payment is subject to a technical review confirming zero bot-detection logic and proof that GTM is still passing hashed customer data correctly. Requirements: • Expert-level Shopify Liquid and Advanced JavaScript. • Specific experience with GemPages and its custom code limitations. • Proven experience removing "fake speed" scripts (mention this in your proposal). • Deep understanding of Core Web Vitals (LCP, CLS, INP).
title: Shopify Forensic Audit: Remove Speed Cloaking + Real Optimization (GemPages / One-Product Store)

description: We are looking for an experienced WooCommerce developer (not just a page builder) to build a bundle product system on our site. This can be done either: - using an existing bundle plugin (and heavily customizing it), OR - building a custom solution from scratch The priority is accuracy of pricing, cart, and checkout logic, while matching a clean, modern ecommerce UI (reference to be provided). Scope of Work We want to create a bundle product page that includes: --- 1. Bundle Structure * Bundle consists of multiple products (e.g. mattress + bed frame) * Backend SKUs must remain tied to individual products * Orders should reflect correct individual items (not just a single bundle SKU) --- 2. Variant & Size Syncing * Products have variants (e.g. sizes: Single, Queen, King) * When a user selects a size, it must apply to all products in the bundle simultaneously * Variant pricing must update dynamically based on selection --- 3. Pricing Logic (Critical) * Bundle price must be pulled dynamically from individual product prices based on selected variants * Apply a bundle discount (%) * Display: * original combined price * discounted bundle price * savings amount / percentage * No hardcoding — pricing must stay in sync if product prices change --- 4. UI / UX Requirements * Display mini product blocks within the bundle: * thumbnail * product name * key info * Each mini product should open a lightbox/modal with more details * Clean, premium UI (similar to Koala reference) * Must integrate well with Flatsome theme --- **5. Add to Cart & Checkout (Critical)** * Add-to-cart must correctly add all bundle items with correct variants * Cart must reflect accurate pricing and not conflict with existing WooCommerce logic * Checkout must preserve correct itemisation and totals * No duplication, mismatch, or pricing errors --- **6. Compatibility & Stability** * Must work with existing WooCommerce setup and plugins * No breaking of: * current product pages * cart behaviour * checkout flow --- **Technical Expectations** * WooCommerce hooks & filters * Custom pricing logic (e.g. before_calculate_totals) * AJAX / dynamic updates * Custom plugin or extendable architecture * PHP, JS, WooCommerce internals Bonus: * Experience with bundle/upsell systems * Shopify/Liquid experience --- **What We’re Looking For** * Someone who can handle both: * frontend implementation (replicating UI cleanly) * backend logic (pricing, cart, variants) * Strong problem-solver (not just plugin installer) * Attention to detail — pricing accuracy is critical --- **To Apply, Please Include** 1. Relevant WooCommerce work (especially bundles, cart, or pricing logic) 2. Examples of: * custom product pages * upsell/bundle implementations 3. Brief explanation of: * how you would approach dynamic bundle pricing + variant syncing --- We are looking for someone we can continue working with for future ecommerce enhancements.
title: WooCommerce Developer (Flatsome) – Custom Bundle Builder with Accurate Pricing & Cart Logic

Our products are crafted with natural, high-performance materials and sold across both Amazon and our Shopify store in the US market. We are a fast-growing, design-led brand with a strong focus on quality, customer experience, and continuous improvement. As we scale, we are looking for a talented Graphic Artist to help bring our brand to life across multiple channels. The Opportunity This is a flexible, ongoing contract role for a skilled Graphic Artist based in the Asia Pacific region. You’ll work closely with our content and marketing team to produce high-quality visual assets across Amazon, Shopify, and paid media. You’ll be part of a team that values clean design, thoughtful storytelling, and performance-driven creativity. What You’ll Be Working On Amazon Creative (Core Focus) Listing images, A+ Content, Brand Story, and Storefront assets Ongoing creative updates for campaigns, promotions, and product launches Iteration and testing of creatives to improve conversion performance Designing for both desktop and mobile optimisation Video Content Short-form video for Amazon (how-to, Q&A, product storytelling) Sponsored Brand video ads Repurposing customer insights and feedback into engaging video content Paid Social (Meta) Static and video ad creatives for Meta campaigns Working with existing assets and stock to produce high-performing ads Shopify & Website Visual assets for product pages, blogs, and campaign updates Adapting Amazon content for D2C use YouTube / Short-Form Content Editing UGC into short-form video (e.g. YouTube Shorts for Demand Gen campaigns) About You You are a designer who balances creativity with performance. You have 3+ years of experience in graphic design and content production You are highly proficient in Photoshop, video editing tools, and comfortable using AI tools in your workflow You create work that feels clean, modern, and elevated You’re comfortable taking direction, but also bring your own ideas forward You work efficiently, manage multiple deadlines, and communicate clearly You are adaptable and open to feedback and iteration You enjoy working as part of a collaborative, remote team Tools & Workflow You’re comfortable working across tools such as: Adobe Creative Suite (especially Photoshop) Video editing software (e.g. Premiere Pro, CapCut, or similar) Canva, Miro Slack and Google Drive How We Work Flexible hours across the week (15–30 hours) Fully remote, with a focus on async collaboration Monthly all-hands (1 hour) + project-based creative brief sessions Clear systems for asset management, feedback, and iteration Close collaboration with our Amazon and marketing teams Compensation US$18–$25 per hour, depending on experience Ongoing contract with consistent workload How to Apply To be considered, please submit: A cover letter outlining your experience and approach Your CV A portfolio showcasing relevant work (Amazon, eCommerce, or performance creative is highly valued) Why join our team Work with a fast-growing, premium eCommerce brand in the US market Be part of a team that values design, detail, and continuous improvement Contribute directly to creative that drives measurable performance Flexible, long-term opportunity with room to grow

We offer virtual coaching programmes, coach training courses, and swim camps. Our audience is primarily adults aged 40–75 who want to swim better. description: Overview: We are a growing concrete coatings distribution company (80% B2B, 20% direct-to-consumer) looking to build a scalable Shopify-based ecommerce system. We need an experienced Shopify developer (or small agency) who understands B2B ecommerce, pricing structures, and system integrations - not just basic storefront design. Project Goals: - Build a clean, modern Shopify website - Implement customer-specific and tiered pricing (without Shopify Plus) - Create a system that supports phone orders, POS, and internal sales reps - Integrate with QuickBooks Online for clean accounting - Set up a foundation that can scale with inventory and order volume Core Requirements: 1. Shopify Setup - Shopify Advanced plan - Clean, professional design (industrial / contractor-focused branding) - Mobile optimized - Fast loading 2. B2B Pricing System (CRITICAL) We need a solution for: - Customer-specific pricing (different pricing per account) - Tiered pricing (contractors vs retail vs VIP) - Ability to adjust pricing quickly - Customer login portal with correct pricing visibility Preferred approach: - Shopify-compatible wholesale pricing app (e.g., Wholesale Gorilla, Bold, or similar) - Open to your recommendation if you’ve implemented better solutions 3. Internal Order System / POS - Shopify POS setup - Ability for team to: - Take phone orders - Create draft orders - Override pricing manually when needed - Simple workflow for customer service team 4. Inventory Setup - Proper SKU structure - Organized product catalog (including kits/bundles if applicable) - Inventory synced across: - Online store - POS - Future-ready for integration with tools like Cin7 or Fishbowl 5. QuickBooks Integration - Integration with QuickBooks Online - Set up via A2X or similar tool - Ensure: - Orders sync cleanly - No duplicate entries - Proper tax handling 6. Payments - Standard checkout for retail customers - Ability to support invoice-style or manual payments for B2B orders Nice to Have (Bonus): - Experience building B2B ecommerce stores - Experience with distribution / materials / construction-related businesses - Experience with Shopify POS workflows - Recommendations on improving operational efficiency Deliverables: - Fully functional Shopify website - B2B pricing system implemented - POS configured and tested - QuickBooks integration working - Basic training / walkthrough for our team To Apply, Please Include: 1. Examples of Shopify stores you’ve built (especially B2B or complex pricing) 2. Which apps/tools you would use for this project and why 3. Your recommended approach for handling customer-specific pricing WITHOUT Shopify Plus 4. Estimated timeline and cost We are looking for someone who can think strategically and help us build the right system, not just complete tasks. Long-term relationship preferred!
title: Shopify Developer Needed – B2B Ecommerce + Custom Pricing + POS + QuickBooks Integration

description: We are looking for a Shopify Liquid developer to build custom landing pages directly inside Shopify using clean and scalable theme sections. This role also includes setting up key marketing and tracking integrations. We are looking for someone who understands Shopify architecture deeply, can build production ready Liquid sections, and is comfortable working on real ecommerce stores with performance and tracking accuracy in mind. No page builders will be used. Everything must be built natively inside Shopify themes.
title: Shopify Liquid Developer Needed for Landing Page Conversion

description: I’m the owner of a business loan consultancy/brokerage firm, and I’m looking to set up HubSpot Starter as a clean, simple system to manage my leads, consultations, and loan pipeline. My primary goals are: • A clear deal pipeline (lead → consultation → underwriting → approval → funded) • Clean contact segmentation (SBA leads, foreign nationals, investors, referral partners, networking contacts) • Integration with Google Calendar for scheduling (Google remains my primary calendar, using Google Meet) • Meeting links that create contacts and log activity automatically • A simple, structured system that a VA can manage for newsletters, contact updates, and follow-ups • Basic automation only (no complex workflows) I also use tools like HiHello (for networking), Wix (website), and DocuSign, and I may want to integrate selectively—but only where there’s a clear benefit. Very important: I do NOT want an overly complicated setup. My priority is usability, clarity, and long-term scalability without needing constant technical support. Please include answers to the following questions, in your response: How would you structure the pipeline and contact properties for a business like mine? How do you ensure the system stays simple enough for daily use (especially for a VA)? Do you recommend using Zapier for HiHello → HubSpot integration, or would you handle this another way? What would your setup include specifically within the HubSpot Starter plan (no upgrades)? Do you provide a walkthrough or SOP so I can confidently manage the system after delivery? Please do not apply if your primary experience is ecommerce funnels, Shopify, or heavy marketing automation. I am specifically looking for someone strong in CRM structure, sales pipeline management, and HubSpot Starter optimization for a service-based business. If this aligns with your approach, I’d be happy to discuss further.
title: HubSpot Starter CRM Setup for SBA Loan Brokerage & Lead Pipeline Management

description: Important: This is a B2B lead-generation site, not an eCommerce store. There are no products to buy — the goal is to generate quote requests from food businesses, supplement brands, and retailers. We are looking for someone with specific experience in B2B services SEO, not just general eCommerce SEO. About We are a new B2B launch offering co-packing, contract food manufacturing, private label production, dry blending, R&D, and packaging services. SQF Level 3, FDA registered, Kosher, HACCP, and GMP certified. The site runs on Shopify and has 50+ individual service pages covering food production types, packaging formats, filling services, warehousing, and design. The goal of SEO is to rank for high-intent B2B search terms and convert that traffic into quote requests. What you'll own -GA4 setup with form submission tracking — quote requests must be tracked as goal conversions -Keyword research for B2B intent: co-packing services, contract food manufacturing, private label food production, food co-packer near me, dry blend manufacturer etc. -Rewrite all core service pages — each needs 400–600+ words of keyword-targeted copy and a clear CTA -Build internal linking architecture across 50+ service pages — establish clear content hierarchy -Implement Organization, Service, and LocalBusiness schema markup -Launch and manage a B2B content blog — educational articles targeting high-intent research queries -Backlink outreach: food industry directories, trade publications, co-packing associations -Technical SEO: Shopify service site audit, canonical tags, crawl budget management across 50+ pages -Weekly reporting on rankings, organic traffic, and — most importantly — quote request conversions What we're looking for -4+ years of SEO experience with a specific focus on B2B services or lead-generation sites -Proven case studies showing lead or conversion growth from organic SEO — not just traffic -Experience with GA4 goal/conversion tracking for non-eCommerce lead-gen sites -Strong B2B content writing ability — you need to write authoritatively about food manufacturing, co-packing, and private label production -Experience managing SEO for sites with large numbers of service or location pages -Hands-on with GA4, Google Search Console, Ahrefs or SEMrush, and schema markup tools -Bonus: experience in food, manufacturing, CPG, or B2B industrial niches -Available for a weekly 30-minute check-in and self-directed in execution Non-negotiables -Weekly documented output report — no exceptions -All work must tie to rankings, traffic, or quote request conversions — not vanity metrics -Underperformance will result in replacement within 1–2 weeks
title: B2B SEO specialist — (ongoing, ~20 hrs/week)

description: We are Flou, an e-commerce company preparing to launch a new, highly-curated product. We are moving away from generalist stores and are building a highly focused, conversion-optimized one-product store. We need a top-tier designer who understands how to build an entire brand experience around a single hero item. Our infrastructure is strictly built on the Shrine Pro theme. We need a versatile designer who deeply understands this specific theme's architecture, e-commerce UI/UX, user behavior, and direct-response principles. We value clean, immersive, and highly credible designs that guide the user straight to the checkout. Scope of Work & Expectations: One-Product Store Architecture (Shrine Pro): Structure and design a high-converting, single-product funnel leveraging Shrine Pro's native blocks (long-form scrolling landing pages, sticky add-to-cart, bundle widgets, custom trust sections) without bloating the site with third-party apps. Brand World Building: Design a cohesive visual identity (colors, typography, hero banners, lifestyle imagery) that makes this single product look like an established, premium brand. End-to-End Graphic Creation: You will be responsible for creating all the necessary visual assets. This includes landing page graphics, benefit-driven infographics, GIF creation (if needed for demonstrations), trust badges, and custom icons. Product Image Optimization: Retouch and elevate supplier images to look premium. Create lifestyle mockups and feature callouts that highlight the product's unique selling propositions. Revisions & Adjustments (Flou Standard): We do not work with a fixed cap on revisions. This project includes all necessary tweaks, adjustments, and changes requested by our team until we are 100% satisfied with the final, conversion-ready design. Requirements: MANDATORY: Proven, verifiable experience customizing and designing specifically for the Shrine Pro Shopify theme, specifically for one-product or single-hero-item stores. Strong graphic design and photo manipulation skills (Photoshop, Figma, Illustrator, etc.). A strong portfolio showcasing clean, modern, and conversion-optimized e-commerce landing pages. Ability to tell a story visually, moving the customer from problem-awareness to product-solution within a single page scroll. How to Apply: To be considered for this role, please include the following in your proposal: Links to your portfolio featuring your best e-commerce projects (Explicitly point out which ones were built using Shrine / Shrine Pro and which are one-product stores). A brief mention of your workflow for customizing premium themes and handling feedback. Start your proposal with the phrase "ONE PRODUCT SHRINE" so we know you read the details.
title: Expert Shopify Web Designer & Brand Builder (One-Product Store / Shrine Pro)

description: Shopify Developer Needed for High-End Custom Fashion Website (Pixel-Perfect Build) Description: I am looking for a highly skilled Shopify developer + UI implementer to build a custom e-commerce website based on predefined visual direction and design references. This is NOT a basic Shopify theme customization job. I need someone who can translate detailed visual references into a fully functional, high-end website with strong attention to detail. Project Overview: Platform: Shopify Industry: Fashion / Vintage / High-end streetwear Inventory: One-of-one pieces (500+ items) Design Direction: Minimal, high-fashion, editorial feel with custom UI elements Scope of Work: Build a pixel-accurate Shopify storefront based on provided visual references Implement custom layouts (not template-based) Develop key pages: Homepage Shop / Collection pages Product pages About page Customer account dashboard Returns management section Custom navigation (including dropdown + horizontal menu variations) Mobile optimization (must match desktop design quality) Integrate product upload functionality (easy for me to swap images/content later) Design Requirements: Clean, modern, high-fashion aesthetic Strong typography + spacing discipline Dark and off-white UI system (not default Shopify white) Smooth UX (hover states, transitions, interactions) IMPORTANT: I will provide visual direction images and mockups. Your job is to recreate them as closely as possible in a live Shopify environment. DO NOT APPLY IF: You only work with pre-built themes You cannot match designs exactly You rely heavily on page builders (unless used minimally) Ideal Candidate: Strong experience with Shopify Liquid, HTML, CSS, JS Experience building custom Shopify themes from scratch Background in fashion / editorial-style e-commerce is a plus Ability to think like both a developer and a designer To Apply: Please include: Links to custom Shopify sites you’ve built (no templates) Examples of pixel-accurate builds from design files (Figma, etc.) Brief explanation of your approach to recreating designs from references Budget: Open to proposals (targeting quality over cheapest option) Timeline: Looking to start immediately This is a serious build with long-term potential for ongoing work if executed well.

description: Full-Time Shopify Manager — Monthly Retainer We're looking for a full-time Shopify manager to own the build side of our store — so our strategists and designers can focus on strategy and design instead of clicking around in the backend. This is a monthly retainer, full-time, long-term. One store, one focus. Not a one-off project and not juggling multiple clients. What you'll actually do day-to-day: — Build and iterate pages — PDPs, collection pages, landing pages. Some in GemPages, some directly in the theme. — Small theme edits: liquid, HTML/CSS tweaks, adding trust bars, spec tables, upsell sections, fixing mobile issues — Install and configure apps (reviews, upsells, analytics, tracking) — Polish — catching the details a designer or strategist would miss because they're not in the builder all day We give you briefs, Figma files, and copy. You make it live, make it work on mobile, and flag anything that looks off before we do. You're the right person if: — You know Shopify deeply — theme editing, liquid, sections, apps — and GemPages is a real part of your toolkit, not something you used once. Tell us how long you've worked in both and on what kinds of stores. — You have taste. You can tell the difference between a page that converts and one that just exists. When a brief has a gap, you fill it the right way instead of asking five clarifying questions. — You work independently. We don't babysit, and we don't want to. — Your timezone overlaps meaningfully with European working hours (we're EU-based), or you can commit to responding inside the working day. — You're fast. Velocity matters — if a build takes two weeks when it should take two days, it doesn't work for us. You're not the right person if: — You need a PM to assign you tickets one at a time — You've only done Shopify theme work through the theme editor — "Design sense" means matching the mockup pixel-perfect without thinking To apply, please answer these two questions at the top of your proposal. Generic proposals get skipped. 1. Link to one build you're proud of (GemPages or theme-based). One sentence on why it's good. 2. What's your working timezone and how many hours a day can you realistically commit? We read every proposal that answers these. We don't read the ones that don't.
title: Full-Time Shopify Manager — Monthly Retainer

description: 🚨 MUST WORK US HOURS (PST OR EST) — NON-NEGOTIABLE 🚨 Phoenix CRM Support Specialist (Ecommerce / Checkout Systems) We are building Phoenix, a next-generation ecommerce CRM and checkout platform similar to CheckoutChamp, Sticky.io, and Connective, and we’re looking for a highly capable Support Specialist to join our team. This is a hands-on role working directly with merchants to build, troubleshoot, and optimize checkout experiences. ⸻ 🔥 Requirements (Read Carefully) • MUST WORK US HOURS (PST OR EST) — NON-NEGOTIABLE • Strong experience with Shopify (front-end + integrations) • Experience with ONE OR MORE of the following: • CheckoutChamp • Sticky.io • Connective • Comfortable working in fast-paced, high-growth environments • Strong problem-solving mindset and ability to work independently ⸻ 🛠️ What You’ll Be Doing You will be working directly inside our platform helping merchants launch and scale: • Build custom checkout pages using our checkout builder • Build and optimize upsell / post-purchase flows • Configure subscription and billing logic (rebills, trials, etc.) • Map products to correct billing structures and offers • Implement third-party redirects and integrations • Assist with Shopify front-end work (themes, linking, tracking, etc.) • Troubleshoot issues and provide hands-on support to merchants ⸻ 💻 Technical Expectations • Comfortable with HTML / CSS (required) • Basic JavaScript knowledge (preferred) • Ability to make custom UI adjustments when needed • Familiarity with: • tracking pixels / attribution • checkout flows and funnel logic
title: CRM Management (Sticky.io Konnektive CheckoutChamp Shopify Experience please)

description: We run a portfolio of UK and US fashion Shopify stores and are hiring a long-term product lister. You'll be listing new products, building size charts, and creating AI product images. This role uses OUR OWN in-house listing app (not the standard Shopify admin). You must be comfortable learning a new tool and following our template exactly. WHAT YOU'LL DO - List new products per week across multiple fashion Shopify stores - Work inside our custom listing app (training provided) - Use Claude AI to write product titles, descriptions, and metadata following our exact template - Create accurate size charts for both UK and US markets (inches + centimeters, UK vs US sizing differences) - Use correct spelling per market: UK stores use British spelling (colour, favourite, organisation), US stores use American spelling (color, favorite, organization). No mistakes tolerated. - Generate AI product images using Higgsfield Nano Banana Pro, maintaining consistent model faces and brand aesthetic across entire catalog - Upload images, set up color/size variants, assign collections and tags - Follow our product listing SOP without deviation MUST-HAVE EXPERIENCE - Confirmed hands-on experience using Claude AI (Anthropic) for writing product content, not just ChatGPT - Confirmed hands-on experience using Higgsfield Nano Banana Pro for AI fashion images - Experience creating size charts with UK and US conversions - Strong awareness of UK vs US English differences (spelling, measurement units, sizing) - Detail-obsessed, able to follow templates exactly without improvising - Fluent written English BONUS - Experience with UK and USA fashion market - Portfolio of AI fashion images with consistent model faces across multiple products - Comfort learning proprietary apps and custom workflows HOW TO APPLY Applications that do not follow these exact steps will be rejected: 1. Start your cover letter with the word "BANANA" 2. Record a 2-3 minute Loom video introducing yourself and covering: - Your Shopify fashion listing experience - Your experience with Claude AI (show me a real example of how you've used it for product content) - Your experience with Higgsfield Nano Banana Pro (show one or two AI product images you've generated, explain the prompt strategy) - How you handle UK vs US spelling and measurement differences in listings 3. Paste the Loom link in your cover letter 4. Include 2-3 links to Shopify fashion stores where you have listed products Text-only applications will be rejected. If you cannot make a Loom video, do not apply. Generic AI-written cover letters will be rejected immediately. Keep it short, specific, real. We pay on time, respect your time, provide clear SOPs and training. Looking to build a long-term relationship with the right person. Strong performers scale up to full-time.

description: We need to migrate a multi-country/multilingual website ae.scotch-soda.com and sa.schotch-soda.com from magento to shopify. There will be two countries UAE, and Saudi Arabia, and two languages English and Arabic per country. We will use Impulse Shopify Team for the frontend Price and Stock File Imports: A stock file and price will be uploaded to an FTP daily. We need a cron to read the files and update the price and stock once daily in the morning. Data Migration: Products, Orders, Customers data migration will be handled by another vendor. Payment Options: 4 payment options Checkout - Shopify plugin available and needs configuration Tabby - Shopify plugin available and needs configuration Tamara - Shopify plugin available and needs configuration Cash on Delivery - Additional AED/SAR 15 surcharge needs to be charged for cash on delivery orders. Shipping: Free shipping on all orders except for orders from outlet category. Baskets having all items from outlet category need to be charged AED/SAR 15 shipping fee. Click and Collect from Store: Customers can choose to pickup their order from stores. Real-Time Stock check: On magento we have a module that does a real time stock check by calling our ERP via API endpoint. The API is called when the cart page is loaded. It returns the stock of the items in the basket, and if any item is out of stock, it gets removed from the basket. We need to re-use the same module from magento for shopify Promotions: These promotions need to be setup Always-On: 15% discount on All Non-discounted product using a coupon code Seasonal- Buy 2 get the lowest priced item free BUY 2 GET EXTRA 10% OFF, BUY 3 GET EXTRA 20% OFF & BUY 4 GET EXTRA 30% OFF Buy More Save More - Spend 1000 get 200 off or Spend 2000 get 400 off Theme: We will configure the theme pages - homepage, PLP, PDP, design, colours typography etc. We need assistance with the following: Designing the mega menu with imagesFixing the CSS for any RTL issues on the arabic pages Marketing: Product Feed order export Member Sale days: During member sale days, we need to hide products from the main categories and display them only in SALE categories. We had a feature in magento built to do this because it takes a lot of time to remove products. But in shopify if we can remove products in categories in bulk and then add them back again after few days by csv import, we dont need this feature.
title: Migration From Magento to Shopify

description: We're building a client portal that tracks program allocations, usage, and remaining balances. Before we can build it, we need to standardize our invoicing in Shopify — right now there's no clean separation between what's sold and what's fulfilled, which means there's no reliable data structure to map from. We need someone who can help us define that structure and build the invoice layer on top of it. What you'll do Advise on and define a program-level data schema in Shopify (program name, tier, unit allocation, rate per home, shipping, coverage period, total value) Set up the Shopify structure using metafields or structured line items — whichever best supports downstream data mapping Build a custom Order Printer invoice template that matches our visual mockup — clean, elevated, brand-aligned Ensure the invoice structure is consistent and reliable enough to serve as a data source for a future Airtable/portal integration Longer-term context Once this is in place, Shopify will be the source of truth for billing, and Airtable/the portal will track inventory allocated, usage, and remaining balance. We're sharing this context upfront so you can make informed recommendations on structure. What we're looking for Solid experience with Shopify metafields, Order Printer, and Liquid templating Comfortable thinking about data structure, not just visual design Detail-oriented with clean, well-commented code Nice to have Experience with Airtable — structuring bases, views, and relational data Familiarity with automation tools (Make, Zapier, or native Airtable automations) to connect Shopify and Airtable Experience building client portals with Softr — this is the direction we're heading for the portal layer We have a visual mockup ready to share. Please include examples of past Shopify invoice or template work in your proposal, and briefly describe how you'd approach structuring program-level data in Shopify. Bonus points if you have Airtable, automation, or Softr projects to show.
title: Shopify invoice template + data structure for client portal

description: Shopify Developer (Custom Product Builder Experience) Overview We are building a custom katana (sword) builder on Shopify and are looking for a developer with experience in interactive product customization tools. The immediate goal is to launch a lean V1 builder quickly, followed by iterative improvements into a more advanced experience (including 2D and eventually 3D customization). Project Scope (V1 – Immediate) We are starting with a simple but functional builder, focused on speed and usability. Key Features: Step-by-step product customization flow (multi-step UI) Option selection (e.g. blade, handle, guard, scabbard) Real-time price updates based on selections Product summary + add-to-cart integration Shopify checkout compatibility Technical Requirements: Works within Shopify (theme or app-based approach) Clean, responsive UI (desktop + mobile) Ability to handle configurable product logic Basic image swapping per option (2D visuals) Future Roadmap (Context Only) This project is part of a larger vision: V2: Enhanced 2D visuals (layered / dynamic previews) V3: Expanded customization logic and product catalog V4: Advanced 3D configurator (interactive model) Ideal Candidate We’re looking for someone who has: Experience building custom product builders / configurators Strong knowledge of Shopify (themes, apps, or headless setups) Experience with: JavaScript (React preferred but not required) Dynamic UI / state management Pricing logic and configurable products Bonus: Experience with 2D layering systems (canvas-based rendering) Experience with 3D tools (Three.js, WebGL, etc.) Deliverables (V1) Working builder on staging environment Fully functional customization flow Add-to-cart + checkout working Clean, maintainable structure for future upgrades Timeline Start: ASAP Target: Working V1 in ~5–8 business days (scope is already simplified) Notes This contract is focused strictly on delivering V1 (lean builder). If the project performs well, we plan to move forward with V2, V3, and V4 (enhanced 2D and eventually 3D configurator) under separate contracts. We’re ideally looking for a developer who can: Execute V1 quickly and cleanly And potentially continue with us long-term How to Apply Please include: Links to any product builders or configurators you’ve built Your approach to building this on Shopify Estimated timeline and cost for V1 Any recommendations for implementation approach We value speed, clarity, and communication. This is a fast-moving project with strong potential for ongoing work.

description: Nous recherchons un web designer expérimenté spécialisé sur Shopify pour réaliser la refonte complète du design de notre site e-commerce. Il est important de préciser que cette mission concerne uniquement la partie visuelle et l’expérience utilisateur (UX/UI). Le fond (copywriting, images, stratégie marketing) est déjà entièrement défini et ne devra pas être modifié. Notre objectif est clair : moderniser l’interface du site, améliorer la lisibilité, la hiérarchie visuelle et l’expérience utilisateur globale afin d’augmenter la conversion, tout en respectant scrupuleusement notre identité de marque. Nous disposons déjà d’un guide très précis comprenant : Logo Palette de couleurs Typographies Type scales Règles de design Instructions détaillées sur l’univers visuel de la marque Le travail attendu consiste donc à exécuter une refonte propre, cohérente et performante en s’appuyant sur ces éléments, sans repartir de zéro. Nous attendons un rendu moderne, premium, clair et efficace, adapté aux standards actuels du e-commerce. Les missions principales : Repenser le design global du site (homepage, pages produits, collections, etc.) Améliorer l’expérience utilisateur (navigation, lisibilité, parcours client) Créer des maquettes sur Figma (ou équivalent) Adapter le design aux contraintes Shopify Proposer une structure visuelle optimisée pour la conversion Assurer une cohérence graphique sur l’ensemble du site Optimiser le rendu mobile (très important) Profil recherché : Expérience confirmée en web design e-commerce (idéalement Shopify) Très bonne maîtrise de Figma ou outils similaires Bonne compréhension des enjeux UX/UI et conversion Capacité à travailler avec un guide de marque strict Sens du détail et exigence sur la qualité visuelle Autonomie et capacité à proposer des améliorations pertinentes Important : nous recherchons un profil qui parle français, afin de faciliter les échanges, la compréhension des consignes et la collaboration au quotidien. Pour postuler, merci de nous envoyer : Des exemples de maquettes Figma réalisées Des exemples de refontes de sites e-commerce Les thèmes Shopify avec lesquels vous avez l’habitude de travailler (Optionnel mais apprécié) : résultats obtenus en termes de conversion ou d’amélioration UX Nous cherchons une collaboration sérieuse avec un profil fiable, réactif et orienté résultats, capable de produire un travail propre et efficace rapidement. Si votre profil correspond, n’hésitez pas à postuler.

description: Overview I run a Shopify store with high‑AOV products and rely on Google Ads and Meta Ads. Tracking has been unreliable since a while. We need a true **expert** to audit, rebuild, and validate tracking across GTM, GA4, Google Ads, and Meta. Goals You will: - Audit and fix tracking for: - GTM - GA4 - Google Ads conversions & audiences - Meta pixel & Conversions API - Implement a clean event setup and ensure accurate reporting from click to purchase. - Document the setup so we can maintain and extend it. Events and Data to Track We want, at minimum: - Page view - View item / product view - View item list / collection view (if relevant) - Add to cart - Begin checkout - Add payment info (if available) - Purchase (with transaction ID, value, currency, items) - WhatsApp button click - Live chat button click - Book appointment / schedule call click For these events, send key parameters (e.g., product ID/SKU, value, currency, quantity, page URL, button location). You can propose additions based on best practice. Additionally: - Help‑me‑choose funnel events: - Form view (widget or section visible) - Form start (first interaction / first question answered) - Form step progress (optional but nice: step 1, step 2, step 3…) - Form submit / completed - Recommendation shown / recommendation selected We want a clear funnel view for this “help me choose” flow: how many users see it, start it, complete it, and select a recommendation, so we can see drop‑offs and performance by traffic source and device Scope 1) Audit - Review Shopify, GTM, GA4, Google Ads, Meta pixel/CAPI, and any tracking apps. - Identify broken, duplicate, or missing events, plus data mismatches between platforms. - Deliver a short audit with prioritized fixes. 2) Tracking plan - Define final event list and naming per platform. - Specify parameters and mapping: Shopify → data layer → GTM → GA4 / Google Ads / Meta. - Deliver a concise tracking plan (doc or sheet). 3) Implementation - Configure GTM tags, triggers, variables for all required events. - Fix/implement GA4 events and conversions, with working e‑commerce reporting. - Implement/fix Google Ads conversions and remarketing. - Implement/fix Meta pixel events and Conversions API with proper deduplication. 4) Testing & validation - Test full flow: landing → product → cart → checkout → purchase. - Validate events in GTM preview, GA4 DebugView, Google Ads, and Meta Events Manager. - Deliver a short validation summary plus a brief Loom or screenshots. 5) Handover - Provide GTM export/backup. - Short documentation or Loom explaining structure and how to add/change events. - 30–60 min handover call. Requirements Must have: - Strong experience with GTM, GA4 e‑commerce, Google Ads conversions, and Meta pixel/CAPI. - Proven Shopify tracking work (please show examples). - Ability to clean up a messy setup and deliver stable, validated tracking. - Clear communication. Nice to have: - Server‑side GTM, consent/cookie banner integrations, high‑AOV/medical‑adjacent e‑commerce. Proposal Instructions Please include: 1. 1–2 concrete project examples (Shopify or similar) with GTM + GA4 + Google Ads + Meta. 2. Your high‑level process (audit → plan → implementation → testing). 3. Confirmation of Shopify experience. 4. Estimated timeline for: - Audit + plan - Implementation + testing 5. Your price (one fixed fee preferred, or a clear breakdown). Project Details - Type: One‑time project with possible ongoing tracking tasks. - Start: ASAP. - Target timeline: - Audit + tracking plan: 5–7 days from hire. - Implementation + testing: within 2 weeks total.
title: Senior GTM / GA4 / Google Ads & Meta Tracking Specialist for Shopify

description: We are looking for a highly skilled n8n & AI Automation Architect to help us build and continuously improve a fully automated, AI-driven business. Our goal is to create an intelligent system where operations, sales, marketing, and content creation are largely automated — powered by advanced workflows and AI agent teams. This is a long-term role for someone who doesn’t just build automations, but designs scalable systems and continuously improves them. Initial Test Project To start, I have a small test project that I have not been able to complete myself. It involves a Gmail label management workflow for incoming emails. The automation works up to the point where the entry is created in Gmail, but the final node does not run reliably, and I have not been able to identify why. This initial task will help me evaluate your technical skills, troubleshooting approach, and overall way of thinking. If the collaboration works well, this can turn into a long-term engagement. Scope of Work: You will be responsible for designing, building, and evolving automation across the entire company: 1. Internal Operations • Automating internal workflows and processes • Connecting tools, databases, and communication systems • Reducing manual workload across teams 2. Sales Automation • Lead capture, qualification, and routing • Automated outreach and follow-ups • CRM and pipeline automation • Reporting and performance tracking 3. Marketing Automation • Funnel automation and campaign systems • Email workflows and segmentation • Data-driven marketing processes 4. AI Content Systems • Building automated content pipelines (social media, blogs, ads, etc.) • AI-assisted copywriting workflows • Scalable content generation systems 5. AI Agent Teams • Designing and implementing AI agent systems for different business functions • Orchestrating multi-agent workflows • Creating autonomous or semi-autonomous task execution systems • Continuously improving agent performance and reliability Core Responsibilities: • Architect, build, and optimize complex workflows using n8n • Integrate APIs, webhooks, CRMs, and AI tools • Design scalable, maintainable automation systems • Continuously analyze, improve, and evolve workflows and agents • Proactively identify inefficiencies and suggest better solutions • Ensure robustness, error handling, and performance optimization Must-Haves: • Advanced experience with n8n • Strong expertise in APIs, webhooks, and JSON • Experience building complex, multi-step automation systems • Hands-on experience with AI tools and LLM integrations • Experience building or working with AI agents or multi-agent systems • Strong troubleshooting and systems-thinking skills • Ability to work independently and take ownership Nice-to-Haves: • Experience with tools like Gmail, Google Workspace, Shopify, HubSpot, Make, Zapier, etc. • JavaScript / Node.js knowledge • Experience in E-Commerce or digital-first companies • Experience with prompt engineering and AI system design What We’re Looking For: We are not looking for a simple task executor. We want someone who can think in systems, solve technical issues independently, improve existing setups, and help build a scalable automation infrastructure for the long term. What We Offer: • Long-term collaboration • High-impact role in building a highly automated company • Full ownership over automation architecture • Remote work with flexible hours • Fast execution, no corporate bureaucracy To Apply, Please Include: 1. 2-3 advanced n8n workflows you’ve built 2. Examples of AI-driven automations or agent systems 3. A short explanation of how you would approach debugging unreliable workflow behavior 4. Your availability and hourly rate We are building a scalable, AI-powered company - and we’re looking for someone who wants to build, optimize, and evolve that system long-term.
title: Senior n8n & AI Automation Architect for Full Business Automation & AI Agent Systems (Long-Term)

description: Read this first. We're a fast-growing startup looking for a long-term email marketing partner, not a one-off gig. If you do great work with us, this role grows — more hours, higher rate, bigger scope, and a real seat at the table as the brand scales. We'd much rather find one person we can build with for years than burn through short-term hires. If you're looking for a stable, long-term engagement where your impact is directly visible in revenue, keep reading. Who we are. We're Futstrikers, a youth soccer ball brand on Shopify (futstrikersclub.com) with real momentum — growing customer base, expanding product line, an active membership program, and wholesale accounts coming online. We sell match balls DTC to players, families, and clubs, and we also run wholesale through the same storefront with segmented customer accounts. We have an in-house team (graphic designer, full-stack developers, brand leadership) and we're hiring on the marketing side because email is the next growth lever. The brand is past the "is this going to work" stage and into the "how fast can we scale it" stage. You'd be coming in at the exact right moment to own a channel from the ground up and grow your role alongside it. What we need. We're looking for an experienced Klaviyo marketer to own our email channel end-to-end — strategy, flow build, copy, segmentation, campaigns, and ongoing optimization. Our goal is simple: grow email-attributed revenue and expand our active subscriber base. Klaviyo will be connected to Shopify and ready to go before you start. You won't be setting up the account or importing lists — you'll be stepping into a live Klaviyo account, building out the segmentation architecture, designing and launching flows, writing copy, and running campaigns. Transactional and account emails (order confirmation, shipping, account creation) currently run on Shopify defaults, and we'll want your input on whether any of those are worth moving into Klaviyo for brand consistency down the road — but that's a judgment call, not an immediate task. Here's the flow library we need, at a high level. On the DTC side: welcome and onboarding flows for new subscribers and first-time customers; a membership conversion flow that promotes the Futstrikers membership ($19.99 per year, which gets members 25% off all balls plus free shipping) to email subscribers who haven't purchased and to one-time buyers who aren't yet members; a member reorder and replenishment flow built around themes like "lost ball," "more training equals more balls," new season, and tournament prep, to keep active members buying; a non-member reorder flow that uses the same reorder logic for single purchasers, with membership itself positioned as the upsell; membership renewal reminders and a lapsed-member win-back; browse abandonment and abandoned checkout; post-purchase and review requests; and announcement and product-launch campaigns. On the wholesale side: reorder reminder flows tuned to B2B cadence (clubs, coaches, and resellers typically reorder seasonally, so flows should reflect that rhythm, not DTC timing); tier-based incentive campaigns, because our wholesale customers sit on different pricing tiers and campaigns need to be segmented so each tier sees the right offer and message; wholesale onboarding and account nurture; B2B announcements for new products, restocks, and seasonal buy windows; and a win-back flow for wholesale accounts that haven't reordered. Beyond flows, you'll own segmentation strategy — cleanly separating DTC and wholesale on a shared storefront, and inside each side segmenting further by membership status (member, non-member, lapsed), wholesale pricing tier, lifecycle stage, product interest, and order cadence. You'll handle copywriting for all flows and campaigns. Our brand voice is direct and youth-soccer-first, not corporate. You'll run the campaign calendar, planning and executing weekly or biweekly sends around drops, restocks, tournaments, and seasonality. You'll deliver weekly and monthly performance recaps covering attributed revenue, flow performance, and next-step recommendations. And you'll own deliverability and list hygiene to keep us in the inbox. What we provide on our end. We have an in-house graphic designer — you write the brief, they produce the visuals. We have in-house full-stack developers, so any technical work (API, Shopify theme, tracking, custom blocks, webhook and event wiring) is handled by our team. Klaviyo is already set up and integrated with Shopify, with our list loaded and ready. Shopify is live with DTC and wholesale segmentation already in place. Brand assets, product imagery, and CDN-hosted visuals are ready for you to use. You focus on strategy, flow architecture, segmentation, copy, and campaigns; we handle design and dev. Who we're looking for. You should have 3+ years of hands-on Klaviyo experience (please share a portfolio or example flows). You should have demonstrable experience with Shopify and Klaviyo working together — segmentation, flow logic, event-driven triggers, and revenue reporting. Experience segmenting DTC and B2B or wholesale audiences on a single storefront is a major plus. You should be a strong ecommerce copywriter — please send 2 to 3 examples of flow emails you've personally written. You should be comfortable working independently against revenue targets. Bonus points for sports, apparel, consumer-goods, or subscription and membership brand experience. How to apply. In your proposal, please include four things. First, 2 to 3 Klaviyo accounts or brands you've worked on and the outcomes — attributed revenue lift, list growth, flow performance. Second, links or screenshots of flow emails you personally wrote and built. Third, your take on the biggest levers for (a) driving membership signups from our existing DTC list and (b) building tier-specific wholesale reorder flows — what would you build first and why? Fourth, your availability in hours per week and confirmation that you can be reachable on Slack during our work hours. Engagement details. This is an ongoing hourly contract starting at $5 to $7 per hour, capped at 20 to 25 hours per week, with an expectation of roughly 3 to 4 hours of focused work per day, Monday through Friday. You must be available on Slack during our work hours, which are 9am to 2pm and 10pm to midnight Arizona time (MST, UTC-7, no daylight saving). You don't need to be working the entire window — but you need to be reachable on Slack during those hours for quick questions, approvals, and handoffs with our in-house designer and dev team. Why the starting rate. We're being upfront: the starting rate reflects where we are as a growing startup, not where this role is going. The plan is to prove fit together over the first couple of months and then step the rate up as email-attributed revenue grows. We reward performance — the person in this seat grows with the company. If you ship great work, you will not be stuck at the starting rate. Please start your proposal with the word "Flowstate" so we know you read this. We're looking for an ongoing engagement, 6+ months minimum, with someone who wants to plant a flag and grow with a brand. You're walking into a live Klaviyo account, a live Shopify store, a supportive in-house team, and a brand with real momentum — your job is to turn that into email-driven revenue. If you're the kind of marketer who would rather own a channel inside a scaling company than bounce between one-off projects, this is your gig. Apply.
title: Klaviyo Email Marketer for Shopify Brand (Flows + Copy + Segmentation)

description: Shopify CRO & AOV Optimization Expert — Ongoing Opportunity About Us We're Montkush, a USDA organic CBD and plant-based wellness brand based in Vermont. We sell CBN/CBD gummies, tinctures, and topicals through our Shopify store. We have a strong returning customer base, solid margins, and a great brand story — and we're ready to tighten up the website to convert more of the traffic we're already getting. What We're Looking For We need an experienced Shopify CRO specialist to audit our store, identify conversion and AOV opportunities, and implement improvements directly. This is a hands-on role — we want someone who can do the work, not just advise on it. Important context: Our store does not yet have the traffic volume required for traditional A/B testing to reach statistical significance. We need someone comfortable making informed, hypothesis-driven direct implementations based on best practices, customer data, and experience with similar Shopify stores. What You'll Be Doing Audit our current Shopify store (product pages, homepage, cart, checkout) and identify high-impact opportunities Document your findings with clear hypotheses — what you're changing, why, and what result you expect Implement changes directly in Shopify (or work with our team to do so) Focus areas include: conversion rate, average order value, subscription attach rate, free gift thresholds, product page copy and imagery, checkout flow, and pop-up/offer consistency Track and report on results as traffic allows You're a Great Fit If You: Have hands-on Shopify CRO experience (please share examples or case studies) Have worked with health, wellness, or supplement brands — CBD or restricted category experience is a plus Understand AOV optimization tools like Boost.shop, Rebuy, or similar Are comfortable working with a small, fast-moving team Communicate clearly and document your reasoning Engagement Details Hourly rate — we are capping the initial engagement at $2,000 This is an ongoing opportunity for the right person — if results are strong we want to keep working together International applicants welcome To Apply Please include: 2-3 examples of Shopify stores you've improved with specific results where possible Your hourly rate A brief note on how you approach CRO for stores with lower traffic volumes
title: Shopify CRO & AOV Optimization Expert Needed

description: About Us: We run a growing men's fashion e-commerce brand. We work with suppliers and a fulfillment team based in China, and we need someone reliable and detail-oriented to keep our shipping operations running smoothly. —————————— The Role: You will be our eyes on every order that leaves the warehouse. Your job is to monitor all active shipments daily, catch problems early, and follow up directly with our supplier team to resolve issues before they reach the customer. This is a self-directed role. You won't be micromanaged — but you will be expected to stay on top of things proactively and report to me daily. —————————— What You'll Do: • Monitor our shipment tracking dashboard daily and flag any delayed, stuck, or lost shipments • Communicate directly with our supplier/fulfillment team via Discord to follow up on shipping issues • Escalate anything that needs my attention in a clear, organized daily report • Track resolution of issues from start to finish — nothing should fall through the cracks • Keep records and documentation up to date —————————— Requirements: • Must be available during China business hours (8am–6pm CST / UTC+8). This is non-negotiable — our fulfillment team is only online during these hours and you need to be able to communicate with them in real time. • Strong written English — you'll be communicating with both me and our China-based team in English • Extremely organized and detail-oriented — you'll be tracking hundreds of shipments • Self-starter who doesn't need to be told what to do every day. If something looks wrong, you follow up without being asked. • Comfortable using Discord, Google Sheets, and basic dashboard/tracking tools • Reliable and consistent — this role requires someone who shows up every single day. Shipments don't take days off. —————————— Nice to Have: • Experience in e-commerce, dropshipping, or supply chain/logistics • Experience working with Chinese suppliers or fulfillment teams • Familiarity with Shopify • Mandarin (not required, but a plus) —————————— Details: • Part-time: 15-20 hours per week • Budget: $3–5/hr depending on experience • Long-term position — I'm looking for someone who wants to grow with the business, not someone looking for a short gig • Start date: Immediate —————————— How to Apply: Please include in your proposal: 1. Your timezone and confirm you can work during China business hours 2. Any relevant experience with shipment tracking, supplier communication, or e-commerce operations 3. A brief description of how you stay organized when managing many tasks at once
title: Shipment Monitoring & Supplier Ops VA — E-Commerce (Part-Time, China Hours)

description: Overview We’re looking for a Shopify designer to migrate our existing site from Turbo to the Impact theme and create a clean, elevated baseline. This is a focused execution project. The goal is to translate our current site into Impact with improved visual clarity, spacing, and hierarchy—without a full UX redesign. Success looks like a clean, cohesive site that feels more intuitive and visually refined than our current version, while staying structurally familiar. We’re not looking for a full UX overhaul, but we do value designers who can make thoughtful layout decisions that improve clarity, hierarchy, and overall flow during the migration. Bonus if you can suggest small, practical UX improvements as part of the build (no need for deep strategy—just good design instincts). We value restraint and thoughtful layout decisions—this is about elevating clarity and flow, not redesigning from scratch. About Us Solid Threads is a graphic t-shirt brand blending vintage-inspired design, humor, and feel-good storytelling. Our designs range from playful and irreverent to thoughtful and values-driven, spanning themes like pop culture, nature, music, and everyday life. We have a large and diverse catalog, and a big part of the challenge is presenting that variety in a way that feels cohesive, easy to explore, and true to the brand’s laid-back, optimistic personality. The goal is a shopping experience that feels both curated and effortless, while still capturing the depth and character of the collection. Scope Rebuild existing site structure in the Impact theme Implement homepage, collections, and key templates using native sections Improve visual hierarchy (spacing, layout, flow) Deliver a clean, cohesive, launch-ready site What This Is NOT Not a full UX strategy or CRO project Not a deep redesign Not backend tagging/filter work Not heavy Liquid development Ideal Fit Strong experience with Shopify themes (Impact preferred) Clean, modern design sensibility Strong instincts for layout, spacing, and content hierarchy Comfortable executing within an existing structure Fast, detail-oriented, and responsive Timeline Start ASAP Target: 3–4 weeks To Apply Please include: Can you share 1–2 Shopify sites you’ve worked (Imoact theme preferred) on where you handled both layout and implementation? What specifically did you improve? What is your experience with theme migrations? description: The short version We're a screen print and embroidery shop in Ann Arbor, MI (No Mids, est. 2023) and we need a custom product designer tool built into our Shopify store. Customers pick a blank, upload or choose artwork, see a live mockup on the selected garment/color, and watch pricing update as they adjust quantity, placement, and decoration method. At checkout, Shopify authorizes the card but doesn't capture — we review each order manually before charging. We already have design direction, architecture decisions, a working React prototype, and a clear data flow mapped out. We're not looking for someone to figure out the problem from scratch — we're looking for someone to execute a well-defined build. Open to hourly for the right person. Timeline: 4–6 weeks, ideally starting within the next 2 weeks. Who we are No Mids is a small but growing custom apparel studio. We print and embroider for brands, bands, breweries, and businesses that care about how their merch looks. Our aesthetic direction is already locked in — think Off-White meets 90s skate zine. Platform: Shopify (we're not open to moving off it) Product import: Supply Master, pulling from SanMar and S&S Activewear APIs Catalog size: thousands of SKUs across t-shirts, hoodies, crewnecks, polos, caps, and accessories Theme: [CONFIRM YOUR THEME HERE — Dawn / Impulse / Broadcast / Custom] Payment capture: will be set to manual (authorization hold, manual capture after review) What needs to get built 1. A custom Shopify theme section — the "Studio" design tool Lives on product pages. When a customer lands on any decorated product, they see: The current variant image (color-specific, already imported by Supply Master) A canvas overlay where their artwork renders at the correct placement zone Controls for: decoration method (screen print / DTG / embroidery), number of print colors or stitch count, placement (front center / left chest / back full / sleeve), quantity Live-updating price display that recalculates on every input change, showing per-piece and total Artwork upload (accepts PNG, JPG, SVG, PDF, AI — stored in Shopify Files or an S3 bucket you set up) An "add to cart" button that packages all customization data as Shopify line item properties 2. A template-per-garment-type architecture — NOT per-variant This is the single most important technical requirement. We previously tried a tool that required us to create a mockup template for every individual product and variant, which was unworkable with thousands of imported SKUs. The right architecture is: ~12 garment templates total (unisex tee, fitted tee, pullover hoodie, zip hoodie, crewneck, polo, trucker cap, dad hat, beanie, tote, etc.) Each template is a JSON config defining print zones as normalized coordinates (0.0–1.0) relative to the product photo Products get assigned a template via a Shopify tag (e.g., garment:tee-unisex) applied automatically during Supply Master import based on category The configurator reads the tag, loads the matching template, and pulls the variant-specific photo from Shopify's product data — no per-SKU setup needed Brand-level offsets allowed for suppliers whose photography is consistently shifted (e.g., SanMar vs S&S having slightly different chest heights) If you've done this coordinate-normalization approach before, please mention it. If you haven't, please at least acknowledge in your proposal that you understand why this matters. 3. A live pricing engine Industry-standard screen print, DTG, and embroidery pricing logic: Screen print: per-color, per-location charge with quantity tier breaks (12–23, 24–47, 48–71, 72–143, 144–287, 288+) plus per-color setup fees DTG: per-piece flat rate with quantity tier breaks, no setup Embroidery: per-1,000-stitches rate with quantity tier breaks plus digitizing setup fee Blank cost: pulled from the Shopify variant price (which Supply Master keeps synced), with a configurable markup percentage applied All rates and tiers need to be editable from an admin-accessible config file or settings panel — not hardcoded — because we'll tweak these regularly The calculator also shows a price-break ladder so customers see how the per-piece price drops at higher quantities — this is a huge conversion tool for our category. 4. Cart handoff via Shopify line item properties When added to cart, the line item must include (as properties): Artwork file URL (or array of URLs if multiple placements) Mockup preview image URL (for admin review) Placement zone Decoration method Color count (for screen print) or stitch count (for embroidery) Per-piece print charge and setup fee (broken out for admin clarity) Any customer notes Properties with an underscore prefix (_artwork_url) to keep them hidden from the customer cart display but visible in admin. 5. Manual capture + admin order view polish We'll configure Shopify Payments for manual capture ourselves. What we need from you: Ensure the order admin view cleanly displays all line item properties (artwork preview thumbnail, specs, pricing breakdown) so our team can review quickly Ideally: a small admin-side helper that generates a print-ready file package (artwork + spec sheet) per order. This is nice-to-have, not required for v1. What we're providing A working React prototype demonstrating the full customer flow, aesthetic direction — you can reference it throughout the build A written build spec with architecture decisions, data flow diagrams, and template config format All visual design assets — fonts, colors, the NO MIDS wordmark, CSS variables Template coordinate measurements — we'll measure these ourselves against reference product photos Clear copy and microcopy — already written Fast feedback loop — we'll respond to questions within a few hours during business days Who we're looking for Required: 3+ years building custom Shopify themes or Shopify apps Strong JavaScript (vanilla or a framework like Alpine / Lit / React — we have no strong preference) Comfortable with canvas or SVG rendering for the mockup layer Experience with Shopify's Storefront API and line item properties Can work from a written spec and the provided prototype without needing the design re-explained Strongly preferred: Prior work on apparel decoration, print-on-demand, or product customizer tools Familiarity with the screen print / embroidery industry's pricing models Experience with SanMar, S&S, or similar wholesale supplier catalog structures Portfolio links to a live Shopify product customizer you've built Not required but nice: Experience with Supply Master or similar Shopify catalog sync tools Design sensibility strong enough to catch aesthetic details the spec doesn't explicitly call out How to apply Please include the following in your proposal — applications missing these will be skipped: Links to 2 or 3 live product customizer tools you've built (Shopify preferred, other platforms acceptable if the work is comparable) A brief description of how you'd architect the garment template system — specifically how you'd avoid the per-variant setup problem. One or two paragraphs is plenty. Your proposed stack — what JS framework/libraries you'd use and why, where artwork files would be stored, how you'd structure the admin-editable pricing config A realistic timeline estimate broken into milestones Whether you prefer fixed price or hourly, with your rate or quote Your availability — how many hours per week you can commit and when you can start One question you have about the project — this tells us how carefully you read the brief Skip generic cover letters. We'd rather see a short, specific proposal than a polished template. We'll give clear feedback and won't scope-creep mid-build. If we want something not in the original spec, we'll treat it as a change order. Final payment happens after the tool is live on our store and handling real customer orders successfully for 7 days — not at code delivery. Milestones before that can be structured however works for you. Look forward to seeing your proposal.
title: Shopify Developer Needed — Custom Product Designer + Live Pricing Tool for Screen Print Shop
