description: Hubspot optimization Go To Market Overview: Current Market Environment: 1) We process traditional sales led orders where reps use the Shopify admin website to create an order that is sent to a customer to approve/ pay. This comes back into hubspot using the free unific pipeline (Currently we use the free version so it's limited detail outside of dollar amount and customer name (not company name) 2) Reps can also set up a wholesale account and help customers place their first order in Shopify. Orders enter the unific pipeline 3) Stores can self service create orders inside Shopify, and those are currently flowing to HubSpot as deals in the unific pipeline Issues: We’re seeing duplicate deals which are inflating our pipeline. We're getting shipped deals in the unific ecommerce pipeline. Those are managed separately Reps are manually building deals for forecasting. However, closed/fulfilled orders are entering the system as deals via the Unific pipeline, which requires manual entry or updating of the forecasted deals. What should we do with the forecasted deals when a closed-won order appears in the Unific pipeline? We aren’t confident in the truth of the current data synchronization, particularly regarding sales revenue versus the reported value in deals/ pipeline (as evidenced by evaluating HubSpot dashboards against Shopify sales data). This is causing inaccuracy of forecasting in hubspot Our process is complex and harder to follow than it ideally should be - we need to simplify the current process/ workflow from managing multiple pipelines We are seeing “looping” of data with automation so all automation has been turned off Project Overview: We use HubSpot CRM to track deals, manage our sales pipeline, forecast future income, and track seller activity through notes, activities etc, We also use HubSpot to show current revenue (through deals closed won) and track historical revenue (closed won). Our process manages new business and recurring revenue through specific deal naming conventions (I assume there's a better way like tagging etc). We are using the free version of Unific (and evaluating their Quotific CPQ tool) to bridge our B2B and e-commerce workflows. Our sales team creates deals in HubSpot (the quotific tool would pivot that to us creating quotes -that push draft orders into Shopify). Today the unific connector pulls completed e-commerce orders from Shopify back into HubSpot as deals in a dedicated e-commerce pipeline, which is separate from our Hemp pipeline. We need a qualified Revenue Operations (RevOps) consultant to walk through our current state, identify structural bottlenecks, map out a clean architecture, and provide a best-practice operational overview to ensure accurate, non-duplicate reporting. Key Deliverables: System Audit & Discovery Walkthrough: Review the existing Unific mapping rules, HubSpot deal pipelines, and Quotific configurations. Pipeline Restructuring Plan: Outline clear delineations between quote-based forecasting and direct transactional e-commerce revenue. Deduplication & Loop Prevention Strategy: Formulate concrete logic to ensure a HubSpot quote that becomes a Shopify order does not sync back to HubSpot as a brand-new duplicate deal. Reporting & Forecasting Framework: Establish a best-practice strategy for custom reporting on closed-won revenue vs. rolling sales forecasts. Eliminate any multisystem loopback We are currently NOT connected to QuickBooks from HubSpot. We are only relying on Shopify to push information into QuickBooks, such as orders and customer information (today, this is mostly limited to contacts, not companies, even with the highest level of Shopify enabled). We also need to understand the optimal structure here. Direct Integration & Technical Questions "How do you handle 'loopback duplication' in HubSpot when a quote-turned-deal triggers a Shopify order, which then attempts to sync back into HubSpot as a second deal via Unific?" "What is your experience specifically with Quotific CPQ by Unific, and how do you ensure the draft order stages in Shopify correctly manipulate the forecast categories in HubSpot?" "Are you comfortable custom-mapping Shopify metafields or line-item properties through Unific to ensure granular data is clean on the HubSpot side?" Forecasting & Revenue Questions "How do you separate purely transactional, automated e-commerce sales from active B2B sales pipelines so that high-volume web orders don't break our human-led sales forecasts?" "To track revenue cleanly without cross-polluting past performance, do you recommend calculating revenue directly from Deal Amount properties or using HubSpot's line-item product library synced from Shopify?" "What are the specific HubSpot entry and exit criteria you would establish to automate a clean transition from 'Deal' to 'Order Fulfilled' across the systems?"
title: Hubspot Optimization for Revenue Management Integrated with Shopify

description: || If you reply with a generic AI response I won't even read it. Reply like a human please || + We're an Australian DTC brand. We currently fulfil all orders through our AU 3PL and are adding a China 3PL to service international orders for one product category. Our Issue: The China 3PL's app is pulling every order into its system by default. We need Shopify Flow to route orders correctly so only the right orders reach the China 3PL. Routing logic: Orders containing ONLY 'sock' products AND shipping outside AU/NZ → China 3PL Everything else (AU/NZ orders, non-sock products, mixed orders) → AU 3PL as normal Critical constraints: -Current AU order flow cannot be disrupted at any point during setup -China 3PL only goes live after full testing and my approval -We use Aftersell for post-purchase upsells (5 to 10 min window), so routing must account for that race condition -Need to verify the China 3PL's app respects Shopify's fulfillment holds Deliverables: -Written implementation plan before any changes are made to the live store -China 3PL location setup in Shopify (location, inventory, shipping zones) -Shopify Flows to handle the routing logic including the Aftersell window -Tested across key scenarios (socks to US, socks to AU, mixed orders, Aftersell upsells, etc.) -Coordination with our China 3PL team via WhatsApp (English) to confirm their side works -Handover doc covering setup and how to add new products to the China 3PL in future Required experience: -Advanced Shopify Flow and Fulfillment Order API -Prior multi-3PL routing setup on a live Shopify store -Clear written English Nice to have: Experience with Chinese 3PL fulfillment apps or Aftersell, DTC apparel background. Communication: I approve every change before it goes live. Written updates every 1 to 2 days. Timeline: 2 days

description: Job Description We’re looking for a high-level Shopify expert who can design and implement a clean, scalable workflow for post-purchase order edits, store credit usage, and exchanges… while working around 3PL limitations. this is NOT a basic Shopify task we need someone who understands systems, data integrity, and operational workflows Context we recently integrated a 3PL that does not recognize edits made to existing Shopify orders this creates a constraint: • any order change must be recreated as a new order for fulfillment • Shopify’s native exchange and edit flows cannot be used directly • store credit cannot be cleanly applied in our current workflow Current Problems • we are forced to cancel orders and recreate them manually • store credit is being simulated using discounts, which breaks reporting • exchanges are split across multiple orders, causing data fragmentation • invoices do not correctly reflect remaining balances after store credit • workflow is manual, error-prone, and not scalable What We Need we need a Shopify expert to design and help implement a system that: • works with our 3PL requirement new order for fulfillment • properly applies and tracks store credit not via fake discounts • maintains clean and accurate financial reporting • handles exchanges without breaking data integrity • allows invoices to reflect the true remaining balance after credits • reduces manual work and improves internal efficiency Ideal Candidate you should: • have deep experience with Shopify Plus or advanced Shopify workflows • understand 3PL integrations and fulfillment constraints • have worked with store credit systems, returns, and exchanges • be comfortable designing workflows not just executing tasks • understand reporting description: About Us: We run a highly successful portfolio of e-commerce businesses specializing in subscription-based supplement products. As we scale, we are looking to radically lean out our manual customer support team and shift our focus heavily toward AI and rule-based automation. The Project: We need a powerful, automated Email CRM / Helpdesk tailored for e-commerce. Think Gorgias, Help Scout, Zendesk, Richpanel, or Commslayer—but customized for our specific ecosystem. Because of our strict 1-to-2-week timeline, we are NOT looking for someone to build this from scratch. We are looking for a developer, agency, or SaaS founder who has already built a similar platform or has a robust, white-label codebase ready to go. We want you to take your existing engine, rebrand it, deploy it on our servers, and add the specific custom automation features we need. Core Requirements: ~ Heavy Automation Focus: The primary goal is ticket deflection and automated resolutions. We need robust rule engines, auto-replies, macros, and intelligent routing to minimize human touchpoints. ~ E-commerce & Subscription Integration (CHECKOUT CHAMP): Must be able to pull customer data seamlessly (e.g., Shopify, WooCommerce, Recharge, Stripe) so the system knows if a customer is an active subscriber, what their LTV is, and their recent order status. ~ Unified Inbox: Clean, fast, and intuitive UI for the few human agents we retain to handle escalated email tickets. ~ Speed & Reliability: It needs to be good, fast, and bug-free. The Ideal Candidate: ~ You have a pre-existing codebase for a Helpdesk/CRM that you own and can legally rebrand and modify for us. ~ You understand e-commerce customer support flows (especially for subscription products). ~ You work fast and communicate clearly. ~ You can commit to delivering a fully functional, customized MVP within 7 to 14 days. Why Take This Job? We are highly motivated buyers. If you have the tech sitting on your hard drive or running as a micro-SaaS right now, this is incredibly easy money for you. We are willing to pay very well for the right solution delivered on time. How to Apply: ~ Start your proposal with the words "I HAVE THE CODE". ~ Send a brief demo video, screenshots, or a link to the existing CRM/Helpdesk platform you have already built. ~ Briefly explain how you will integrate e-commerce/subscription data and your approach to the automation engine. ~ Provide your estimated price and confirmation that you can hit the 1-2 week deadline.
title: Custom E-commerce Helpdesk & Automation CRM (Gorgias / Zendesk Alternative)

description: Cherry Collectables has come to us with concerns about their GA4 data. Starting in October 2024, they've seen a significant and sustained spike in organic search sessions. At the same time, there was a matching surge in organic sessions adding items to cart — but cart-to-order conversion rates dropped sharply at exactly the same point. The year prior showed stable, even performance across these metrics, so October 2024 stands out as a clear crossover point. The trend isn't a one-off — it has continued consistently since then, and the client describes the pattern as "really distinct." The client's theory is that bot traffic is inflating their organic sessions and add-to-cart numbers without converting to real orders, which would explain both the traffic spike and the conversion drop happening together. They want us to investigate the data and confirm (or rule out) whether bots are the cause. What we need to investigate Validate the October 2024 inflection point in GA4 Look for bot signals (geography, device/browser mix, engagement time, landing page concentration, referrer anomalies) Cross-reference GA4 organic traffic against Search Console impressions and clicks
title: GA4 Bot Traffic & Add to Cart to Checkout Investigation

description: Project Overview: ToddArt.com is the new digital home for the work of legendary artist Todd Goldman, the creative force behind the global phenomenon David & Goliath. With a multi-million dollar retail history and a track record of creating iconic characters, we are now scaling our Shopify presence. We aren’t just selling art; we are building a high-volume lifestyle platform—similar to TeePublic or LookHuman—featuring apparel, stickers, mugs, and home decor. The Opportunity: We have the world-class creative assets and a $5,000/month dedicated ad budget to start. We need a strategic "Growth Engine" to manage these funds in exchange for a Performance-Based Tiered Revenue Share. This is a chance to partner with a proven brand name on a high-upside, long-term basis. Compensation Structure (Gross Retail Sales): 5% commission on the first $10,000 of monthly sales. 7.5% commission on sales between $10,001 – $30,000. 10% commission on all monthly sales exceeding $30,000. Scope of Work: * Full Media Buying: Strategy, setup, and daily management of Meta (FB/IG) and Google Ads. * Conversion Optimization: Analyzing and improving the Shopify store flow from "Click to Sale." * Retention Marketing: Implementing automated email/SMS flows (Abandoned Cart, Welcome series) to maximize ad spend. * Creative Strategy: Identifying "winners" in our vast catalog and advising on ad hooks/copy. * Performance Reporting: Weekly updates on Spend, Revenue, and ROAS. Who You Are: You are an expert-level marketer who understands how to sell lifestyle products and pop-culture niches. You are data-driven, comfortable with a performance-based model, and looking for a long-term Contract-to-Hire partnership. You should have a proven track record of scaling Shopify stores from $0 to high-volume revenue.. How to Apply: Please start your proposal with the word "TODDART" so I know you have read the requirements. In your proposal, please answer: Which product category (stickers to hoodies) is your "entry point" to hit 2.5x ROAS fastest? Briefly describe a Shopify store you scaled; what were the key levers? Confirm you are comfortable with the 5–10% tiered commission + 2.5x ROAS floor.
title: Shopify Growth Partner: Manage $5k/mo Ad Budget for 5-10% Revenue Share

Please include case studies or results (ROAS, conversions, revenue) in your proposal.

description: I’m building a fashion brand and looking for a Shopify designer/developer to create a clean, elevated e-commerce website for launch. I can share visual references and direction to guide the design. Timing: I’m planning for the project to begin toward the end of May. Scope (initial phase): * Homepage design and build * Collection page * Product page * About page * Mobile optimization * Basic Shopify setup I’m looking for someone who can: * Design and build within Shopify (not just Figma) * Create a custom-feeling site (not overly template-based) * Incorporate subtle, refined interactions (hover states, movement, etc.) without making the site feel busy or slow * Maintain a strong sense of layout, spacing, and typography This will be a focused initial build, with potential for ongoing work as the brand grows. If applying, please include: 1. 2–3 Shopify websites you’ve designed and developed (fashion, beauty, or similar preferred) 2. A brief note on how you approach making Shopify sites feel custom and elevated 3. Your availability starting late May Start your proposal with the word “Editorial” so I know you’ve read this. Looking forward to connecting.

description: We are looking for a skilled Next.js Developer to build the frontend for Store Link, a powerful multi-store synchronization platform. Key Responsibilities - Develop a clean, responsive dashboard using Next.js and Tailwind CSS. - Integrate the frontend with our existing REST APIs. - Implement complex data tables, search/filtering for products, and store-connection wizards. State Management: Proficiency in managing complex application states (e.g., TanStack Query, Zustand, or similar). API Integration: Expert at consuming custom APIs and handling authentication. Clean UI: Eye for detail using Tailwind CSS or specialized component libraries (like Shadcn/ui). PLUS: if the developer have some experience with shopify, other than that I will be helping with everything related to shopify

description: I’m looking for a highly skilled Shopify designer/developer to redesign my website with a strong focus on conversion rate optimization, user experience, and clean, elevated design. This is not just a visual redesign. I need someone who understands how to structure a site to drive sales, reduce decision fatigue, and guide customers clearly from homepage to checkout. About the Brand: We are an elevated essentials clothing brand based in New York, focused on premium everyday pieces designed to be recognized by design. We create clean, high-quality staples that feel as good as they look, built for everyday wear and styled your way. Our pieces are minimal, refined, and identifiable through a signature design detail. Scope of Work: 1. Homepage Redesign (Conversion-Focused) • Strong hero section with clear messaging and CTA • “Start Here” or Shop by Category section to guide users • Bestsellers section to highlight top-performing products • “Why [Brand]” section with clear, conversion-focused positioning • Product spotlight or featured product section • Trust bar integration: • Free Shipping on Orders $100+ (U.S.) • Free Exchanges • Easy Returns • Clean, minimal, elevated design aligned with brand identity • Mobile-first optimization 2. Social Proof Integration (High Priority) • “What Customers Are Saying” section on homepage • Instagram or social proof integration (UGC, tagged photos, or feed) • Star ratings displayed clearly across product listings and product pages 3. Product Page Optimization (High Priority) Product pages should be structured to increase clarity, confidence, and conversion. Top of Page: • Star rating summary with total number of reviews (clickable) • Clear pricing and short, benefit-driven description • Size selector with size guide pop-up • Model sizing information Mid Page Enhancements: • “Why You’ll Love It” section with benefit-driven bullets • Trust badges under Add to Cart: • Free Shipping $100+ • Free Exchanges • Easy Returns Product Recommendation Sections: • Complete the Look • Similar Styles • You May Also Like Tabbed Content Layout: • Details • Fit & Fabric • Shipping & Returns Bottom of Page: • Full reviews section with detailed customer reviews and optional images 4. Conversion Features • Product badges on images: • Bestseller • New Arrival • New Color • Trending • Low stock or urgency indicators • Optimized email pop-up (low friction, conversion-focused) 5. Customer Accounts • Customer profile accounts • Order history and tracking • Easy reordering functionality (No loyalty or rewards system needed at this stage) 6. UX and Site Structure Improvements • Simplified navigation • Clear customer journey • Reduce friction from homepage to product to checkout • Improve overall usability and flow What I’m Looking For: • Strong experience with Shopify (Online Store 2.0) • Proven experience building high-converting e-commerce websites • Understanding of conversion rate optimization (CRO) • Ability to think strategically, not just design • Clean, modern, elevated design aesthetic Please Include: • Examples of Shopify stores you’ve built (start to finish) • Any measurable results (conversion rate improvements, UX improvements, etc.) • Confirmation of your role in the projects you share Goal: To create a website that clearly communicates the brand, builds trust immediately, and drives consistent sales while keeping the experience simple and intentional for the customer. Looking for someone who understands both design and conversion psychology, not just aesthetics.
title: Shopify Website Redesign for Elevated Essentials Brand (Conversion-Focused + UX Optimization)

description: We are a premium wellness brand launching in the UAE. We're a lean founding team moving fast toward launch and need a technically sharp freelancer to help us get our backend commerce infrastructure right from day one. Our domain is our primary sales channels are a Shopify DTC store and Amazon UAE. What we need: We're looking for a Shopify-specialist freelancer, ideally with UAE/GCC experience, to handle four core integration workstreams: 1. 3PL ↔ Shopify integration We're working with IQ Fulfilment as our third-party logistics partner. We need automated order forwarding from Shopify to IQ and tracking sync back to customers. If a native Shopify app exists, great - if not, we need a clean webhook or middleware solution (Zapier/Make acceptable if robust). 2. Multi-channel inventory sync (Shopify + Amazon UAE) We need reliable, real-time inventory sync across both sales channels to prevent overselling. Open to tools like Linnworks or Veeqo - recommend what you'd use and why. 3. Landing page ↔ Shopify commerce wiring Our brand landing page is built outside of Shopify. We need buy buttons, cart embed, and checkout flow connected cleanly so the purchase experience is seamless. 4. Social commerce integrations (Meta + TikTok) We run active paid and organic campaigns on Instagram, Facebook, and TikTok. We need: - Meta Shopping: product catalogue connected to Meta, shoppable posts enabled, and Meta Pixel + Conversions API configured correctly for accurate attribution - TikTok Shop: seller account connected to Shopify, product catalogue synced, and inventory managed alongside our other channels UAE/GCC experience with TikTok Shop specifically is a strong plus, as onboarding in this market has nuances. Bonus (nice to have): Server-side conversion tracking via Meta Conversions API if not already covered above. Tech stack - Shopify Basic - Payment gateway: Telr or Checkout.com - Email/CRM: Klaviyo - Analytics: GA4 + Meta Pixel - Accounting: Zoho Books - Customer support: Tidio / WhatsApp Business Timeline We're targeting a fast turnaround — ideally 3–5 days of focused work. This is a project engagement, not an ongoing retainer (though we're open to a light support arrangement post-launch). Budget Please quote a fixed project fee. We're a startup and value transparency — if something is out of scope, tell us upfront. To apply, please share: - 2–3 examples of similar Shopify integrations you've built (3PL connections, multi-channel sync, or social commerce preferred) - Your recommended approach for the 3PL integration specifically - Your experience with TikTok Shop in the UAE/GCC, if any - Your fixed project fee and estimated timeline - UAE/GCC experience, if any We move quickly and will shortlist within 48 hours of posting.

description: About the Role We’re looking for a Zendesk optimisation expert who understands ecommerce operations and can leverage AI to transform customer support into a high-efficiency, revenue-aware function. What You’ll Do Audit and optimise existing Zendesk setups (or build from scratch) Design efficient ticket flows, triggers, automations, and macros Implement AI-driven support (chatbots, intent detection, auto-responses) Reduce support load through self-service, help centre strategy, and automation Integrate Zendesk with ecommerce platforms (Shopify, marketplaces, 3PL systems) Build reporting dashboards that tie support metrics to business outcomes (refunds, churn, conversion) Continuously test and improve workflows for speed, accuracy, and customer experience Key Responsibilities Zendesk configuration (Support, Guide, Chat, Explore) AI tool integration (Zendesk AI, GPT-based tools, third-party automation platforms) Workflow optimisation and ticket routing logic Help centre structure and content strategy SLA and performance optimisation Data analysis and reporting Collaboration with ecommerce, ops, and tech teams What We’re Looking For Proven experience optimising Zendesk for ecommerce businesses Strong understanding of support operations (not just tool usage) Experience implementing AI/automation in customer service Familiarity with Shopify and ecommerce workflows (orders, shipping, returns) Ability to think in systems, not just tickets Data-driven mindset with strong problem-solving skills Nice to Have Experience with 3PL integrations and logistics workflows Knowledge of CX metrics tied to revenue (LTV, churn, refunds) Experience with API integrations or low-code tools (Zapier, Make, etc.) Background in scaling support for high-growth ecommerce brands Success in This Role Looks Like Significant reduction in ticket volume per order Faster resolution times without sacrificing quality Increased automation rate across support interactions Clear visibility of support impact on revenue and operations Why This Role Matters Customer support is often treated as a cost centre. We see it as a lever for efficiency, retention, and growth. This role is about building a system that scales, not just answering tickets. How to Apply: Send through a short overview of: Zendesk setups you’ve optimised (before/after impact) Examples of AI or automation you’ve implemented Ecommerce brands or systems you’ve worked with

rules Final report updated manually in Google Sheets Inventory tracking Daily units sold per SKU are entered manually Stock is deducted manually Reorder decisions are made based on thresholds Amazon reporting Amazon sales data is copied manually from Shopkeeper into Google Sheets EBITDA / expense reporting Expenses are stored in Xero by category Figures are copied manually into Google Sheets for management reporting Goal: Build a reliable automated system that: Pulls data daily from Shopify, Triple Whale, Xero, and Amazon/Shopkeeper computes P&L automatically using our SKU cost logic updates Google Sheets automatically posts a daily summary into Slack tracks inventory depletion by SKU alerts us when reorder thresholds are reached reduces manual work as much as possible Nice to have: API / webhooks Xero API experience building reporting automations for ecommerce brands Preferred Triple Whale experience Amazon seller reporting experience Shopkeeper familiarity strong understanding of ecommerce P&L logic, COGS, contribution margin, inventory planning Deliverables: working automation system clear documentation editable cost/rules tables error handling and fallback logic handover / Loom explaining the setup To apply Please send: examples of similar ecommerce reporting / automation systems you built tools used how you would structure this system estimated timeline by milestones whether you would use Make/n8n, custom Python, or a hybrid approach The right way to scope the project I would split the hire into 3 milestones: Milestone 1: Daily P&L automation Shopify + Triple Whale + Amazon consolidation + Google Sheets + Slack Milestone 2: Inventory automation SKU stock deduction + reorder alerts Milestone 3: Xero Expense sync + EBITDA sheet

description: Overview We are looking for an expert Shopify developer or agency to migrate a complex WooCommerce-based site to Shopify. This is not a basic migration. We are specifically looking for someone who: Has built high-converting Shopify stores with experience in revenue focus Understands AOV optimization, bundling, upsells, and post-purchase flows Has experience with conversion-focused landing pages (Meta / Google traffic) Ideally you have directly improved store performance through bundling, upsells, and post-purchase optimization, not just theme design. Current Stack WooCommerce (physical products) MemberPress (gated streaming content) Vimeo (video hosting behind paywall) FluentCRM (email + automation) Elementor (landing pages) ~500 SEO blog posts Amazon MCF fulfillment Coupon-based digital access (for Amazon customers) Core Objective Migrate to Shopify while improving conversion rate and AOV, not just replicating the current site. Scope of Work 1. Shopify Migration (Ecommerce) Rebuild product catalog (mats, towels, blocks, etc.) Preserve SEO structure Integrate Amazon MCF fulfillment Optimize PDPs for: Higher conversion rate Higher AOV 2. AOV Optimization (Critical) We expect strong implementation of: Bundling strategies (kits, “complete your practice”) In-cart upsells Post-purchase upsells (1-click) PDP cross-sells Subscription or repeat purchase opportunities (if applicable) This is a core part of the project—not an add-on. 3. Membership / Gated Content Replace MemberPress with a Shopify-compatible system: Users must be able to: Log in Access streaming video content (Vimeo embeds) Retain access to previously purchased content 4. Customer Migration (Critical) Migrate all users Preserve access to purchased content Support existing Amazon customers using coupon-based unlock system Password resets acceptable if necessary 5. Coupon-Based Access System We currently include physical coupon codes in Amazon orders. System must: Accept existing codes Unlock digital content Be scalable for future use 6. Email & CRM Migration Migrate from FluentCRM → Klaviyo Rebuild: Purchase flows Product-specific flows Lifecycle automation 7. SEO Migration Migrate ~500 blog posts Maintain rankings via: URL preservation or redirects Improve blog UX/design 8. Landing Pages (Meta / Google Traffic) We run (and plan to scale) paid traffic. We currently have about 15 landing pages and need them to be redesigned under new theme to be: High-converting landing pages (not generic templates) Clear CRO structure: Strong hooks Visual hierarchy Product education Conversion-focused layout 9. Migrate wordpress based quiz to new website. Bonus if you have experience with: Quiz funnels (Octane AI or similar) Design Direction Clean, minimal, premium Conversion-first (not just aesthetic) Shopify theme-based is fine, but must be customized for CRO Deliverables Fully functional Shopify site Membership/content system Migrated blog + redirects Klaviyo setup Amazon MCF integration Coupon unlock system Optimized PDPs + upsell flows Conversion-focused landing pages Ongoing Work (Preferred) We are looking for a partner for: CRO improvements AOV optimization Iteration on landing pages Ongoing development 10. Budget (important, must answer) Please provide: Rough fixed cost range based on the scope above Breakdown if possible: Migration Membership system CRO / AOV optimization Landing pages We understand final pricing may require review, but we want a real estimate—not “TBD after audit.” TO APPLY (REQUIRED) 1. Shopify CRO Examples (MANDATORY) Share 3 Shopify stores you’ve worked on that include: Bundling Upsells (cart or post-purchase) High-converting PDPs Landing pages for paid traffic Links required. 2. Performance Context For at least 1 example, include: What you improved (CVR, AOV, etc.) What you implemented (bundles, upsells, flows) 3. Migration Experience Have you migrated: WooCommerce → Shopify? Sites with gated content or memberships? Explain briefly. 4. Tech Approach Answer briefly: What tools would you use for: Memberships? Post-purchase upsells? Bundling? Landing pages? Quiz funnels? 5. Key Problem Solving Short answers: How would you preserve access for existing users? How would you handle SEO migration for ~500 posts? How would you rebuild a coupon-based unlock system? 6. Timeline + Cost Estimated timeline Rough cost range
title: Shopify Migration Expert with CVR, AOV, Funnels Focus (From WooCommerce/Memberpress/Elementor Stack)

description: Hi, We're PWG Corp, a French e-commerce holding company. Our flagship brand (Nail brand) runs on 7 Shopify stores across FR / DE / IT / ES / NL / PL / EN. We have several new brands launching this year (10+). Our 7 Klaviyo accounts are currently running on autopilot. We're leaving money on the table every single week. We need someone to take full ownership. We're not looking for a freelancer who'll send 2 campaigns and disappear. We're looking for the person who becomes our Head of Retention across all PWG brands over the next 12 months. ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ WHAT YOU'LL DO (first 2-3 months — freelance) - Full audit of our 7 Klaviyo accounts (flows, segments, deliverability, current revenue share) - Rebuild core flows: Welcome, Abandoned Cart, Browse Abandonment, Post-Purchase, Winback - Plan and execute 2-4 campaigns/month per store (~14-28 campaigns/month total) - Systematic A/B testing (subject lines, timing, offers, segments) - Weekly reporting: revenue share, key metrics, next actions - Collaborate with our creative, product, and fulfillment teams WHAT YOU'LL DO (month 3+ — full-time if we're a match) - Take ownership of retention for all new PWG brands - Build reusable playbooks and SOPs - Hire and manage a junior or copywriter if needed - Extend to SMS / push / WhatsApp where it makes sense ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ WHO YOU ARE Must-have: - 1-3+ years in e-commerce email marketing - Advanced Klaviyo mastery (conditional splits, dynamic content, advanced segmentation) - Solid English copywriting (we translate the rest with AI so English-first is fine) - Track record: at least one account where you took email revenue share from under 15% to 25%+ - Full autonomy: we give you the goal, you deliver - Deliverability fundamentals (DKIM, SPF, DMARC, warm-up, sender reputation) Nice-to-have: - Multi-store / multi-country experience (managing 3+ Klaviyo accounts in parallel) - Email basic design skills - SMS marketing (Attentive, Klaviyo SMS, Postscript) ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ THE DEAL - Freelance trial: 1000-1500€/month for the 7 stores (2-3 months) - + retention performance bonus - 100% remote, flexible hours - Stack: ClickUp, Discord, Klaviyo, Shopify Plus, Postmark - Young, ambitious team. Zero bureaucracy. Things move fast. ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ HOW TO APPLY Just reach out on Upwork — a short message is enough. No need to write an essay. Tell me: - Why this role caught your eye - 1-2 lines on your Klaviyo experience (biggest account, what you did) - Your rate expectations I'll review your profile, check your work history, and get back to you within 48h if it's a match. From there, we jump on a quick call to see how we can work together. Looking forward to hearing from you, Baptiste — PWG Corp

description: Overview We are Velyx Research Ltd, a newly established UK limited company supplying research-grade peptides to the UK market. We are looking for an experienced Shopify freelancer to build a clean, professional, and fully compliant e-commerce store for our initial product launch of two compounds: BPC-157 and GHK-Cu. This is not a standard e-commerce build. Our industry has specific legal and compliance requirements that must be built into the website from the ground up. We need a freelancer who understands — or is willing to learn — the research chemical sector and can implement the necessary compliance features without cutting corners. What We Have Already Shopify account set up Brand colours selected Hero section designed Brand name: Velyx Logo: to be confirmed What We Need Built 1. Age Verification Gate A compliant age verification popup that appears before any visitor can access the site. This must: Confirm the visitor is 18 or over Confirm they understand products are sold for research purposes only Be impossible to bypass without confirmation Appear on every first visit and after cookies are cleared 2. Homepage Clean, professional, trust-focused design Brand colours and fonts applied consistently Hero section already started — needs integrating properly Clear "for research purposes only" framing throughout Trust signals: UK registered company, third-party tested, COA available No medical claims, no therapeutic language anywhere on the site 3. Product Pages — 2 Products to Start BPC-157 (5mg) GHK-Cu (50mg) Each product page must include: Clean product imagery Compliant product description (research use framing — we will provide copy) Certificate of Analysis (COA) display — either embedded PDF or downloadable link, batch specific Purity percentage display Clear pricing Add to cart functionality Prominent research use disclaimer on every product page 4. Certificate of Analysis (COA) Display System This is critical. Every product must display its current batch COA clearly. We need: A simple system to upload and update COAs per batch PDFs displayed or downloadable directly from the product page Batch number and test date visible 5. Essential Pages The following pages must be built and properly written or templated: About Us — who we are, our commitment to quality and compliance FAQ — covering what research peptides are, how to order, shipping, COAs Shipping Policy — UK shipping, cold chain handling, estimated delivery times Refund Policy — compliant with UK consumer law Terms of Service — including research use only clause Privacy Policy — GDPR compliant Contact Page — contact form and email address 6. Navigation Clean header navigation linking to Shop, About, FAQ, Contact Footer with full legal links, company registration number, disclaimer Full legal disclaimer in footer on every page 7. Checkout & Payments Checkout must include a tick box confirming the buyer acknowledges products are for research use only — this must be mandatory before purchase completes Payment provider integration — we will advise on provider once confirmed with a high-risk merchant processor Stripe may or may not be available for this sector — freelancer must have experience working with alternative payment gateways if needed 8. Legal & Compliance Copy Throughout the site the following language must be used consistently: "For research purposes only" "Not for human consumption" "Not intended to diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any disease" No therapeutic claims No before/after imagery No testimonials implying health benefits Design Direction Clean, minimal, and trustworthy — think clinical but approachable Not overly scientific or cold — we want it to feel credible but accessible Colour palette already established — will be shared with successful applicant Mobile responsive — must look excellent on phone and desktop Fast loading — page speed is important for SEO and user experience Technical Requirements Built on Shopify Mobile responsive SEO basics in place — page titles, meta descriptions, alt tags Google Analytics integration Fast page load speed GDPR cookie consent banner Secure checkout (SSL) Budget & Timeline Budget: Up to £500 Timeline: As soon as possible — ideally live within 2 weeks of project start We are ready to begin immediately upon finding the right freelancer. What We Are Looking For in a Freelancer Proven Shopify experience — please include examples of previous Shopify builds in your proposal Experience with compliance-sensitive industries (research chemicals, supplements, CBD, pharmaceuticals) is a strong advantage Understanding of UK e-commerce regulations Clear communicator — we want regular updates throughout the build Ability to work quickly without sacrificing quality Based in UK or Europe preferred but not essential. How to Apply Please include in your proposal: Links to 2–3 Shopify stores you have built previously A brief explanation of how you would approach the age verification and COA display requirements specifically Your realistic timeline to complete the full build Confirmation you are comfortable working within the research-use-only compliance framework Applications that do not address the compliance requirements specifically will not be considered. We are looking for someone who has read this brief carefully and understands what we are building.
title: Shopify Store Build for UK Research-Grade Peptide Supplier

description: Orthora is a fast-scaling DTC footwear brand built for workers who spend 8 to 12+ hours a day on their feet — nurses, teachers, warehouse workers, hospitality. We're doing $20K/day in revenue, scaling toward $100K/day, and our customers trust us with a purchase that literally carries them through their shift. That trust is earned at every touchpoint — and customer experience is where we win or lose it. THE ROLE We're hiring a Customer Experience Manager to own the full post-purchase experience end-to-end: support operations, dispute resolution, chargeback defense, team management, and AI-powered workflows that let us scale CX without scaling headcount linearly. You will not be handling tickets day-to-day (we have agents for that). You'll be building the system — training the team, designing the playbooks, owning the metrics, and winning back money we shouldn't be losing. WHAT YOU'LL OWN Support Operations. Manage a remote CX team (agents and VAs), own staffing, shift coverage, SLAs, and QA. Hit under 4hr first response, under 24hr resolution, 90 percent or higher CSAT. Dispute and Chargeback Defense. Own PayPal, Shopify Payments, Stripe, and credit card dispute responses. Build evidence templates, reduce chargeback rates, and recover every dollar that's recoverable. You know what "compelling evidence" actually means to each processor. Refund and Return Policy. Own the refund logic — when to approve, when to push back, when to offer store credit, when to save the sale. Reduce refund rate without tanking CSAT. Crisis Management. When we have a shipping delay, a sizing issue, or a supplier problem, you're the calm in the storm. Proactive outreach, coordinated messaging, damage control. AI Workflows. Deploy and tune Gorgias/Zendesk AI, macro libraries, sentiment routing, auto-tagging, and LLM-powered response drafting. Cut ticket handle time by 50 percent or more while maintaining quality. Reporting and Insights. Weekly CX dashboard to leadership: volume, resolution time, CSAT, refund rate, chargeback rate, top complaint categories. Turn ticket data into product, ops, and marketing feedback. Team Development. Hire, train, and develop a team of 5 to 15 CX agents across time zones. WHAT YOU'VE DONE BEFORE 10+ years in ecommerce customer experience, with at least 5 in a management or leadership role at a DTC brand. You've scaled a CX team through a period of rapid growth (from $1M to $10M+ ARR or equivalent). Deep fluency in dispute and chargeback workflows — PayPal, Shopify Payments, Stripe, Klarna, Afterpay. You've personally written and won chargeback responses and can quote win rates you've driven. Ran CX for a physical product brand — ideally footwear, apparel, or fashion where sizing, fit, and returns are core challenges. Expert in at least two helpdesks (Gorgias, Zendesk, Intercom, Kustomer, Freshdesk). Comfortable in Shopify admin (orders, refunds, fulfillment, tags). Strong written English — you'll be writing policies, responses, and customer-facing copy. AI FLUENCY — NON-NEGOTIABLE This is an AI-native role. You should be able to answer "how would you use AI to..." questions without hesitation: Deployed AI-powered ticket automation (Gorgias AI Agent, Zendesk AI, Intercom Fin, or custom LLM flows) at a previous company and can show measurable impact on deflection rate, handle time, or CSAT. Built macro and response libraries with LLMs to standardize quality across a team. Used AI for sentiment analysis, auto-tagging, and escalation routing. Comfortable prompting ChatGPT, Claude, or similar to build internal tools, analyze ticket trends, and draft responses. Bonus: experience with AI voice agents (Sierra, Ada, Decagon, Parloa) or building custom GPTs or Claude Projects for CX. If your answer to "how are you using AI in CX today?" is "a little bit of ChatGPT here and there," this role isn't for you. BONUS POINTS Experience with footwear, orthopedics, or healthcare-adjacent products. You've handled high-volume dispute fire — e.g. a viral product launch, a shipping disaster, a supplier issue — and know how to triage without burning out the team. Familiar with post-purchase platforms: Loop Returns, Returnly, AfterShip, Parcel Panel, Klaviyo for CX-triggered flows. Experience coordinating with 3PLs and international logistics (we ship globally). You've built a voice-of-customer program that fed insights back to product and marketing. WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE — FIRST 90 DAYS Day 30: Full audit of current CX ops — team, tools, policies, ticket data. Presented to leadership with prioritized gap list. Day 60: Chargeback response SOP live, dispute win rate measurably up, AI ticket automation deployed or tuned. Day 90: CX dashboard live, team structure optimized, CSAT trending up, refund rate trending down. LOGISTICS Remote, long-term contract with potential to transition to full-time leadership role. Competitive compensation based on experience. We invest in the stack you need to win.
title: Customer Experience Manager — DTC Footwear

description: Project Description I need a Magento 2 technical SEO specialist to diagnose why my site has severe indexing problems. This is a diagnostic-only job — I am not hiring for the fix in this engagement. I want a written report identifying the root causes, with specific recommendations, so I can make an informed decision about next steps. Current situation Magento 2.4.7-p4 on Nexcess hosting UK B2B ecommerce site (glass partition systems) Approximately 843 enabled products. Two product ranges (SP straight partitions and LP corner/L-shape partitions) have been consolidated using MageWorx Advanced Product Options into 28 and 16 products respectively (down from 392 and 223), with 301 redirects in place from the old URLs. A third range (TP T-shape, ~320 products) has not yet been consolidated. Several other product ranges (frameless, framed door, industrial/Crittall styles, hardware kits, and accessories) make up the remainder. Custom calculator/configurator as the primary purchase path 5,114 backlinks, on-page SEO score 78 (Ubersuggest) Only 34 organic visits over the last 30 days — site is effectively invisible in Google The specific problem Google Search Console shows: 29,486 pages indexed 2,366,207 pages NOT indexed, broken down as: 2,010,091 "Crawled — currently not indexed" 238,708 "Not found (404)" 71,696 "Duplicate without user-selected canonical" 35,263 "Blocked by robots.txt" 8,822 "Alternate page with proper canonical tag" 1,334 Server errors (5xx) 251 Page with redirect The not-indexed count has grown from ~413,000 in Sept 2025 to 2.36M today despite multiple rounds of remediation work (robots.txt updates, 301 redirects after product consolidation, canonical fixes, sitemap regeneration, removal requests). Fixes have not held. Something in the Magento configuration is generating URLs faster than they can be blocked. No manual actions. No security issues. What I need you to diagnose What is generating the 2M+ "Crawled — currently not indexed" URLs? Identify the specific Magento configuration, module, extension, or URL rewrite pattern responsible. I need the root cause, not a list of symptoms. Why are 238K URLs returning 404? These are likely from the product consolidation. Identify whether proper 301 redirects are in place and whether there are patterns that need addressing. Why are 71K pages showing as "Duplicate without user-selected canonical"? Diagnose the canonical tag configuration at the Magento level and identify why it's not resolving. What's causing the 1,334 server errors? Identify which URL patterns are returning 5xx responses and why. Are there Magento configuration issues (url_rewrite table bloat, layered navigation settings, configurable product duplication, category path settings, etc.) that are contributing to the problem? Review the current robots.txt, sitemap.xml, and canonical tag implementation for effectiveness. Deliverables A written report (PDF or Google Doc) containing: Root cause analysis for each of the six areas above Specific technical findings with examples (URLs, config settings, table entries, etc.) A prioritised list of recommended fixes with effort/impact estimates Your estimated cost and timeline to implement those fixes if I choose to hire you (or someone else) for the remediation work Loom video walkthrough of the key findings (preferred) What this job is NOT Not the fix. I may hire you for remediation afterward, or I may hire someone else, or I may decide to migrate platforms. This is a diagnostic to inform that decision. Not a generic SEO audit. I don't need a list of meta description recommendations or backlink suggestions. I need a Magento-specific technical diagnosis. Not a sales pitch for ongoing retainer work. Access I will provide Google Search Console (read-only) Google Analytics 4 (read-only) Magento admin access (restricted to required permissions) SFTP access if needed to inspect files Database read access if needed (via Nexcess) Timeline and budget Budget: £200 fixed price for the diagnostic report Timeline: completed within 10 days of hire I will consider proposals slightly above £200 if clearly justified by experience To be considered, please include in your proposal Confirmation you have worked on Magento 2 technical SEO specifically (not Shopify or WooCommerce). A brief example of a previous similar diagnostic you've done — one real example, with specifics (client details redacted is fine). Generic "I've done this many times" proposals will not be considered. The specific Magento areas/tables/configurations you would examine to diagnose URL explosion problems (this is my filter — a competent specialist will list things like url_rewrite table, catalog_url_rewrite, layered navigation configuration, configurable product URL handling, CMS page routing, etc.). How long you estimate the diagnostic will take. Whether you would be available to implement fixes afterwards, and a rough cost range for the remediation work based on what you've read above. I will not respond to generic proposals. Please address points 1–5 directly.
title: Magento 2 technical SEO diagnostic audit — indexing and URL generation issues

description: **Shopify Horizon Integrator — Mistricotine Redesign** Project type: Visual redesign on Shopify Horizon (no migration) Primary language: French (English secondary, already configured via Shopify Markets) Start: Within 3 to 4 weeks of selection --- ## About Mistricotine Mistricotine (mistricotine.com) is a French premium baby knitwear brand — merino wool, knitted in France, designed for the newborn wardrobe. Positioning centered on softness, craftsmanship and the birth-gift — with a strong focus on premium visual quality and editorial design. The site runs on Shopify Horizon with a curated, premium range — every product photographed in a Parisian studio, every collection editorial. The brand is founded and run by Sophie Delas. The team includes a photographer, an AI retouching designer, an illustrator, and now — you — the Shopify integrator. --- ## Context & working methodology We are redesigning the existing site — no migration. The instance, SKUs, inventory, orders and customer accounts stay in place. **The team has 3 members with strict roles:** - **Claude (AI)** generates all Liquid, JS vanilla, CSS and JSON-LD code. Each component is delivered annotated, ready to paste, with a detailed technical brief. Claude also generates guides, QA checklists and documentation. - **Sophie (project manager)** configures the theme editor (colors, fonts, content), creates metafields and 17 collections via the admin interface, configures Search & Discovery and Shopify Email, imports products and 301 redirects via native Shopify CSV, runs the project, executes cross-browser QA and validates every delivery visually. - **You (integrator)** integrate the provided code into the Horizon 3.5.1 DEV theme, test in the real environment, and debug integration conflicts. **You do not code from scratch.** Your role is to integrate, test and debug. --- ## Your exact role 1. Apply the Mistricotine CSS tokens (provided) to the Horizon 3.5.1 DEV theme — **mandatory first task** 2. Integrate Liquid sections (6 templates, code provided by Claude) 3. Integrate custom JS components (code provided) and test live 4. Debug JS integration conflicts between apps and theme 5. Integrate Schema.org + canonicals into theme.liquid (code provided) 6. Integrate Judge.me Liquid snippet into PDP + Home (code provided) 7. Integrate Alma widget into PDP + payment sticky bar (code provided) 8. Verify Search & Discovery filters display correctly live (Sophie configures the S&D interface) 9. Execute Core Web Vitals corrections live (action plan provided by Claude) 10. Fix WCAG AA bugs reported by Sophie (VoiceOver + keyboard navigation tests) 11. Fix bugs reported by Sophie during cross-browser QA --- ## Scope of work ### 1. Template integration (6 templates — Liquid code provided) **HOME** — Video hero + animated ticker + USP strip + press strip + bundles + Offrir Mistricotine + featured pieces + annotated know-how + birth list (static editorial section) + reviews + newsletter + payment bar + footer + exit intent JS + sticky CTA **COLLECTION** — Hero + active filters + swatch hover + sticky bar + wishlist + product grid + SEO content + related collections *(FAQ Teaser: out of scope — handled by Sophie via theme editor)* **PRODUCT PAGE (PDP)** — Gallery + ATC + embroidery field JS + sticky ATC bar + tabs + complete the look + upsell + reviews **BLOG HOME** — Editorial hero + featured article + category navigation **ARTICLE** — Reading progress bar + share buttons + inline product block **NOTRE HISTOIRE (About)** — Editorial storytelling sections ### 2. Technical setup - **Schema.org** — JSON-LD integration in theme.liquid (code provided) - **Canonical tags FR/EN** — Liquid snippet integration (code provided) - **Search & Discovery** — verify filters display correctly live (Sophie configures the interface) - **301 redirects** — Sophie imports the CSV in Admin → Navigation → URL Redirects (Claude generates the file) ### 3. Apps - **Judge.me Reviews** — Liquid snippet PDP + Home (code provided) — free plan - **Shopify Email** — Sophie configures entirely in the interface, no dev required - **Alma** — 3x payment widget PDP + bar (code provided) — no subscription, commission on sales only ### 4. Performance - Sophie runs Lighthouse → Claude generates action plan → you execute corrections and measure live - Preload fonts, lazy load, defer scripts (snippets provided) ### 5. Accessibility - Sophie tests VoiceOver + keyboard navigation (protocol provided by Claude) - You fix WCAG AA bugs reported (aria attributes, focus states, contrast) ### 6. QA & handover - Sophie runs cross-browser QA with Claude’s checklist - You fix reported bugs - Claude generates full documentation — Sophie records Loom walkthroughs --- ## What we provide **At day 1:** - Access to Horizon 3.5.1 DEV theme (staff account, theme permissions only) - Mistricotine CSS tokens file to apply at kick-off - Brand guide (palette, typography, tone of voice) - All technical briefs per component - Step-by-step guides per phase **Delivered progressively, before each phase:** - Liquid section files (annotated, ready to paste) - JS vanilla components (ticker, exit intent, embroidery, sticky ATC, progress bar) - JSON-LD Schema.org + canonical snippet - Judge.me snippet + Alma integration code - 301 redirects CSV (native Shopify format) - QA checklist + VoiceOver protocol --- ## Expected deliverables - CSS tokens applied on Horizon 3.5.1 DEV theme - 6 templates integrated and functional - Zero JS console errors - Lighthouse mobile score ≥ 85 - WCAG AA on PDP, ATC flow and checkout entry - Judge.me + Alma integrated and tested with real data - QA bugs fixed --- ## Out of scope — Phase 2 post-launch **Birth list (functional)** — customization of Horizon native wishlist (renamed, PDP button, shareable link). To evaluate after launch based on customer demand. Estimated: 2–3h. --- ## Technical requirements Required: - Shopify Horizon v3.x (non-negotiable) - Liquid 5, JSON templates, Shopify 2.0 sections/blocks architecture - Metafields, Shopify Markets - Debugging JS conflicts between apps and theme Nice to have: - French proficiency - Experience with premium D2C brands - Experience with Judge.me or Alma on Shopify Not required: design, full-stack development, React, Next.js, headless Shopify, Search & Discovery configuration, 301 redirects import. --- ## Estimated scope **20–25 hours total.** Async workflow, task-by-task handoff. Target rate: max $35/h (equivalent to 30 €/h). | Phase | Duration | Deliverable | |---|---|---| | 1. Kick-off | 1 week | CSS tokens applied, DEV theme configured | | 2. Templates | 4–6 weeks | 6 templates integrated and validated | | 3. Apps + SEO | 2–3 weeks | Judge.me + Alma + Schema.org + canonicals | | 4. Performance + QA | 2–3 weeks | CWV ≥ 85, WCAG AA, bugs fixed | | 5. Post-launch | +1 week | Minor fixes | Total estimated duration: 10–15 weeks. No hard deadline — quality over speed. --- ## How to apply Applications without these 5 elements will not be reviewed. **1. Portfolio** — 2 to 3 live Shopify Horizon projects (verifiable links). **2. Methodology note (half page)** — how you handle a redesign on an existing Shopify instance without breaking inventory or checkout, and how you work async with a non-developer project manager. **3. Answers to these 3 questions:** - Have you worked with Shopify Horizon v3.x specifically? If yes, share a store URL or describe the project. - Describe a JS conflict between a Shopify app and a theme you debugged. What was the problem and how did you solve it? - Do you have experience with Judge.me or Alma on Shopify? **4. Quote** — fixed price preferred with breakdown per phase. Hourly acceptable if you estimate total hours with confidence. **5. Availability** — start date and weekly hours committed. We will shortlist 3 candidates within one week, schedule a 30-minute call, and select within 10 days.
title: Shopify Horizon Integrator — Premium Baby Brand Redesign (Liquid + JS, code provided)

description: Project Description I run a UK-based B2B ecommerce business (glass partitions) on Magento 2.4.7. I'm currently spending approximately £450/month on Google Ads but can't tell whether it's producing results because my conversion tracking is broken. I need a Google Ads specialist to audit the account, fix the tracking, remove waste, and hand it back working properly. Current situation Magento 2.4.7-p4 ecommerce site Google Analytics 4 is installed Google Ads account has multiple conversion actions, most are broken or not firing "Purchase" conversion flagged as "Needs attention" — 0 conversions recorded in 30 days despite real sales happening "Calls from ads" conversion active but recording 0 calls in 30 days (customers do call after clicking ads) A legacy Universal Analytics conversion action is still present and needs removing Most of my customers call or request a quote rather than buy online — offline conversion visibility is poor I don't have a developer on retainer, but I have full admin access to Magento, Google Ads, Google Analytics, and Google Tag Manager (if installed) What I need you to do Audit current conversion tracking across Google Ads and Google Analytics 4. Identify what's firing, what's broken, what's duplicate, and what's dead. Fix online purchase tracking so that every completed Magento order (including bank transfer orders, which go through Magento checkout but don't take payment immediately) is recorded as a conversion in Google Ads, with the correct order value passed through. Set up phone call conversion tracking so that calls from people who clicked an ad are recorded. Minimum threshold 60 seconds to count as a lead. Clean up the conversion actions list — remove the Universal Analytics action, remove any duplicate or redundant conversions, and designate one clear primary conversion for Purchase and one for Phone Call. Review campaign settings, search terms, and keywords. Add negative keywords to exclude irrelevant searches. Identify whether any campaigns are structurally misconfigured. Set up a simple reporting dashboard (in Google Ads or Looker Studio) that shows me, at a glance: spend, clicks, online conversions, call conversions, total conversion value, and ROAS. Provide a written handover covering what you changed, how the tracking now works, and what I should monitor going forward. Loom video walkthrough preferred. What I do NOT need Ongoing management. This is a one-off fix. Website redesign or SEO work (that's separate). Anyone who wants to push me straight into Performance Max without fixing tracking first. Access I can provide Google Ads admin access (via email invite) Google Analytics 4 admin access Magento admin access (to install/verify tags if needed) Google Tag Manager access if relevant Timeline and budget Target completion: within 7–10 days of hire Budget: £200–£400 fixed price for the full scope above Happy to pay more for a clearly experienced specialist with verifiable results To be considered, please include in your proposal Confirmation that you've set up Magento 2 → Google Ads conversion tracking before (specifically, not just Shopify or WooCommerce) How you'll verify the tracking is working end-to-end after you set it up (I want to understand your testing process) Whether you use Google Tag Manager or direct tag installation, and which you'd recommend for my situation and why An example of a previous similar job (screenshots or case study, with client details redacted is fine) Your proposed fixed price for the full scope Please do not send a generic proposal. I'll prioritise proposals that directly address points 1–5.
title: Google Ads audit and conversion tracking fix — Magento 2 B2B ecommerce site

description: We're Ringly.io. We sell "Seth," an AI phone agent for Shopify stores that handles US customer calls (orders, returns, shipping, product questions) in a native US accent. title: WeChat + Email Outreach Specialist — Chinese Shopify sellers selling in the US (Appointment setting)

description: We're Ringly.io, a European SaaS company that sells AI phone support to Shopify merchants selling into the US. We need you to help us hire a small team of WeChat marketing VAs in China who will reach out to Chinese cross-border Shopify sellers on our behalf. ─────────────────────────── WHO WE'RE HIRING (your job is to find these people): We're looking for remote, part-time VAs who: • Are Mandarin-native and based in mainland China • Have an active WeChat account they use daily • Understand Chinese cross-border ecommerce (independent Shopify stores, 跨境电商, 独立站) • Can write natural-sounding WeChat messages to merchants • Speak functional English (B2 — enough to read our briefs and reply in our Slack) • Want 10 hrs/week at ¥1,500-2,500/month (~$210-350 USD) • Will work from home with flexible hours They don't need sales experience. They don't need a university degree. They don't need to be in a Tier-1 city. We care more about judgment, work ethic, and WeChat/ecommerce literacy than a CV. The ideal candidate is probably someone who: • Has worked in ecommerce or cross-border (Shopify store assistant, Amazon FBA ops, operations at a 独立站, etc.) • Wants a flexible side income • Is curious, writes well in Chinese, and learns fast ─────────────────────────── WHAT WE NEED FROM YOU: Deliver a shortlist of 10 candidates that pass your screening. For each one we want: • A short profile (name, city, background, WeChat account age) • Your notes from a video interview in Mandarin • A 30-second English video from the candidate • Their rate expectation and availability • Your recommendation How you source them is up to you. You probably know the Chinese market better than we do — use whatever platforms, groups, or networks you think will produce the best candidates. We'll interview your top 5, hire 2-3, and run them through a 1-week paid trial. ─────────────────────────── YOUR PROFILE: • Based in mainland China (preferred), Hong Kong, or Taiwan • Comfortable recruiting remote workers • Can verify candidates' identity and language level via video • Fluent Mandarin + functional English (B2+) • Honest about what you can and can't deliver You don't need to be a senior recruiter. If you've hired 5+ people before and know how to screen for real humans vs scam applicants, you're qualified. ─────────────────────────── COMPENSATION: Hourly rate: $15-20/hr (based on your experience) Weekly cap: 15 hours/week Expected duration: 2-3 weeks Estimated total: $450-900 Payment via Upwork's hourly tracker. We review hours every Friday. If you deliver quality hires, we'll keep you on retainer ($200-300/month) to source more VAs as we grow.

description: Head of Customer Support — Multi-Brand E-CommerceRate: $6–9/hr USD depending on experience Commitment: Long-term, full-time or part-time Type: RemoteAbout the roleWe run a multi-brand e-commerce operation across several markets (UK, AU, FR, US). You'll review their work, coach them, own the refund process, and work directly with the two founders on improving our CS operation.What you'll do Lead a team of CS representatives across multiple stores Review 10 tickets per rep daily and share feedback with the team Review 100% of new rep tickets during their first 2 weeks Approve and process all refund tickets Maintain and propose improvements to our CS templates and partial refund funnel Train new CS reps using our existing training materials Run weekly 1-on-1s with each rep Submit a daily tracker to the founders Manage team scheduling across timezones What we're looking forRequired: 2+ years of customer support experience, with time in a team lead, QA, or supervisor role Fluent written English — you'll be giving feedback that needs to be clear and professional Strong attention to detail — you catch what others miss in their tickets Coaching mindset — you enjoy developing people, not just pointing out mistakes Reliable, self-driven, and comfortable owning outcomes without being managed long-term commitment Nice to have: Dropshipping CS experience Experience managing a small remote team Freshdesk experience (we'll train you if not) Additional languages What we offer Long-term stable role with a growing operation Direct access to two founders — you'll learn how an e-commerce business is actually run Real ownership of the CS function — this isn't a ticket-closer role Room to grow as we scale to more stores How to apply Start your application with the word "leather" so we know you read the full post. The customer got the right outcome, but the tone was cold and two sentences were off. How do you give feedback — and would you give it publicly in a team channel, or privately? What's your track record with CS teams — how many reps have you led, on what platforms, for what type of business? Applications without the word "leather" and answers to these three questions will not be reviewed.

You’ll start by managing our email support channel, with the opportunity to later oversee live chat and phone support as the team expands. What you’ll do: - Manage the customer support team and ensure strong daily performance - Make sure agents meet KPIs and quality standards - Audit tickets and agent performance regularly - Provide feedback, coaching, and training to agents - Answer agent questions and support escalations - Help improve workflows and enforce SOPs - Step in and process tickets when queue volume is high - Use Gorgias and other tools to keep support operations running smoothly What we’re looking for: - 5+ years of experience managing customer support teams - Strong background in QA, KPI management, and agent coaching - Willing to be hands-on, not just supervise from a distance - Excellent written and spoken English - Highly detail-oriented, organized, and process-driven - Experience in eCommerce support is a strong plus - Experience with Gorgias is required or strongly preferred - Familiarity with Google Sheets, Shopify, Slack, and Notion is a plus Work details: - Fully remote - 30–40 hours per week - Monday–Friday, 9:00 AM–5:00 PM EST availability required - Competitive pay based on experience If you’re an experienced support leader who knows how to improve team performance while keeping service quality high, we’d love to hear from you.

description: Hello! We are a fast-growing e-commerce fashion brand looking for a rockstar Web Developer to build, optimize, and scale our online store for performance and growth. This role is not just about writing code—it’s about creating a seamless, high-converting shopping experience that drives revenue. Who We’re Looking For: We want an A+ developer who: Builds fast, responsive, and conversion-optimized eCommerce experiences. Thinks strategically—understands how development impacts revenue, UX, and retention. Works independently and solves technical challenges without hand-holding. Is fast, efficient, and can handle multiple projects simultaneously. Continuously improves site speed, UX, and backend performance. Stays on top of new tools, AI, and eCommerce tech trends. Loves eCommerce, performance optimization, and scaling brands. Key Responsibilities: Develop, customize, and maintain our eCommerce website (Shopify / custom stack). Optimize site speed, mobile responsiveness, and overall UX. Build high-converting landing pages, product pages, and funnels. Implement A/B tests and CRO improvements. Integrate apps, APIs, and third-party tools (Klaviyo, analytics, etc.). Troubleshoot bugs, checkout issues, and performance bottlenecks. Collaborate with marketing, design, and media buying teams. Ensure clean, scalable, and well-documented code. Must-Have Skills: Strong experience with Shopify (Liquid), HTML, CSS, JavaScript. Experience with conversion rate optimization (CRO) and performance-focused builds. Understanding of site speed optimization and Core Web Vitals. Experience with eCommerce integrations (email, SMS, analytics, tracking pixels). Ability to build custom sections, templates, and landing pages quickly. Familiarity with Git/version control and clean dev practices. Preferred Skills: Experience with Shopify Plus or headless commerce. Familiarity with React / Next.js or modern frameworks. Experience with A/B testing tools and data analysis. Understanding of paid traffic funnels and attribution tracking. Basic design/UX eye (knows what converts vs. just what looks good). Bonus: Available to work weekends when needed (big plus 💰) Experience in fashion, apparel, or DTC brands Experience working with high-growth brands Our Values: Customer First – every build improves the user experience High Integrity – clean work, accountability, no shortcuts Ownership – you treat the site like it’s your own Speed Wins – we move fast and execute To Apply: Include: Links to eCommerce sites you’ve built or worked on Examples of before/after improvements (speed, conversion, UX) Your experience with Shopify or similar platforms Answer this in your subject line: What’s 3 + 5 = ?

description: Description: I’m looking for an experienced Shopify store designer and developer to help me build and design a professional website for my new online business. I sell baby safety products and Montessori-inspired sensory toys, and I also offer holistic child care consulting services. The website needs to function as both an e-commerce store and a platform where clients can book consultations with me. Key Requirements: 1. Shopify Store Design and Development: • Experience with Shopify: Must have previous experience designing and developing Shopify stores, preferably for businesses in the baby products or wellness niche. • Responsive and Clean Design: The website needs to be mobile-friendly and have a professional, clean, and calming look, reflecting the trust and care parents look for in baby products. • Montessori Section: A dedicated section for Montessori products, organized by age group: 0-12 months, 1-3 years, 3+ years. • Secure Payment Integration: Set up payment gateways like PayPal, Stripe, and ensure the checkout process is easy to navigate. 2. Consultation Booking System: • Integration of Booking System: I need a booking and scheduling system integrated with the website to allow clients to book consultations with me. • Consultation Options: The system should allow clients to choose from different types of consultations (e.g., sleep training, holistic child care advice). • Payment Integration for Consultations: I would like clients to be able to pay for consultations when booking, so payment integration for services is needed. • Calendar Integration: The system must sync with a calendar tool (like Google Calendar or Outlook) for easy scheduling and updates. 3. SEO Optimization: • Basic SEO optimization for the e-commerce side of the website and for the consultation services page, ensuring it ranks well for relevant keywords. 4. Shopify Apps Integration: • Familiarity with integrating Shopify apps to manage products, handle inventory, and support the overall functionality of the store. Additional Skills: • Experience with e-commerce platforms like Shopify and user experience (UX) best practices. • Ability to integrate booking systems with e-commerce platforms. • Strong communication skills and the ability to work collaboratively to build a seamless user experience for both product sales and service bookings. Project Details: • Type of Work: Shopify store design, customization, and development, as well as integration of a booking system for consultations • Budget: Please provide a fixed price for the complete project. • Timeline: I’m looking to launch the store and consultation system in 3-4 weeks About Me: I’m a holistic child care consultant with a focus on Montessori-inspired toys and baby safety products. I offer personalized consulting services to help parents create a nurturing, safe environment for their children. The website will showcase my products, offer consultations, and allow parents to easily book appointments with me.
title: Shopify Store + Consultation Booking System for Holistic Child Care Consultant
