description: We're a Danish e-commerce marketing agency running paid traffic (Meta, TikTok, Google) for 20+ Shopify brands. We're looking for an ongoing tracking freelancer who can handle a steady stream of funnel setups — paid per funnel, not hourly. 🟢 The setup we need tracked: For many of our clients, we run sales funnels that look like this: → Paid ad (Meta / TikTok / Google) → Advertorial or listicle (hosted on Webflow, styled as if it's from the Shopify store for credibility) → CTA button to the Shopify product page → Checkout / purchase The Webflow-to-Shopify handoff is where tracking breaks for most people. We need clean, reliable attribution across the full path — ad → advertorial → product page → purchase — so we can actually see which creatives and funnels drive sales. 🟢 What we're looking for: - 3+ years with Shopify tracking (GTM, pixels, server-side, customer events) - Hands-on with Stape.io (sGTM containers, Conversion APIs, server-side setups) - Proven experience tracking across separate domains (Webflow landing → Shopify store) without losing attribution - Solid grip on Meta CAPI, TikTok Events API, Google Enhanced Conversions 🟢 Bonus: You've set up tracking for advertorial/listicle funnels before and know exactly what breaks You're filling the one gap that's costing us attribution clarity — nothing more, nothing less. 🟢 How it works: - You pitch us a fixed price per funnel setup - We start with 1–2 client funnels as a test - If it clicks, we have a steady pipeline — new funnel setups regularly, paid per setup 🟢 To apply — answer these 4 questions (generic applications get ignored): - Have you set up tracking across two separate domains (e.g. Webflow landing → Shopify store) before? Walk us through exactly how you solved it. - How do you handle Meta CAPI / TikTok Events API when the user journey starts on a domain that isn't the Shopify store? - Share one tracking setup you're proud of — what was the challenge, and how did you solve it? - What's your fixed price per funnel setup for a standard Webflow→Shopify case with Meta + TikTok + Google tracking?
title: E-commerce Tracking Specialist — Webflow→Shopify Funnel Tracking (Ongoing, Fixed Price Per Setup)

description: We run an online store and export our order data monthly from Shopify into Excel. The data is messy — inconsistent product names, duplicate orders, missing customer regions, and mixed date formats. We need a freelancer to clean the dataset, organise it properly, and build a clear sales dashboard we can use for monthly reporting. The dataset covers 12 months of orders (~1,500 rows) and includes: order ID, date, customer name, product name, category, quantity, unit price, discount, total revenue, sales channel (website, Amazon, social), and shipping region. Scope of work: — Import and clean the raw CSV export using Power Query — Fix inconsistent product names, remove duplicates, standardise date formats — Add calculated columns: net revenue after discount, month, quarter, channel group — Build a monthly revenue trend with MoM % change — Analyse top 10 products by revenue and units sold — Break down sales by channel (website vs Amazon vs social) — Identify low-performing products and high-return months — Build an Excel dashboard with slicers for Month, Category, and Channel Deliverables: — Cleaned Excel file with Power Query steps intact (so we can refresh it next month) — Product performance table (top/bottom performers) — Channel breakdown summary — Monthly revenue trend with chart — Dashboard with slicers — one-screen, no scrolling — Short written summary: what sold, what didn't, and where to focus next quarter Required skills: Microsoft Excel · Power Query · Data cleaning · Pivot Tables · Chart design · Attention to detail Preferred: Experience with Shopify or e-commerce order data · Understanding of sales KPIs (AOV, conversion, return rate) · Ability to deliver a clean, non-technical summary.
title: Dashboard for E-commerce reporting

description: Title: Shopify Theme – Implement Complete Website Design (Theme Editor First, Step-by-Step Brief Provided) Hi, I'm looking for a reliable Shopify expert to implement my complete website design into my Shopify store. ⚠️ IMPORTANT: This is NOT a design or copywriting job. I will provide a complete, finalized brief with: - All sections defined and numbered - All texts ready to copy-paste (in German) - All images named and organized - All colors, fonts, button styles specified - Mobile + desktop layout reference Your job is to implement this brief faithfully — section by section. No creative changes, no rewrites. ⚠️ IMPORTANT: 90% of the work must be done through the Shopify Theme Editor (Customizer) — NOT through code edits. Custom CSS or Liquid edits should ONLY be used when absolutely necessary, and must be approved by me first. ABOUT THE PROJECT: - Brand: Sibicura — self-care & wellness brand - Theme: [TO BE CONFIRMED — currently Canyon, possibly switching] - Language: German (you do NOT need to speak German — just copy-paste the texts I provide) - Pages: Complete website (Homepage, Product Page, About, Contact, Footer, Header) - Access: Shopify Collaborator access will be provided WHAT I NEED FROM YOU: ✅ Implement every section exactly as specified in my brief ✅ Use the Theme Editor / Customizer for all changes (90%+) ✅ Apply all color, font, and button settings as specified by me ✅ Place all texts and images in the correct positions ✅ Configure buttons, links, CTAs as instructed ✅ Ensure perfect mobile responsiveness ✅ Test all pages on desktop + mobile before delivery ✅ Up to 2 revision rounds included WHAT I DO NOT WANT: ❌ Editing theme code unless explicitly approved by me ❌ Rewriting or "improving" my texts ❌ Making creative decisions about design (colors, fonts, layout) without my approval ❌ Adding sections that are not in the brief ❌ Installing apps without my approval WHY MANUAL / NO-CODE: I want to maintain and update the website myself in the future. Theme code edits make this difficult and create dependencies. TIMELINE: - Brief will be delivered within 24h after hire - Expected completion: by Tuesday, April 28, 2026 (latest: Wednesday, April 29) REQUIREMENTS: - Minimum 95% Job Success Score - At least a few completed Shopify projects - Portfolio with at least 3 live Shopify stores (please share links) - Experience with section-based premium themes (Canyon, Impact, Prestige, Symmetry) SCREENING QUESTIONS (please answer in your proposal): 1. Have you worked with Shopify premium themes before? Share an example. 2. Are you comfortable working ONLY in the Theme Editor without modifying theme code? 3. How do you handle a brief that requires faithful implementation without creative changes? 4. How many revision rounds do you include? 5. Can you complete this by April 28–29, 2026? Looking forward to your proposal.
title: Shopify - Implement Complete Website Design (Theme Editor Only, Step-by-Step Brief Provided)

description: Shopify Theme Developer — Convert 51 HTML pages to custom theme (£1,200- 1,800 fixed price) About this project I'm Maria Daramola, founder of milk & more - a premium baby and toddler essentials brand launching from London. I have 51 fully designed HTML pages ready to convert into a custom Shopify Basic theme. What's already done 51 mobile-responsive HTML pages in a finished design system Full brand identity (fonts, colours, logo) UK GDPR-compliant privacy and terms pages with my company details A CSV product template ready to populate Shopify theme scaffolding as reference (I'll share this) What I need you to do Convert my 51 HTML pages into a custom Shopify Liquid theme Build custom page templates (not the default page template) for: Our Story, Our Promise, Expert Advice, Contact, Careers, Hampers, Build-a-Bundle, all 5 gift sub-pages, and both Personal Shopping pages (I'll tell you which version to use) Build custom collection templates for: main shop, Feeding, Nursery, Clothing, Essentials, Travel, Gifts, Sale, New In Build product page with variants, tabs, recommendations Build cart, account, sign-in, forgot-password pages Wire up forms: Contact, Consultation, Careers, Newsletter to submit to my email via Shopify forms or Formspree Install and style gift registry app (Guest List or similar) Install and style wishlist app Style Shopify's checkout via Checkout Editor to match my design as closely as Basic plan allows (I understand full checkout custom requires Plus) Set up Shopify Payments, shipping zones (UK + EU + ROW), taxes, policies Style the Shopify-generated email templates (order confirmation, shipping, welcome) Test on desktop, tablet, iPhone — all links, all forms, a live test order Connect my domain with SSL One-hour handover call + one-page cheat sheet for me to manage the store day-to-day Minimum quality standard Every page you port must match its HTML reference at 94% visual accuracy or better when compared side-by-side at 1440px desktop and 375px iPhone widths. If you're not sure whether a specific design element is achievable in Shopify Basic, tell me before starting so we can agree. Scope (Shopify Basic plan, £25/month) I know checkout on Basic plan uses Shopify's structure with styling customisation only — this is acceptable. Do NOT propose Shopify Plus (£2,000/month) for this project. Timeline 4-6 weeks from kickoff to live. Budget Fixed price £1,200-1,800. I will NOT consider hourly rates or time-and-materials pricing. Payment structure 30% on contract signing 30% after homepage + product page + cart templates delivered and approved 40% after final testing, launch, and handover What I need in your proposal Links to 2-3 Shopify stores you've converted from external HTML designs Your fixed-price quote within the £1,200-1,800 range Your committed timeline with 3 milestone dates Confirmation you'll build custom page templates (not default) for all content pages Confirmation of Shopify Basic scope (no Plus upsell) List of any apps you'd recommend beyond registry + wishlist I have all reference materials ready to share in a zip file once we agree to work together. Happy to have a 20-minute kickoff call with shortlisted candidates.
title: Shopify Theme Developer — Convert 51 HTML pages to custom theme (£1,200-1,800 fixed price)

description: I'm launching Vettique (vettique.com) — an AI-powered supplier vetting tool for Amazon and Shopify sellers. The product is live! And, now I need someone to build the marketing presence from scratch. This really could be an exciting opportunity. This is a ground-up role. There are no existing accounts, no content calendar, no brand voice document. You'll create all of it. If that excites you, keep reading. If you need structure to already be in place, this isn't the right fit. What you'll own: - Create and manage our presence on LinkedIn, Reddit, X, and Instagram - Develop a content strategy and execute it yourself — no team, no delegation - Build genuine community engagement in Amazon FBA and Shopify seller spaces - Work with one researcher who has already mapped the key Reddit communities and identified what resonates - Drive organic traffic and early user signups through content and community You are the right person if: - You've built social presence for a product or startup from zero before - You're a doer — you write the posts, engage in the threads, and track what works - You understand the Amazon seller and e-commerce ecosystem, or can get up to speed fast - You have good instincts for what sounds authentic versus what sounds like an ad - You want to grow with a portfolio of ventures, not just do a one-off job You are not the right person if: - You manage a team and would pass execution to others - You've only worked on established brands with existing audiences - You need a detailed brief for every piece of content Phase 1: 3 weeks, $300 fixed: - Set up and brand all social accounts - Deliver an execution plan within 48 hours of hire - Begin daily content and community engagement - Weekly report with links to all activity and honest assessment of what's working - Target: first 50 organic visitors from social and community channels What comes next: If Phase 1 goes well, this role expands. I have other ventures in the pipeline and want one person who becomes the marketing lead across all of them. This is the ground floor of something bigger if you want it to be. To apply, please answer these three questions: - Share an example of social or community presence you personally built from scratch — link or screenshot - What would you do in the first 48 hours after being hired? - How many hours per week are you available and what is your timezone?
title: Founding Marketing Person for AI SaaS Startup — Organic Growth, Socials & Community (Ground Up)

description: We’re looking for a website designer/developer to help build a premium editorial-style website for a food/culture video series. The main purpose of the site is to act as a home for our YouTube series. The videos will be hosted on YouTube, but the website needs to present them in a more premium, editorial and cinematic way — closer to Nowness, a film/culture publication, or a high-end food/lifestyle platform than a standard YouTube channel or ecommerce store. We already have a working prototype and clear visual direction. The site is image-led, cinematic and editorial, with large visual tiles, story pages, smooth transitions, strong typography and video content at the centre of the experience. The website needs to be optimised for video content, including easy browsing, clean embedded YouTube playback, episode/story pages, and a layout that encourages people to watch more of the series. There will also be a small Shopify-integrated shop, but the shop should feel secondary and integrated cleanly into the overall editorial experience. We do not want a generic Shopify template or basic ecommerce site. The project will likely involve: Reviewing and refining our existing prototype/design direction Building a responsive website for desktop and mobile Creating a homepage built around featured video content Creating episode pages with embedded YouTube videos Creating editorial/story pages to support each episode Creating a clean video archive/library so users can browse episodes easily Ensuring YouTube embeds feel polished and integrated into the design Adding smooth transitions, hover states and clean navigation Integrating Shopify for a small online store Setting up the backend so we can update videos, stories, images and products ourselves Advising on the best build approach, whether that is Webflow + YouTube embeds + Shopify, Shopify with a custom theme, or another suitable setup The priority is a site that feels premium, cinematic and easy to watch — not a generic Shopify store. Ideal experience: Strong design eye, not just development ability Experience designing video-led, editorial, magazine, film, food, culture or creative studio websites Experience embedding and presenting YouTube videos in a polished way Figma, Webflow, Shopify or custom front-end experience Ability to work from an existing prototype and improve it Good understanding of mobile-first video viewing Strong attention to typography, spacing, image treatment, transitions and user experience - Please send 2–3 examples of video-led, editorial or premium websites you have designed or built. - How would you approach building a site that hosts a YouTube series, while making the videos feel more premium than a standard YouTube embed? - How would you structure the site so users can easily browse episodes and watch more content? - Would you recommend Webflow + YouTube embeds + Shopify, a custom Shopify theme, or another setup? Why? - How would you make sure the website performs well on mobile with embedded video? - Are you more design-led, development-led, or comfortable doing both? - What timeline would you recommend for this project, broken down by stages — design review/refinement, website build, Shopify integration, testing and launch?
title: Editorial Website Designer / Developer Needed — Nowness-style site with Shopify integration

description: We're a fast-growing women's wellness brand doing multiple 7-figures every month on Meta. Three SKUs, all on Shopify. We scale hard on the front end. Now we need someone to fix the back end. Our business model is built on 2-month breakeven, which means every subscriber who stays past month 2 is pure profit. Right now too many don't. Most customers stay 2 months and leave. That breaks our model. I'm looking for one operator to own the entire retention engine. Not an email marketer. Not an agency. A real retention lead who can move renewal rate, save rate, LTV, and chargebacks, with proof. This seat owns subscriptions, LTV, retention, and everything that happens after the customer clicks "buy." The problem: -Average customer stays 2 months. We need that number up, minimum 5 months. -Renewal rate is too low (around 60%). Target is 75%+. -Save rate on cancel is working but needs to push higher. -Target is 15%+ across all SKUs. -Chargeback rate is at 0.37% but target is under 0.30%. -Customer portal needs a full rebuild, portal per product, cancellation per product, not one global flow. If you can fix this, this is the seat. If you can't, don't apply. What you'll own on day one: -Cancel flow optimization in Loop, keep pushing save rate past 15% -Subscription retention strategy, discount ladders, pause logic, swap to bundles, skip cycles -Customer portal rebuild, portal per product, cancel per product (not global) -Chargeback reduction, pre-rebill flow, refund SOP, dispute automation -Post-purchase experience, insert cards, first-refill delight, etc. -Loyalty program, build it from scratch -LTV analysis + cohort reporting -Klaviyo flows, pre-rebill, winback, reactivation, welcome, post-purchase, everything about subscriptions -Klaviyo campaigns, weekly newsletters, promos, drops, education series -First 30 days = 100% balm subscription. After that, all three SKUs. The goal: -Get customers staying 5+ months, not 2 -Renewal rate to 75%+ -Save rate to 15%+ -Chargebacks under 0.30% -LTV up across every cohort Our business model is 2-month breakeven. Every extra month a customer stays = pure profit. You're not just fighting churn. You're compounding LTV. How we work here (read this carefully): -We move fast. Speed beats perfection every time. -We ship weekly. Not quarterly. Weekly. -We test everything. No decisions on vibes. Data or strong opinions loosely held. -We pull from the best. Always studying brands ahead of us. Magic Spoon. Liquid IV. Oats Overnight. Gruns. Everyday Dose. IM8. Primal Queen. Ryze. We get the idea and we test FAST. -Proactive first. I'll send you ideas to test when I see things, and you'll do the same for me. But don't wait on me to feed the pipeline. You're running it. -Straight talk. If you don't know, say so. If you disagree, push back. Don't hedge. Don't corporate-speak me. If any of that sounds annoying to you, we won't last a week. Who you are: -You've worked brand-side at an 8 or 9-figure subscription DTC brand. Supplements or wellness is a plus. If your resume is all client work or one-time purchase brands, close the tab. -Bonus if you've also worked SaaS retention, the cohort math and LTV modeling translates. But you must have DTC sub experience first. SaaS-only = skip. -You know Loop Subscriptions cold (or you've run Recharge at real scale and can transition). -You've moved real retention numbers, churn, save rate, renewal, LTV, and you can prove it with before/after data. -You understand trial-to-subscription models. You know the 2-month breakeven math. -You read A/B tests. You design them. You call them when they're done. You document the learnings. -You've built a loyalty program before or know exactly how you'd build one. -You're fluent in English, written and spoken. You study the best DTC subscription brands obsessively. Bonus (huge plus): You're AI-native. You use ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity, or whatever's current to move 10x faster than people who don't. You're ahead of the curve, not catching up to it. If you're using AI to write flow copy at scale, segment customers, automate cohort analysis, personalize cancel offers, or build faster, tell us exactly how. This isn't a requirement. It's an edge. The person who has it is the person who wins this seat. Who you are NOT: -Agency-only, never worked brand-side -Email agency owner who'll pitch their team instead of themselves -"Email marketer" who sends newsletters, we need a retention strategist who owns subs -Info product or coaching background -Never worked on subscription DTC -SaaS-only with no consumer subscription experience -Someone who needs hand-holding -Someone who measures hours instead of output If any of these are you, skip. Compensation: -30-day paid trial: balm subscription only -After trial: full-time, long-term, in-house -Performance-based bonuses tied to renewal rate, save rate, and LTV -30+ hours per week -We don't count hours. We count results, what gets shipped. Put the word "retention" at the top of your application. If it's not there, we don't read the rest. How to apply: -The word "retention" at the top -2-3 subscription DTC brands you've worked on (live links to the brands) -Before/after numbers for renewal rate, save rate, or churn, whatever you moved -Your answers to the 5 questions below -A 1-minute Loom video telling us why you're the right fit for this seat. No script. Just talk. Link it in your application. Use tools like: https://www.loom.com Anything missing, we skip you.
title: Head of Retention & Subscriptions · Own LTV · 8-Figure DTC Brand

Could you please review the website and let me know your initial SEO observations, especially in terms of: * What key SEO issues you can identify (technical, on-page, speed, structure, etc.) * What improvements are needed to make the site more visible on search engines like Google * Any immediate “quick wins” you can see for traffic growth Also, I would like to understand your working approach: * How many hours of work would you estimate are needed to make the site fully error-free and SEO-ready? * What potential do you see for this website once your SEO work is implemented? * Realistically, how long do you think it would take for Bikemandu.com to start appearing ahead of competitors in search results for relevant keywords? * What would be your step-by-step SEO strategy for improving rankings and traffic? description: Job Description: We are looking for a Shopify expert to set up a reliable system that ensures orders are routed to the correct regional warehouse or 3PL based on the customer’s location. We operate in multiple regions (US, UK, EU), and we want to ensure that: US orders are fulfilled by our US 3PL UK orders are fulfilled by our UK 3PL EU orders are fulfilled by our EU warehouse This needs to work seamlessly and automatically within Shopify. Scope of Work: Configure Shopify to route orders based on shipping country/region Set up or optimize Shopify Markets, shipping profiles, and/or location-based fulfillment logic Integrate or configure our 3PL(s) to ensure correct order assignment Recommend the best approach (native Shopify vs apps vs custom logic) Ensure inventory is properly mapped per location Test end-to-end order flow across US, UK, and EU Troubleshoot any conflicts with current setup Key Requirements: Strong experience with Shopify multi-location inventory & fulfillment routing Experience working with 3PL integrations (Mintsoft, Extensiv, Amazon MCF, or similar) Deep understanding of Shopify Markets and international store setups Ability to design a scalable and low-maintenance solution Nice to Have: Experience with multi-store vs single-store architecture decisions Knowledge of tax/VAT implications (UK/EU) Experience with routing apps (if needed) Deliverables: Fully working fulfillment routing system by region Verified test orders for US, UK, and EU flows Clear documentation of setup Recommendations for scaling or improvements To Apply: Please include: Examples of similar multi-region Shopify setups Your recommended approach (briefly) Timeline and tools/apps you’d use
title: Shopify Expert Needed – Multi-Region Order Routing (US / UK / EU Fulfillment)

description: Shopify Template Customization for Premium Functional Candy / Electrolyte Brand We are looking for an experienced Shopify designer/developer to customize an existing Shopify template for a new premium functional candy/electrolyte brand. This is not a custom-coded website from scratch. We want to use a strong Shopify theme/template as the foundation, then customize it so the site feels polished, premium, fun, trustworthy, mobile-friendly, and built to convert. We like the general e-commerce style and conversion flow of high-performing CPG brands such as Olipop, SmartSweets, Poppi, Liquid Death, Magic Spoon, LMNT, Feastables, and AG1. We also mocked up a design with copy on BASE44 (however all images need to be replaced, and colors as well) https://electralix-suckers-shop.base44.app We are not looking to copy any brand directly. We want the same level of clean product presentation, strong homepage flow, benefit-driven storytelling, trust-building sections, bold CTAs, subscription/bundle-friendly layout, and mobile-first shopping experience. Budget: $250 USD fixed price Platform: Shopify Approach: Existing Shopify theme/template customization What we need help with: * Customize an existing Shopify theme/template * Create a polished homepage layout * Set up a strong above-the-fold hero section * Add clear CTA sections throughout the site * Improve product page flow and layout * Create sections for benefits, ingredients, how it works, and product education * Add trust-building elements such as icons, guarantees, reviews, shipping/payment reassurance, and social proof * Add bundle/product-selection style sections if supported by the theme * Make the site clean, responsive, and mobile-first * Refine typography, spacing, image placement, and visual hierarchy * Make the site feel premium, modern, fun, and conversion-focused Ideal candidate: * Strong Shopify experience * Experience customizing Shopify templates * Good eye for modern CPG/e-commerce design * Understands DTC homepage flow and product storytelling * Understands CTA placement, product education, trust-building, and mobile UX * Can work efficiently within a lean budget * Can recommend best practices instead of only waiting for instructions Important: We understand this is a lean budget, so we are not expecting a fully custom website from scratch. We are looking for someone who can take a strong Shopify template and make it look professional, polished, and conversion-focused. If this project goes well, there may be ongoing paid work for additional pages, product pages, landing pages, CRO improvements, and future e-commerce brands. To apply, please send: 1. 3–5 Shopify or e-commerce websites you have worked on 2. Examples of Shopify template customization work if available 3. Your recommended Shopify theme/template for this type of CPG brand 4. Your estimated timeline 5. Confirmation that you can complete the first version within the $250 USD budget 6. A short note on how you would structure the homepage to improve conversions
title: Shopify Web Designer / Developer Needed for Premium E-Commerce Brand

description: **Overview** We are seeking an experienced e-commerce bookkeeper to help set up a new Xero account with a clean, accurate, and scalable financial structure. A critical part of this project is properly implementing A2X for Shopify and Amazon accounting. The initial project is focused on setup, with the potential for an ongoing monthly bookkeeping relationship. **Primary Responsibilities (Initial Project)** * Set up a new Xero account from scratch * Create a clean, e-commerce-appropriate chart of accounts * Set up and configure A2X for Shopify and Amazon (this is a key requirement) * Configure integrations (Shopify, Amazon, payment processors, etc.) as needed * Ensure proper handling of revenue, fees, refunds, COGS, and inventory * Establish best practices for ongoing bookkeeping and reporting * Provide clear documentation or guidance for maintaining the system **Potential Ongoing Responsibilities (Optional)** * Monthly bookkeeping and reconciliations * Financial reporting (P&L, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow) * Sales channel and payment processor reconciliation * Inventory and COGS tracking support * Cleanup and optimization as the business scales **Requirements** * Proven experience with e-commerce bookkeeping (Shopify, Amazon, etc.) * Strong expertise in Xero * Hands-on experience with A2X for Shopify and Amazon * Deep understanding of e-commerce accounting nuances (fees, payouts, inventory, etc.) * Experience setting up chart of accounts for e-commerce businesses * Detail-oriented and organized * Ability to communicate clearly and proactively **Nice to Have** * Experience with multi-channel or multi-entity setups * Familiarity with additional tools in the e-commerce accounting stack **Project Scope** * Phase 1: Xero setup + A2X implementation (fixed-price or hourly) * Phase 2 (optional): Ongoing monthly bookkeeping **To Apply** Please include: * A brief summary of your relevant e-commerce experience * Examples of similar Xero + A2X setups you’ve completed * Tools and integrations you typically use * Your availability and preferred engagement structure We are looking for someone who can build a clean foundation and potentially grow into a long-term partner.

How do you approach Shopping vs Performance Max? What’s your strategy for non-branded search without wasting spend? What are the most important factors for strong Shopping performance? (Bonus) How do you use Google Ads data to inform SEO opportunities?

description: This is not a basic PM role. description: Got you. Here’s the full spec you can hand to a developer. This gets old stock onto Marketplace under your business name only, keeps your assistant off your personal account, and doesn’t touch your current ads. Project Brief: Segment Shopify Clearance Stock to Facebook Marketplace Goal: List clearance/old stock on Facebook Marketplace under Page name. No personal profile involvement. No impact to existing Advantage+ ads. --- PART 1: Shopify Setup - Hide Clearance From Main Store + Ads Create hidden collection Shopify Admin → Products → Collections → Create collection Name: Clearance - Hidden Type: Manual Conditions: None Search engine listing: Click "Edit" → Uncheck "Search engines can see this page" Sales channels: Uncheck "Online Store" so it doesn't show in navigation Add products: Add all old stock items here Tag all clearance products Shopify → Products → Bulk edit → Add tag: clearance Also add tag: main_store to ALL non-clearance products Why: Lets us auto-segment in Meta later. Stop clearance syncing to main Facebook catalog Shopify → Settings → Apps and sales channels → Facebook & Instagram → Settings → Product catalog → Publishing → Include products: Custom → Condition: Product tag IS main_store → Save Result: Your current Advantage+ ads keep running from Main Catalog. Clearance is excluded within 24h. --- PART 2: Meta Business Setup - New Clearance Catalog Create separate clearance catalog business.facebook.com/commerce_manager → Catalogs → Add Catalog → Create New Catalog → Type: E-commerce → Name: Clearance Only AU → Connect to: Select your Business Portfolio + Page Connect Shopify as data source for clearance only Commerce Manager → Clearance Only AU catalog → Catalog → Data Sources → Add Items → Partner Platform → Shopify → Connect → Choose: "Select specific collections" → Tick "Clearance - Hidden" only → Default product category: Set to match your goods → Import schedule: Hourly Set sales channel visibility Commerce Manager → Clearance Only AU catalog → Settings → Sales Channels Marketplace: ON Facebook Shop: OFF Instagram Shopping: OFF Ads: OFF for now - we'll create separate ads later Turn OFF Marketplace for Main Catalog Commerce Manager → Your existing Main Catalog → Settings → Sales Channels Marketplace: OFF Facebook Shop: ON Instagram: ON Ads: ON This stops new stock from appearing on Marketplace. --- PART 3: Permissions - Give Assistant Access Without Personal Profile Add assistant to Business Portfolio business.facebook.com/settings → People → Add Email: [assistant email] Employee access: ON Assign assets: - Catalog → Clearance Only AU → Manage - Commerce Account → Manage - Page → Messages + Content access only DO NOT give: Ad Account, Main Catalog, Business Settings, Personal profile Assistant workflow after this: She logs into: business.facebook.com Sees only: Commerce Manager → Clearance Only AU + Page Inbox Can: Add/edit clearance products, reply to Marketplace messages Cannot: See your personal profile, touch ads, see main products --- PART 4: Marketplace Listing Settings Configure Commerce Account Commerce Manager → Settings → Business Assets → Commerce Account → Business Info: Add GST number, business address in Sydney → Payouts: Connect bank account or PayPal → Shipping: Create profile "AU Standard" → $10 or Free over $50 → Return policy: 30 days, buyer pays return → Customer service email: support@[yourdomain].com Set checkout method for clearance Commerce Manager → Clearance Only AU → Settings → Checkout Method Recommended: "Message to buy" for fastest setup Alternative: "Checkout on another website" → Link to Shopify with auto-discount code --- PART 5: Optional - Run Ads for Clearance Only Only do this AFTER steps 1-4 are live Ads Manager → Create → Sales campaign → Advantage+ Catalog ON → Catalog: Select "Clearance Only AU" → Product set: All products → Audience: Remarketing OR Broad AU → Budget: $10-20/day to start This will NOT affect your existing ASC because it uses a different catalog. --- PART 6: QA Checklist Before Handoff | Check | Where to look | Pass condition | | **Name on Marketplace** | Incognito → http://facebook.com/marketplace → Search test SKU | Shows "[Your Store Name]" not personal name | | **Ads isolation** | Ads Manager → Existing ASC → Product set | Still uses Main Catalog only | | **Assistant access** | Business Settings → People → Assistant | Sees Clearance catalog only, no Ad Account | | **Shop tab clean** | Facebook Page → Shop tab | No clearance items visible | | **Messages routing** | Send test message to Marketplace listing | Lands in Page inbox in Meta Business Suite | --- Developer Notes + Gotchas Time to live: Meta takes 24-72h to approve Commerce Account + first Marketplace listings. Category rules: Used/refurbished/digital goods banned on Marketplace AU. "Last season new" is fine. Inventory sync: Shopify → Clearance catalog is 1-way. If item sells on Marketplace, manually set to 0 in Shopify to prevent oversell. GST: Add "Price includes GST. ABN: XX XXX XXX XXX" to product descriptions for AU compliance. Images: Marketplace crops to square. Use 1:1 images or Meta will auto-crop badly.
title: Meta commerce - Shopify listing

description: We're launching a premium beauty brand and need a talented Shopify designer and developer to finalise the already built website structure. The pages are already built and structured using a third party app. This is not a build from scratch - we need someone who can come in, think like a CRO specialist, execute like a developer, and finish like a designer. This is a staged project. Stage one is the coming soon page only. We want to see how you work, how you think, and what you deliver before we hand you the rest of the site. Perform well here and the rest of the project will follow (stage two will require knowledge of Shopify Subscriptions). STAGE ONE SCOPE — Coming Soon Page 1. Functional Fix (priority) The email and SMS capture form on the page was AI-generated and doesn't currently function correctly. We need: - Email field to capture subscribers directly into Shopify - SMS opt-in to work alongside email as a single form submission - Both to integrate as one customer record in Shopify with email and SMS marketing consent captured correctly 2. Branding & Design Pass (IMPORTANT) The page is structurally solid but unbranded. Complete brand guidelines will be provided. Every section needs to feel premium, conversion-focused, and visually stunning - not just technically correct. Colours, typography, spacing, buttons, and visual hierarchy all need to be on point and aligned to the guidelines. 3. Visuals (optional) Add high quality stock or AI-generated imagery/video to sections that currently have empty visual slots. You should have a strong eye for what works in a premium skincare context. STAGE TWO SCOPE - Home Page & Product Page. Both pages are already built and structured to the same level as the coming soon page. The scope mirrors stage one with the following additions: - Full branding pass across both pages consistent with the coming soon page - Product page requires knowledge of Shopify subscription apps - we have a specific subscription strategy that needs to be built and designed to match our requirements - A second version of the product page will need to be created for A/B testing with an alternate subscription layout - main different will be in the production information block (different subscription model to test) - Any missing sections built out and copy dropped in from provided brief - Visual placeholders replaced with final or high quality stock/AI imagery Stage two is not guaranteed. It is only offered if you perform in stage one. WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR - Shopify Liquid and theme customisation experience - Strong visual design sensibility - you can look at a page and instinctively know what's off - CRO awareness - you understand why elements are placed where they are and how to make them convert - Experience with Shopify subscription apps - we have not picked one yet. - Someone who can elevate an existing build without needing a brief for every decision - Clean communication and fast turnaround TO APPLY Please include: - 2-3 examples of Shopify stores or landing pages you've worked on — tell us exactly what you worked on with each example. We see a lot of people share websites with no explanation of what they actually did on them. - At least one example that shows your design sensibility, not just technical execution - When quoting, feel free to quote for stage one only or for both stages — stage two is not guaranteed and is only awarded based on performance in stage one
title: Shopify Designer & Developer (CRO-Minded) - Final 20% of Site Before Launch

description: We're seeking a developer to build, in phases, an automated system covering inventory management → demand forecasting → factory purchase orders for our e-commerce business (Rakuten Ichiba + Shopify). Inventory sync is currently handled manually; automating this and building a data foundation is the starting point. The end goal is an AI system that forecasts demand from accumulated data and auto-generates purchase order drafts for our factories. ■ End Goal Accumulate inventory data in-house, forecast demand with AI, and auto-generate factory POs. Independence from third-party SaaS with data as our own asset is the core objective. ■ Development Phases Phase 1 has two scope options; please quote both. ----------------------------------------- PHASE 1-A — Lite: Ingestion + Master Inventory Only ----------------------------------------- Ingest orders from Rakuten and Shopify and decrement master inventory in our DB. No write-back to channels. Scope: - Rakuten order polling (scheduled) - Shopify order ingestion (webhook + polling redundancy, HMAC verified) - SKU-level master inventory decrement (event-sourced append-only log) - Admin UI: SKU mapping, inventory view, manual adjustment (3–4 screens) - Daily export to BigQuery Out of scope: inventory write-back to channels. Rakuten ↔ Shopify sync continues to be handled separately. ----------------------------------------- PHASE 1-B — Full: Two-way Sync with Write-back ----------------------------------------- Everything in 1-A, plus automated inventory push to both channels so they stay in sync. Additional scope: - Inventory push to Rakuten and Shopify (idempotent, retry, rate-limit handling) - Daily full reconciliation (re-push all SKUs) - Sync error queue UI + manual retry - Missing-mapping alerts - Slack notifications for critical errors - Admin UI expanded to 5–6 screens Goal: fully automated inventory sync + data foundation. This is the "CrossMall replacement" scope. ----------------------------------------- PHASE 2 — Visualization + Stockout Prediction Dashboards for SKU sales/inventory trends, low-stock alerts, stockout date prediction, lead-time-aware reorder alerts, stocktake and goods receipt, supplier master management. PHASE 3 — Recommended Orders + AI PO Draft Generation Demand forecasting, recommended order quantity (MOQ/lead-time/safety stock aware), per-factory PO draft auto-generation (PDF+CSV, EN/JA), forecast accuracy dashboard. Final approval and sending remain human-operated. ----------------------------------------- ■ When Applying, Please Include 1. Specific Rakuten RMS API + Shopify Admin API project examples 2. Effort + budget estimates for Phase 1-A, 1-B, 2, and 3 (Phase 3 requires ML experience; otherwise quote 1-2 only) 3. Earliest start date and weekly availability 4. Your experience with AI coding tools 5. Portfolio / GitHub Phase 1-only engagement is welcome. We may add Phase 2/3 based on results. ■ Expected Tech Stack Python 3.11+ / FastAPI, PostgreSQL (Cloud SQL), Cloud Run + Cloud Tasks + Secret Manager, BigQuery (already in use), Retool or FastAPI + Jinja2 + Tailwind for admin UI, Rakuten RMS API + Shopify Admin GraphQL API. Stack proposals welcome. ■ Required Phase 1-B: Hands-on Rakuten RMS API experience (order fetch + inventory update), Shopify Admin API experience (GraphQL preferred), PostgreSQL (transactions, UNIQUE constraints, row-level locking), solid understanding of idempotency/retry/consistency, GCP experience. Phase 1-A only: Python/FastAPI experience, either Shopify webhook or Rakuten order API experience, PostgreSQL basics, GCP experience. ■ AI Coding Tools Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot are welcome. However, inventory integrity logic must be human-reviewed, test coverage must be human-verified, and actual Rakuten/Shopify API behavior must be verified against real environments. ■ Working Setup Client: product decisions, API credentials, test data. Developer: design refinement, implementation, tests, documentation. Private GitHub repo. Hourly or monthly retainer preferred. Note: Rakuten RMS API documentation is Japanese-only with legacy SOAP/REST quirks. Please be upfront if you don't have direct Rakuten experience — it's essential for Phase 1-B. Questions-only inquiries welcome.
title: Rakuten × Shopify Inventory → Demand Forecasting → AI PO Generation — Phased Bid Request

description: About the role We're a direct-to-consumer health brand running high-volume paid traffic to conversion-focused content pages — advertorials, listicles, comparison pages, quiz funnels, and sales pages. Our growth depends on how fast and how cleanly we can ship new pages and iterate on live ones. We're looking for a developer who lives inside Funnelish, Replo, and Shopify and can turn briefs into shipped pages without hand-holding. What you'll actually do Build advertorial pages, listicles, and editorial-style landing pages (primarily Replo on Shopify, sometimes Funnelish) Ship new sales pages, PDPs, and quiz funnels from design briefs or rough references Iterate fast on live pages — section swaps, layout fixes, mobile polish, conversion tweaks Build reusable custom HTML/CSS/JS components that work cleanly inside Replo and Shopify (countdown timers, bundle selectors, trust bars, low-stock alerts, etc.) Small Shopify backend work when needed: theme edits, Flows, notification templates This is execution, not greenfield design. You're building against briefs and references — cleanly, quickly, and with minimal back-and-forth. Who we're looking for You have real, shipped work in Funnelish, Replo, and Shopify. Revenue pages, not demos. You can show us live URLs. You're genuinely fast. A section rewrite is hours, not days. A full advertorial build is days, not weeks. You solve your own problems. When something breaks in Replo or a Shopify theme throws a conflict, you don't escalate — you debug, fix, and ship. You communicate like an adult. Short, direct messages. When something's blocked, we hear about it the same day. You take pride in clean, mobile-first work. Pages don't break when a Liquid variable is missing. Layouts don't collapse below 400px. Non-negotiables on availability We operate in European hours (DACH market). We need someone who responds within a few hours during the working day and same-day always. If our baseline rhythm for messages is 24+ hours, this won't work. We're not asking for 24/7 — we're asking for reliability during normal working hours. What fast execution means here Standard advertorial page build: 2–4 days Section or component rewrite: same day or next day Urgent fixes on live pages: within hours A new sales page from brief to launch: under a week for most projects If those numbers feel aggressive to you, we're probably not a fit. What you get Steady recurring work — we're building and iterating constantly, not one-off gigs Briefs with clear direction (we know what we want and how to communicate it) Competitive rates for the right person, paid on time every time A long-term working relationship if it clicks Not a fit if You've dabbled in Replo or Funnelish but haven't shipped production work on them You need detailed spec documents for every task You routinely go quiet for 24+ hours You're managing five other clients and we'd be number six To apply Send us: 2–3 live page links you built on Replo, Funnelish, or Shopify (health/supplement/DTC a bonus) Your typical turnaround for a standard advertorial-style page Your hourly or project rate Your working hours and time zone
title: Funnelish / Replo / Shopify Developer — Fast Execution, Long-Term Work (Health DTC)

description: TITLE: Shopify Horizon theme setup — 5 digital PDFs via Digital Downloads app — 6hr turnaround — clinical fertility brand BUDGET: $100 fixed · 6-hour delivery Not a dropshipping store. Not a migration. Not print-on-demand. This is a clinical digital-product store for a Ph.D.-authored fertility brand. Looking for Top Rated Shopify developers only. Rising Talent, $10/hr generalists, and anyone who leads with "flexible budget?" will not be considered.The project:Skeleton Shopify store setup on the Horizon theme to sell 5 digital PDF products via Shopify's native Digital Downloads app (free, not SendOwl, not Sky Pilot, not a paid alternative — Digital Downloads only).The brand is already established: logo, color palette, product PDFs, and copy are done. I am not asking you to design the brand. I am asking you to install the theme, apply my brand colors, wire up the products, connect the domain, and confirm end-to-end checkout works.BRAND COLOR PALETTE — apply exactly in Horizon theme settings: Primary / accent / buttons / CTAs: hot pink, hex code FF 3E AF (brand signature color) Primary text and headings: pure black Body text: dark charcoal gray Background: pure white Secondary bars / footer: black with white text and hot pink accents Borders and muted elements: light neutral gray All buttons, links, CTAs, sale badges, and interactive states must use the hot pink (hex FF 3E AF). No other pinks, no gradients, no lifestyle-pastel variants. The brand is clinical, not cute.Typography: Horizon defaults are fine (clean sans-serif). Do not install custom fonts unless specifically asked.Exact deliverables — 6-hour window: Install and activate Horizon theme Apply brand color palette above in Horizon theme editor (Theme settings → Colors) Connect custom domain (DNS managed via Cloudflare — domain details sent post-hire) Connect Stripe and PayPal as payment processors Install Digital Downloads app (free, native Shopify) Upload 5 digital PDF products (I will supply the PDFs, titles, descriptions, prices, cover images) Create one collection: "Fertility" — ladder order: RI Audit, Supplements, Nutrition, Uterine, Roadmap Configure checkout so buyers receive automated PDF delivery after purchase Test end-to-end: test purchase of free lead magnet, test purchase of paid product, verify download links work Basic homepage with hero section and featured collection (no custom design — Horizon defaults using my palette are fine) Mobile responsive check What I am NOT asking for: Custom theme development Custom CSS beyond Horizon's built-in color/style settings Logo or brand design (logo will be supplied) Copywriting Email marketing setup (already configured separately) Ongoing retainer Brand context (so you don't propose the wrong thing):The brand is fertility and reproductive health. The buyer is a woman spending real money on IVF who judges credibility in 3 seconds. The aesthetic is clinical, minimal, Ph.D.-authored — not lifestyle, not wellness-influencer, not dropshipping. The hot pink is intentional brand signature, used as a bold accent against black and white, not softened or diluted. Horizon's default layout is a good fit; use my palette, do not add decorative elements.To be considered, your proposal must include all three: Links to 2 Shopify stores you have built that sell digital products (not physical goods, not POD, not dropshipping). If you do not have 2 digital-product store examples, do not apply. Your specific Horizon theme experience — which Horizon features you have worked with, any known gotchas. Confirmation you can start within 1 hour of hire and deliver within 6 hours. Screening rules — please read before applying: Do not pitch SendOwl, Sky Pilot, FetchApp, or any paid digital delivery app. The spec is Digital Downloads (native, free). If you recommend an alternative, I will assume you did not read this post. Do not ask "is the budget flexible?" or offer a "priority turnaround upgrade." The budget is fixed at $100. The 6-hour turnaround is the spec, not an upsell. Do not pitch Shopify migrations, theme development, SEO services, CRO, or ongoing management. Those are not this job. Do not send generic templated proposals. I will read every proposal and generic ones get ignored. First line of your proposal should be the Shopify store URL of one digital-product store you have built. If your first line is "Hi, I hope this message finds you well" or any variation, I will not read the rest.Payment: $100 fixed-price milestone, released on confirmed working end-to-end test purchase. No hourly, no partial milestones, no scope expansion.SKILLS TAGS: Shopify · Shopify Horizon · Digital Downloads · Stripe · PayPal · Cloudflare · digital products
title: Shopify Horizon theme setup — 5 digital PDFs — Digital Downloads app — fertility/health brand

description: Hey, I’m building a new brand called NexClean — we’re launching eco-friendly detergent sheets designed for modern homes. I’m looking for someone who truly understands clean, minimal design (no clutter, no template feel), premium brand execution (spacing, typography, visual balance), and conversion-focused thinking (not just “good-looking” design). The scope includes building a Shopify store using a theme like “Sense” (or similar), customizing it properly (not just drag-and-drop sections), and translating my mockup into a polished, responsive website. I’ll provide the logo, final color palette, product images, reference website and mockup, and clear structure so you won’t have to guess. From your side, I expect strong attention to detail (spacing, alignment, consistency), solid Shopify experience, the ability to think from a customer’s perspective, and clear, fast communication. The timeline is around 5–8 days, and the budget is open depending on experience — I’m not looking for the cheapest option, but the right fit. Create a clean, high-end layout I like a simple, clean, premium look similar to Apple’s website Not cluttered Easy to browse Mobile-friendly Professional homepage, collection pages, and product pages to add online shopping later Add a quote cart / request-a-quote feature I want customers to be able to browse products and add items to a cart Instead of checking out and paying online right away, I want them to submit the cart to me for pricing I want to receive their selected items, quantities, color/options, business information, and shipping address This allows me to provide the best price, volume discount, package deal, or freight/shipping quote Quote request should collect Customer name Business name Phone number Email address Shipping address Products selected Product colors/options/variants Quantity for each item Notes or special requests Build the website for future expansion Today I sell one type of product In the future, I may add more product categories, such SCOPE OF WORK: STEP 1 — Store Structure & Theme Shopify account configured (I provide login access) Shopify Payments activated with business credentials Dawn theme installed and configured Three brand collections with correct URL handles: /collections/kke, /collections/bnbb, /collections/and-inc Navigation menu, policy pages, and all store settings per my specification Custom domain connected STEP 2 — POD Connections & Product Catalog Printify and Printful installed and connected to Shopify 15 launch products created (5 per brand) — I provide exact specs, you build Products tagged correctly, SKU convention applied, shipping profile configured Printful branding options configured (inside label + pack-in card) STEP 3 — Shipping, Tax & Store Settings Shipping zones configured for US domestic NY state tax settings confirmed All policy pages live: Privacy, Terms, Refund, Shipping Email notification templates customized per brand STEP 4 — Klaviyo Email Flows Klaviyo installed and authenticated Three brand subscriber lists created Signup popup forms installed per brand collection Welcome Series, Abandoned Cart, Post-Purchase, and Win-Back flows installed All email copy provided by me — you install the structure STEP 5 — Google & Meta Technical Setup Google Merchant Center created and product feed synced GA4 conversion tracking verified Meta Pixel verified with all 5 events firing Custom Audiences built in Meta Ads Manager STEP 6 — SEO Technical Foundation Google Search Console verified, sitemap submitted Product images compressed to WebP SEO app installed, schema markup verified Three Shopify blogs created (one per brand) WHAT I PROVIDE: Complete build guide PDFs for every step Exact product list with specs, supplier names, and pricing All email copy — you install the flows, I write the words All brand assets: logos, color codes, design files Direct availability via Upwork messaging throughout NDA REQUIRED before any project materials are shared. Covers confidentiality, 24-month non-compete, and work-for-hire ownership of all deliverables by MYLINEAGE LLC. Do NOT send a generic proposal. Answer these questions or I will not respond: Q1 — Have you built a multi-brand POD store on Shopify before? Describe the collection structure. Q2 — Have you built Klaviyo automated flows — Welcome Series, Abandoned Cart, Post-Purchase? Describe one. Q3 — Have you configured Google Merchant Center and resolved feed disapprovals? What issues did you fix? Q4 — Are you willing to sign an NDA with a 24-month non-compete and work-for-hire clause? Yes or No.

description: # Shopify Tracking Specialist Needed — Server-Side GTM, Meta CAPI, GA4 & Google Ads Audit ## About the project We run a Shopify store (Moonglow Australia — personalized moon phase jewelry) and need a specialist to upgrade our tracking infrastructure end-to-end. Our current setup is functional but basic: native Shopify-Meta CAPI, multiple duplicate Google tags flagged by Tag Assistant, and no server-side GTM. We want this rebuilt properly, once, by someone who knows what they're doing. ## What we currently have - Shopify store with Facebook & Instagram app (Maximum data sharing — native CAPI active) - Meta Pixel installed and firing (single pixel, confirmed via Meta Pixel Helper) - Google Ads + Merchant Center connected and active (PMAX running) - GA4 receiving traffic - **Multiple duplicate Google tags flagged in Tag Assistant** — Shopify is showing a yellow warning to clean these up - No server-side GTM container yet ## Scope of work **Server-side infrastructure** - Set up server-side Google Tag Manager via Stape - Configure custom tracking subdomain (e.g., data.moonglow.com.au) for first-party cookies - Web GTM container properly linked to the server container **Meta** - Implement Meta Conversions API via server-side GTM - Replace or properly supplement the existing Shopify-native CAPI - Event deduplication between browser Pixel and CAPI using event_id - Advanced Matching with hashed customer parameters (email, phone, fbp, fbc, external_id, IP, UA) - All standard ecommerce events: PageView, ViewContent, AddToCart, InitiateCheckout, AddPaymentInfo, Purchase - Target Event Match Quality of 8+/10 - Verify in Events Manager that events show "Browser and Server" with proper deduplication **Google** - Audit and remove the duplicate Google tags currently flagged in Shopify - Implement Google Ads Enhanced Conversions server-side - Audit and properly configure GA4 events (purchase, add_to_cart, begin_checkout, view_item, etc.) with full ecommerce parameters - GA4 measurement protocol via server-side for ad-blocker resilience - Conversion linker properly configured ## Deliverables - Fully working server-side tracking setup as described above - Written documentation of what's been configured (containers, triggers, variables, tags) - Loom walkthrough of the GTM web + server containers - Verification screenshots: - Meta Events Manager (showing browser + server, deduplication, EMQ score) - Google Tag Assistant (confirming no duplicates) - GA4 DebugView (events firing with parameters) - Google Ads conversion diagnostics - 30 days of post-launch support for any issues that come up ## What to include in your proposal 1. Brief description of your experience with **Shopify + Stape specifically** (not just generic GTM) 2. One example of a previous project: ideally showing before/after Event Match Quality scores or attribution improvements 3. Your approach to event deduplication between browser and server 4. Estimated timeline 5. Fixed-price quote for the full scope above ## What we're looking for - Demonstrated Shopify + Stape experience — please don't apply if your experience is only with WooCommerce or custom sites - You proactively mention deduplication, event_id matching, and first-party cookies in your proposal - Clean communication, fixed-price, single milestone via Upwork escrow ## Budget No fixed budget set. Please quote based on the full scope and your approach. We're evaluating proposals on quality and fit, not lowest price — so quote what the work is worth to do properly. Payment: single milestone via Upwork escrow. We fund the full amount up-front and release on verification. ## Timeline Looking to start within 1–2 weeks. Project should take 1–2 weeks of elapsed time to complete.
title: Shopify Tracking Specialist Needed — Server-Side GTM, Meta CAPI, GA4 & Google Ads Audit

description: Job Title: Inventory & Stock Update Manager (CSV, API, Web Scraping Automation) Description: We are an ecommerce retailer (department store / marketplace model) looking for an experienced Inventory & Stock Update Manager to handle ongoing product stock updates across multiple suppliers. This role combines data management, automation, and scraping, and is ideal for someone who has worked with ecommerce inventory systems at scale. 🔧 Key Responsibilities Manage and maintain accurate stock levels across our product catalogue Process and upload supplier CSV stock files regularly Set up and maintain automated stock updates via: Supplier APIs Scheduled CSV imports Web scraping (where APIs are not available) Build systems to track and compare stock changes over time Clean and standardise supplier data (SKU mapping, formatting, etc.) Identify and flag inconsistencies (out-of-stock, discontinued items, etc.) Work with our Shopify-based system and internal workflows 🚀 Initial Scope (Important) We will begin with ~8 suppliers, all providing CSV stock files. Phase 1: Review and standardise all supplier CSV formats Set up a reliable process for regular CSV-based stock updates Ensure accurate SKU matching and inventory syncing Phase 2: Identify suppliers suitable for automation Build scripts or integrations to: Automate CSV ingestion Connect via API (where available) Implement scraping solutions for suppliers without feeds Phase 3 (Ongoing): Maintain and optimise all stock update systems Add new suppliers over time Improve automation and reduce manual work ⚙️ Technical Requirements Strong experience with: CSV data handling and bulk uploads APIs (REST, JSON) Web scraping tools (Python, Scrapy, BeautifulSoup, Selenium, etc.) Experience with automation tools (cron jobs, Zapier, Make, or custom scripts) Ability to manage large datasets efficiently Understanding of SKU mapping and inventory logic Experience with Shopify or ecommerce platforms is highly preferred 🧠 Ideal Candidate Has worked with B2B supplier data feeds Understands inventory syncing challenges Can build reliable, scalable automation systems Detail-oriented and proactive in fixing data issues Communicates clearly and can suggest improvements 📦 Scope This is an ongoing role, not a one-off project. You will start with CSV-based workflows and progressively move toward automation and integrations. 💬 To Apply Please include: Examples of similar inventory / scraping / automation work Tools and tech stack you typically use How you would approach syncing stock from multiple suppliers Your availability We’re looking for someone long-term who can become a key part of our operations.
title: Inventory & Stock Update Manager for Shopify Site

description: Shopify Pricing Specialist (Quick Audit + Fix – 15,000 Products) Description: We’re looking for someone to do a fast, practical pricing update across our Shopify store ahead of a relaunch. This is not a long strategy project — we want a quick audit and sensible improvements we can implement immediately. 🎯 Scope Review current pricing across products and suppliers Identify obvious issues: Inconsistent margins across suppliers Underpriced / overpriced items Poor rounding or price presentation (important for Google Shopping) Apply or recommend simple pricing rules (by supplier or category) Do a light competitor sanity check (not deep analysis) Ensure pricing is suitable for Google Merchant / Shopping compatibility ⚡ Goal Get us to a clean, logical pricing baseline quickly ahead of relaunch. We are not trying to be the cheapest — we want sensible, competitive pricing while protecting margin. More advanced competitive pricing and optimisation can come later — this is about getting the fundamentals right fast. 🧠 What We’re Looking For Experience with Shopify ecommerce pricing at scale Ability to handle large catalogues using rules, not manual edits Practical, no-nonsense approach (not overly theoretical) Comfortable working with messy supplier data Understanding of how pricing impacts Google Shopping performance ⏱️ Timeline Ideally completed within a few days. 📩 To Apply Please include: A brief example of a pricing audit or bulk pricing update you’ve done How you’d approach this dataset (15k SKUs, multi-supplier) in a short timeframe Your availability Engagement: Short-term project (potential follow-up work) Location: Remote

description: Project Overview I operate an established Shopify store selling custom wood shelving. Products are custom-sized to order with pricing that varies across 6 configuration dimensions. I have a complete, clean pricing database of 68,640 exact price points and fully documented business rules. I am looking for an experienced Shopify developer to build a unified order form section that is embedded across all product pages, with real-time pricing driven by a secure server-side database lookup. This is a well-defined, well-documented project. The data work is done. The business rules are documented. Mockups of the desired order form are included. What is needed is precise technical implementation. What Needs to Be Built 1. Unified Order Form Section A single Shopify Online Store 2.0 app block embedded across all product pages. The section reads the current page’s material type as the default selection but exposes all 10 material types so the customer can switch without leaving the page. One codebase, one API connection, one validation layer — no duplication across product pages. 2. Product Configurator UI The order form presents the following options in a clean logical sequence: • Material: dropdown defaulting to current page material, all 10 selectable • Finish: Finished or Unfinished — availability depends on material (see rules below) • Product Type: Slab (no hardware) or Shelf with Hardware • Thickness: 0.5”, 0.75”, 1”, 1.5” (coming soon — slab only), 1.75”, 2”, 2.1–4”, 4.1–6” (slab only) • Width: two coordinated dropdowns — whole inches 9–96 plus 1/16th fraction • Depth: two coordinated dropdowns — whole inches plus 1/16th fraction, maps to one of 6 pricing ranges 3. Real-Time Dynamic Pricing As the customer changes any option the displayed price updates instantly. Pricing is a direct server-side database lookup — no formulas, no client-side exposure, no approximations. Prices display as whole numbers. The price the customer sees is exactly what they pay at checkout. 4. Availability and Validation Rules Finish availability by material: Materials Finish Options Notes 1, 2, 3 Finished and Unfinished Both available 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9 Finished only No unfinished option 10 Unfinished only No finished option Shelf with hardware availability: Depth Width Range Shelf with Hardware? 3–6" 9–96" Yes 6.1–12" 9–96" Yes 12.1–16" 9–96" Yes 16.1–20" Any No — slab only 20.1–24" Any No — slab only 24.1–28" Any No — slab only Depth pricing ranges: Customer Enters Depth Used for Pricing 3" to 6" 3–6 6.1" to 12" 6.1–12 12.1" to 16" 12.1–16 16.1" to 20" 16.1–20 20.1" to 24" 20.1–24 24.1" to 28" 24.1–28 Dimension Input Rules Width Two coordinated dropdowns — primary whole inches (9–96) and secondary fractional inches in 1/16th inch increments (1/16 through 15/16). Fraction rule: any fraction present rounds UP to the next whole inch for pricing. Example: 30 and 1/8" looks up the price for width 31". Ceiling rule: if 96" is selected, the fraction dropdown must be disabled — no fraction permitted at maximum width. Depth Two coordinated dropdowns — whole inches plus 1/16th fraction — within the selected pricing range. Fraction rule: any fraction rounds UP to next whole inch then maps to the appropriate pricing range. Example: 6 and 1/16" rounds up to 7", maps to 6.1–12 range. Ceiling rule: 28" is the maximum depth. If 28" is selected the fraction dropdown must be disabled. Thickness Option Type Pricing Fraction Input 0.5", 0.75", 1", 1.5" Coming soon Placeholder price — hide or mark coming soon in UI Not applicable until live 1.75" Fixed Always prices as 1.75" Not applicable 2" Fixed Always prices as 2" Not applicable 2.1–4" Range All values price as "2.1-4" regardless of exact measurement Shown for order record. Disabled at 4" ceiling. 4.1–6" Range All values price as "4.1-6" regardless of exact measurement Shown for order record. Disabled at 6" ceiling. Ceiling validation summary: Dimension Maximum Rule Width 96" Fraction dropdown disabled at 96" Depth 28" Fraction dropdown disabled at 28" Thickness 2.1–4" range 4" Fraction dropdown disabled at 4" Thickness 4.1–6" range 6" Fraction dropdown disabled at 6" General rule: fractions entered by the customer are recorded on the order for manufacturing purposes but do not affect pricing beyond the round-up rule above. Data Provided to Developer The following files will be provided upon engagement: • slab_pricing_complete.csv — 68,640 price records with columns: material, finish, product_type, hardware, thickness, depth, width_inches, price, placeholder • slab_pricing_schema.sql — ready-to-run SQL with CREATE TABLE statement, lookup index, and all INSERT statements • Two order form mockup PDFs showing the proposed UI layout including open dropdown states Price lookup query: SELECT price FROM slab_pricing WHERE material = ? AND finish = ? AND product_type = ? AND hardware = ? AND thickness = ? AND depth = ? AND width_inches = ? LIMIT 1; The placeholder column flags the four coming soon thickness options (0.5", 0.75", 1", 1.5") with placeholder = yes and price = $1,967. These should be hidden or marked coming soon until real pricing is available. The architecture must support a simple CSV import to activate them when ready. Technical Environment • Platform: Shopify (existing live store, Online Store 2.0) • Architecture: OS 2.0 app block embedded across all product pages • Database: MySQL — schema and complete data provided and ready to import • API: lightweight server-side endpoint — Node.js, Python, or Cloudflare Workers all acceptable • Pricing data must not be exposed client-side Deliverables 1. Unified order form section (OS 2.0 app block) embedded across all product pages, defaulting to current page material, with all 6 configuration options correctly presented and validated 2. Real-time price display updating correctly as options change, driven by server-side database lookup 3. All availability and validation rules enforced — options hidden or disabled per the rules documented above 4. Correct price passed through to cart and checkout — not a frontend estimate but the actual looked-up value 5. Password-protected CSV import tool allowing pricing to be updated by uploading a new CSV file — complete database replacement in one operation, no developer involvement required for routine price updates 6. Brief implementation documentation for future reference and maintenance What I Am Looking For • Demonstrated experience with Shopify custom product configurators and database-driven dynamic pricing — please include a live example • Understanding of OS 2.0 app block architecture • Ability to build and connect a server-side MySQL pricing API to a Shopify storefront • Getting it right the first time matters more than speed • Clear communication throughout To apply, please describe a relevant project you have completed involving custom product configuration with database-driven dynamic pricing on Shopify. A live example link is strongly preferred. If you have built a configurator where price updates in real time based on customer dimension inputs, that is exactly the kind of experience this project requires. Full pricing data, SQL schema, and order form mockups provided upon engagement.
title: Shopify Custom panel Configurator with Database-Driven Dynamic Pricing

description: --- We are a luxury resort and beachwear brand, founded in 2003. We are a pure e-commerce business. We are migrating our current Shopify site from the Atlantic theme (with GemPages page builder) to a new Shopify Horizon theme build. Our product pages are currently built entirely in GemPages, which is causing severe mobile performance problems and costing us conversions. We need a clean, native Liquid build on Horizon with zero page builder dependencies. *Scope of work:* - Full site build on Shopify Horizon theme - Native Shopify Liquid only — no page builders, no GemPages, no apps that replace Liquid templates - Pages: homepage, all product pages, collection pages, navigation, cart - Content migration (all products, copy, and images are already in Shopify — no new content creation required) - App integrations: Klaviyo, Stamped.io (reviews), BOGOS (gift with purchase), shipping settings - Mobile-first throughout — paid traffic is our primary channel, mobile performance is the #1 priority *What we are NOT looking for:* - Copywriting, photography, or branding work - Page builder customisation — if you rely on GemPages, Shogun, PageFly or similar, this project is not for you - Shopify Plus (we are on standard Shopify) *How we work — important, please read:* All development happens in a duplicate/development theme. Nothing touches the live site until it is fully approved. At each milestone there are two sign-off requirements: 1. Technical QA — Liquid code review by our CMO, checking for native sections, zero page builder dependencies, clean template structure 2. Visual approval — founder sign-off on staging Payment at each milestone releases only when both sign-offs are complete. *Milestones:* 1. Theme setup + homepage 2. Product pages (this is the core problem we are solving — priority) 3. Collection pages + navigation 4. Full site QA and launch *Communication:* WhatsApp. We expect regular progress updates and reasonable response times during your working hours. *To apply — read this carefully:* Before you write your proposal, open littlejoewoman.com on your mobile phone and tell us in the first line of your proposal what you observe about the technical setup of the product pages. Do not Google this. Open the site, look at it, and tell us what you see. This is the first screening step and we read every proposal. Your proposal must include: - Your response to the mobile site test above (first line — no exceptions) - Links to 2–3 live Shopify Horizon theme sites you have built (not Atlantic, not other themes — Horizon specifically) - Your fixed project fee for the full scope - Your estimated timeline from start to launch - Where you are based *We are prioritising Philippines-based developers for this project.* ---
title: Shopify Developer — Horizon Theme Build for Luxury Fashion Brand (Philippines preferred)

description: Job Title: Senior Creative Strategist — Facebook/Meta Ads (E-commerce) Job Type: Full-time / Part-time Ideally full time About the Role We're looking for an experienced creative strategist who makes ads that scale profitably on Meta. Not boosted posts. Not organic content. Direct response ads that spend real money and generate sales. You'll research customers, develop angles and hooks, write scripts and briefs, and build creative testing roadmaps. You are NOT editing videos or running the ad account — you're the strategist behind winning ads. If you want to edit/ design your own ads that is also fine. You need to be able to manage teams, outreach to UGC creators to get consistent inflow of content. If you haven't managed a brand doing over at least $10k a day please don't apply We have 2 video editors and 1 Graphic designer for static ads. They are both amazing. This role requires proven experience. If you've never worked on ads spending $1000+/day, please do not apply. This role has HUGE winning ad bonuses. Every winning creative you make, You will get a % of ad spend. --- Requirements - Minimum 2 years experience in creative strategy for paid Meta ads - Have worked on e-commerce brands (Shopify, DTC) — not lead gen, not local business - Can show specific ads you strategized and their performance (spend, ROAS, results) - Strong understanding of hooks, angles, offers, and why ads convert - Able to write creative briefs that UGC creators and editors can execute - Excellent written English --- Big Plus - Helped scale a brand from low spend to $50K-$100K+ per month - BIGGG + if you have worked with any first to market product. Or brands with very little direct competitors - Experience working with a performance marketing agency - Understand creative testing frameworks (concept testing, iterations, 3-2-2 method) - Familiar with ad spy tools (Foreplay, AdSpy, Atria Meta Ad Library) _ familiar with AI ads, we can teach as well, But ideally very experienced with AI UGC ads --- **Do NOT Apply If:** - Your only experience is organic social media or content creation - You've never worked on ads spending more than $500/day - You cannot provide examples of ads you personally strategized - You're looking for a "virtual assistant" role — this is specialist work --- To Apply Send a message with: 1. Short intro — your experience with paid ads creative strategy 2. 2-3 examples of ads you strategized (screenshots, links, or videos) with results 3. Full Portfolio/ Case studies 4. Your expected monthly salary in PHP or USD No examples = no interview. I respect your time, please respect mine. NO AGENCIES - I am building an in house team. Pay is negotiable, will be monthly salary in ranges from $2000-4000 based on your experience and capabilities

description: Role Overview • Own Sevenesy’s email marketing channel end-to-end, with a clear focus on retention, repeat purchases, and long-term customer value. • Work closely with the marketing team to develop and execute high-performing flows, campaigns, and segmentation strategies in Klaviyo. • Help turn email into a consistent revenue-driving channel that strengthens the customer experience and supports overall brand growth. Responsibilities • Build, manage, and continuously optimize all core Klaviyo flows, including welcome, abandoned cart, post-purchase, browse abandonment, win-back, VIP, and other lifecycle automations. • Plan and execute weekly and monthly campaign calendars aligned with product launches, promotions, seasonal moments, and brand-led “just because” sends. • Write high-converting email copy that matches the brand voice and customer psychology, while also designing clean, on-brand email creatives and templates in Figma or a similar tool for consistent use in Klaviyo. • Run A/B tests across subject lines, preview text, send times, copy, offers, layouts, and CTAs to improve performance over time. • Segment the email list based on purchase behavior, engagement level, customer lifecycle stage, and other relevant data points to deliver more personalized messaging. • Track and report on key performance metrics such as open rate, click-through rate, revenue per recipient, unsubscribe rate, placed order rate, and flow/campaign performance on a weekly basis. • Maintain strong list hygiene, suppression logic, and deliverability best practices to protect sender reputation and improve inbox placement. • Collaborate with the wider marketing team to ensure email supports broader brand, product, and promotional initiatives. Requirements • Several years of hands-on experience with Klaviyo, ideally within a DTC e-commerce brand. • Strong copywriting skills, with a real understanding of customer psychology, conversion principles, and how messaging should change depending on the audience, offer, and stage of the customer journey. • Good visual taste and layout sense, with the ability to create polished, on-brand email designs that balance aesthetics with conversion. • Deep understanding of email segmentation, flow logic, lifecycle marketing, and retention strategy. • Data-driven mindset, with confidence in analyzing performance, pulling insights from reports, and making decisions based on results rather than guesswork. • Experience working with Shopify and customer purchase data is a strong plus. • Highly organized and self-directed, able to manage a full campaign and flow calendar with minimal supervision while staying ahead of key seasonal and promotional deadlines.
title: Email Marketer & Copywriter | Klaviyo | DTC Jewelry Brand
