description: Hey there 👋 First, let me introduce myself. I'm the founder of BitBrahma — a small tech brand from Bengaluru that sells refurbished laptops and mobile parts. The name means "Creator of Computing" and honestly, right now the only thing I haven't been able to create is... my own website. 😅 I have the products. I have the brand. I have the logo. I even have the domain — bitbrahma.com — just sitting there, waiting, judging me silently every day. Today that changes. I want to launch. --- 🧡 What I'm Building A complete Shopify store for BitBrahma — selling refurbished laptops and mobile parts & accessories to everyday Indian buyers who want good tech without spending a fortune. This isn't a side project. This is my livelihood. So I need someone who actually cares, not just someone who copy-pastes a theme and disappears. --- 📋 What Needs to Get Done (All of It) 1. Shopify store setup on bitbrahma.com — Dawn free theme, brand colors (saffron #ff6b00, dark #1a0a00), BitBrahma logo uploaded, homepage with hero banner, 6 collection blocks, trust badges, WhatsApp in footer 2. 6 collections with proper navigation — Refurbished Laptops, Screens, Batteries, Cables & Chargers, Back Covers, Earphones & Accessories — Laptop section needs filters by brand, RAM, and processor 3. 65 products uploaded from my Excel sheet — 15 refurbished laptops (specs: processor, RAM, storage, grade, warranty) — 50 mobile parts (10 per category, tagged by phone brand & model) — I'll provide the data sheet and images. You just bring the skill. 4. Razorpay + COD payments — Razorpay integration (UPI, cards, net banking, wallets) + Cash on Delivery — Please test a dummy transaction before telling me it's done 🙏 5. Shipping setup — Free shipping above ₹999, flat ₹99 below (mobile parts) — Flat ₹199 for laptops — Shiprocket/Delhivery plugin if possible 6. WhatsApp chat button + Judge.me reviews app (both free) 7. All the important pages — About Us, Contact, Shipping Policy, Returns Policy, Privacy Policy, T&C — I'll write the content. You create and link the pages. 8. Mobile tested and fast — Works perfectly on Android and iPhone — Pages load under 3 seconds — Google PageSpeed screenshot before handover (don't be shy if it's not perfect, just show me) 9. Full handover — All logins and credentials — A short Loom video (10 mins) showing me how to add a product, edit a price, and process an order — 15 days of bug-fix support after delivery (not "ghost and pray" support — actual support 😄) --- 💰 Budget & Payment I'll be honest — ₹7,000 is what I have. It's fixed. I'm not hiding a secret budget behind this number. But I'll pay in 3 fair milestones so you're never working without security: • Milestone 1 — ₹2,500: Store live on bitbrahma.com, theme and homepage done • Milestone 2 — ₹2,500: All 65 products uploaded, payments working • Milestone 3 — ₹2,000: Training video, full handover, mobile tested ✅ Deadline: 10 days. I'll be available on WhatsApp daily. I respond fast. I won't ghost you. --- 🤝 Who I'm Looking For Honestly? Someone who has done this before and doesn't need me to explain what a collection is. Someone who takes a little pride in their work. Someone who treats my ₹7,000 like it matters — because to me, it really does. Bonus points if you: ✅ Have built at least one live Shopify store (please share the link) ✅ Have integrated Razorpay before ✅ Are based in India and understand what Indian buyers actually want ✅ Can finish in 10 days --- 📝 Before You Apply — 3 Quick Questions I'll read every proposal. But please answer these 3 things or I genuinely won't know how to evaluate you: 1. Share a live Shopify store you've built 2. Have you done Razorpay integration before? Yes / No 3. Can you deliver everything in 10 days for ₹7,000? If you've read this far — thank you. That already tells me you're someone who pays attention. That's exactly who I want building BitBrahma's first home on the internet. Let's make something good together. 🚀 — BitBrahma Founder, Bengaluru
title: Help a Small Indian Tech Brand Launch Their First Shopify Store 🙏 (₹7,000 | 10 Days | Full Store)

description: Hey 👋 I’m the founder of **BitBrahma**, a Bengaluru-based tech brand launching an online store for **refurbished laptops and mobile parts/accessories**. My Shopify store will be ready in ~10 days. I’m now looking for a **performance-focused marketing freelancer** who can help me launch and test ads properly — not just make creatives, but actually try to get results. --- ## 🧡 What I Need I need someone who can handle **basic end-to-end ad execution** for launch: ### 1. Product Image Sourcing & Cleanup * Collect product images from **copyright-safe / free / supplier sources** * Clean them for ecommerce use (white background, sharp, consistent) * Maintain a **uniform style across products** --- ### 2. Ad Creative Creation * Create **simple, clean ad creatives** (no overdesign) * Formats: * Static image ads * Basic video/slideshow ads (optional but preferred) 👉 Focus: trust, value-for-money, real product feel 👉 NOT flashy “fake offer” ads --- ### 3. Ad Setup & Launch * Platforms: * Meta Ads (Facebook + Instagram) — mandatory * Google Ads — optional bonus * Setup: * Campaign structure * Basic targeting (India-focused) * Budget control (small testing budget) --- ### 4. Testing & Optimization (Basic) * Run ads for initial testing phase * Monitor performance * Suggest: * What’s working * What’s not * What to improve --- ## 🎯 Goal (Be Honest) I’m NOT expecting magic ROI in ₹5K. What I want: * A **clean, proper ad setup** * Initial **testing data** * Direction on what to scale next --- ## 💰 Budget ₹5,000 (fixed) This is for: * Setup + creatives + initial testing phase 👉 Ad spend will be separate (I will fund it) --- ## ⏱ Timeline * Start: After store launch (~10 days from now) * Initial work: 5–7 days --- ## 🚫 Please Don’t Apply If * You only design creatives but don’t understand ads * You promise “guaranteed sales” (instant rejection) * You use copied or copyrighted images blindly * You don’t explain your strategy --- ## ✅ Ideal Candidate * Has run **Meta Ads for ecommerce** * Understands **Indian audience behavior** * Can work with **low budgets smartly** * Communicates clearly (this matters a lot) --- ## 📝 To Apply (Mandatory) Answer these: 1. Share 1 ad campaign or store you’ve worked on 2. What would be your strategy to sell **budget refurbished laptops in India?** 3. Which platform would you focus on first and why? --- If you’ve read everything — thank you. That already tells me you’re serious. Let’s build something that actually works. — Founder, BitBrahma 🚀

Luxury tone, not mass-market. Can read analytics dashboards and explain what the numbers mean in plain language. Reliable daily turnaround. We post every day; you need to deliver every day. Nice to have Background in home décor, furniture, lighting, or interior design niches. Pinterest SEO experience (this is a big channel for us). Basic Photoshop / Figma for occasional custom graphics. Familiarity with Shopify content and GEO (Generative Engine Optimization). How to apply Send us: Links to 2–3 brand accounts you've managed, with the dates you managed them. description: WonderMe (wonderme.com) makes personalized AI portrait canvases and blankets — gifts people actually cry over. We're scaling fast and our biggest bottleneck is shipping speed on the storefront. We're looking for a Shopify developer who builds at a different tempo than the rest of the market. The reason that's possible now is Claude Code — and we want someone who has already rewired their workflow around it, not someone who's "open to trying it." This starts as a paid trial project. If you're the operator we think you are, it becomes ongoing or full-time within 2–3 weeks. What you'll do Build full custom Shopify themes from Figma → live store (not Dawn reskins — actual ground-up builds) Own performance: Core Web Vitals in the green, every build, no exceptions Ship sections, components, and conversion experiments on a near-daily cadence Work directly with our founder and designer — no PMs, no tickets, no Jira theater Must-haves (do not apply without these) Proven Claude Code proficiency. You have a CLAUDE.md you've iterated on. You know what subagents are for. You don't paste code back and forth into a chat window. Shopify Liquid + theme architecture mastery. Sections, blocks, metaobjects, theme settings, app blocks — fluent, not Googling. Performance obsession. You can talk LCP, CLS, INP without a cheat sheet. You know why Shopify's image CDN params matter. Strong taste. You've seen good DTC sites and bad ones and can tell the difference instantly. Nice-to-haves: Hydrogen / headless experience Klaviyo, Judge.me, Rebuy, common DTC stack integrations Past work with print-on-demand or personalized product flows (our fulfillment is non-trivial) Rate & engagement $25–50/hr depending on what you bring (lean toward the top of that band if your screening submission is strong) Trial project: 1–2 weeks, paid hourly If it works: ongoing contract or full-time, your call Bonuses for fast and accurate work. TO APPLY Loom walkthrough (5–10 min) of a recent Shopify build where you used Claude Code. Show us the actual workflow — how you prompt, how you structure the repo, where Claude saves you time, where you still drive manually. Live Shopify store links you've built or substantially customized. Tell us which parts are yours. Your CLAUDE.md (or equivalent context/workflow setup). Paste it, gist it, however. We want to see how you've configured your environment. Confirm you're available for a paid 2-hour micro-task as the final step — you'll build one section to a Figma spec. Paid regardless of outcome.

description: About the Role: Our digital agency is expanding and seeking a talented SEO & GEO Specialist to enhance our clients' search visibility across various sectors, including e-commerce, SaaS, B2B, and consumer brands. Based internationally, we serve companies throughout international markets, Europe, and the US. In this role, you will be responsible for developing organic search strategies that combine technical improvements, content optimization, and data-driven insights to drive consistent and compounding growth for a diverse portfolio of websites. Responsibilities: 1. Develop and execute effective SEO strategies: Enhance site visibility and improve search engine rankings for clients. 2. Technical SEO: Conduct site audits, prioritize fixes (including crawl budget, Core Web Vitals, structured data, indexation, and hreflang), and collaborate with development teams to implement these changes. 3. On-Page Optimization: Create keyword maps, revamp metadata, optimize internal linking, and ensure that content aligns with search intent and best practices for Answer Engine Optimization (AEO). 4. Content Strategy: Work with writers to build topic clusters that target Featured Snippets, People Also Ask (PAA) spots, and SGE citations. 5.O ff-Page & Digital PR: Plan and oversee link-building campaigns to strengthen domain authority while avoiding potential penalties. 6. Analytics & Reporting: Track performance using GA4, Looker Studio, and Search Console; translate data into clear roadmaps and monthly reports. 7. Tool Mastery: Utilize platforms such as Screaming Frog, Ahrefs, Semrush, and ChatGPT/LLMs to identify opportunities and automate repetitive tasks. 8. Cross-Team Collaboration: Coordinate with PPC, CRO, and creative teams to ensure that organic insights inform paid and social campaigns, and vice versa. 9. Trend Spotting: Monitor algorithm updates, schema changes, and AI-generated search experiences, adapting strategies proactively to stay ahead of competitors. Requirements: 1. 2+ years hands-on SEO experience (agency or in-house). 2. Proven wins in technical SEO and content-led growth. 3. Proficiency with Search Console, GA4, Screaming Frog, Ahrefs/Semrush, and log-file or server-log analysis. 4. Working knowledge of schema.org, Core Web Vitals, international SEO (hreflang), and mobile-first best practices. 5. Comfortable using LLMs/AI tools for keyword clustering, content briefs, or SERP analysis. 6. Strong analytical skills. 7. Excellent written and verbal communication in English. 8. HTML/CSS familiarity, experience with Shopify or WordPress, or a history of outranking household-name competitors. About Us: We are a trailblazing digital agency committed to helping brands achieve exceptional growth. We combine creativity, analytics, and strategy to craft impactful campaigns that drive measurable results. With a portfolio spanning international markets, Europe, and the US, we pride ourselves on our ability to adapt to diverse markets and deliver outstanding value to our clients. What We Offer: 1. Opportunity to work with a diverse and global client base. 2. A collaborative and supportive work environment. 3. Access to cutting-edge tools and resources to elevate your creative strategies. Ready to turn organic traffic into rocket fuel? Apply now and help our clients own the SERP.
title: SEO & GEO Specialist

description: I have a Hetzner VPS with OpenClaw 2026.4.15 already installed. WhatsApp is linked and Discord is partially configured, but I am not satisfied with the current communication setup – it feels unreliable and not best-practice. I need an experienced OpenClaw specialist to redo and optimize the entire communication layer and then build a professional, secure and highly autonomous multi-agent system for my Amazon FBA/private-label business. Core Requirements: 1. Full best-practice communication setup ○ Redo and optimize both WhatsApp (primary channel) and Discord (backup). ○ Make it stable, user-friendly and reliable for both text and voice notes. ○ I must be able to talk directly to the CEO agent and to each individual sub-agent. 2. 5 specialized sub-agents with strict human-in-the-loop ○ Research Agent ○ Supplier Agent ○ Launch Agent ○ Marketing Agent ○ CEO Agent (orchestrator) Important: Initially the agents must only draft all external communication (emails to suppliers, proposals, negotiations, etc.). They are not allowed to send anything without my explicit approval. When I later trust the system, I want a clear, documented process for gradually increasing their autonomy (stage-by-stage). 3. Best-practice SOUL.md + IDENTITY.md for all agents (professional, direct, honest, ROI-focused, no fluff). 4. Best-practice Mission Control / Control Center ○ I want a beautiful and pragmatic organization chart exactly like this example: https://youtube.com/shorts/Tr-RW09qcho?si=1sQ-54jIaQeDRBzN (Hierarchic tree with CEO at the top, colored agent nodes below, live status, clickable cards). ○ Clickable agent cards that open a live “virtual screen” / side panel when clicked. This panel must show: § What the agent is currently working on (live task + log) § Recent actions § Internal status / memory ○ Tabs: Marketing, Supply Chain, New Products Pipeline, Overall Status, Token Usage ○ Automatic 6-hour heartbeat reports 5. LLM model recommendations – suggest and configure the optimal mix (cost vs performance). 6. All essential tools & APIs must be installed and configured ○ Amazon SP-API (full integration for orders, inventory, listings, PPC, etc.) ○ Product research tools (Jungle Scout and/or Helium 10 API or best current alternatives – with programmatic access) ○ Dedicated email accounts for the agents (separate business emails or SMTP setup) so each agent can draft and (after my approval) send emails independently. ○ Any other best-practice tools needed for a fully autonomous Amazon workflow. 7. Future webshop development and autonomous management Over time the agents should be able to autonomously develop, launch and manage a dedicated webshop for me. Recommend the optimal platform and tools (e.g. Shopify, WooCommerce or best alternative in 2026) and integrate it fully with the agents for autonomous product creation, inventory sync, order processing, SEO, marketing, etc. Important pre-kickoff requirement: Before we start the actual work, please share with me examples or mockups of how the Mission Control / Control Center dashboard will look (including the organization chart style from the YouTube link above + clickable virtual screen), and explain your recommended best practices for it. I want to see and approve the design before any coding begins. Delivery method – Stage-Gate: We work in clear milestones. I will test each phase thoroughly before we move to the next. Milestones: • Milestone 1: Redo WhatsApp + Discord to best-practice + security hardening + SOUL/IDENTITY files + dedicated agent emails • Milestone 2: Create 5 sub-agents + approved Mission Control dashboard (with organization chart + clickable virtual screens) + heartbeat system • Milestone 3: Install all APIs/tools (Amazon SP-API + research tools) + draft-only email workflow + LLM configuration • Milestone 4: Full testing, documentation, handover session and training (including webshop roadmap) What I expect: • Proven experience with OpenClaw multi-agent setups (please share examples). • Strong focus on security, human-in-the-loop design and gradual autonomy. • Good English and clear communication. • Ability to work via Zoom screen-share. If you have built similar autonomous Amazon/agent systems before, please mention it in your proposal.
title: Openclaw setup for Amazon business

Who we are looking for We are looking for someone who is: Fluent in written English Clear, professional, and warm in communication Reliable and structured Detail-oriented Comfortable working with Google Sheets Comfortable using Notion or willing to learn it Able to communicate with suppliers clearly Able to handle difficult customer situations calmly Proactive and willing to think along with the business Available for a long-term collaboration Can work with standard templates for responses, and does this quickly. Experience with Shopify, Gmail, chargebacks, returns, or e-commerce customer support is a must. Why work with us Court Four is still early in its journey, but we are growing quickly and building something with real brand potential. You will not just be answering random support tickets, you will become part of the operational backbone of a premium lifestyle brand. We value people who are reliable, thoughtful, and want to grow with the company. If the collaboration goes well, this can become a long-term role with more responsibility over time. Important Please start your application with the word court so we know you read the full job post. In your application, please include: Your previous customer support experience Whether you have worked with Shopify before Whether you have experience with chargebacks Your experience with Google Sheets and Notion Your availability per week Why you think you would be a good fit for Court Four We are looking for someone who can help us create the kind of customer experience that matches a premium brand: warm, clear, professional, and trustworthy.

description: About Us Jod-Z is a premium girls' legwear brand, a specialist, not a generalist. We make high-performance leggings with silicone knee grips, squat-proof fabric, and secure pockets designed for girls who move. We've built a loyal customer base through organic traffic alone and we're now ready to scale. We have a clear vision and we need the right person to bring it to life on our Shopify store. The Vision: The Crossover Angle This is the heart of everything we are building. Jod-Z cannot just be horse riding leggings. The opportunity in front of us is to position Jod-Z as the go-to premium performance legwear brand for active girls, from the stable to the schoolyard, from the sports pitch to the playground, from pony club to casual Saturdays. This crossover angle is not a small tweak to the website. It is a complete shift in how we communicate, how we show the product, and who we are speaking to. Every single touchpoint on the site needs to reflect this expanded identity. A parent landing on our page should instantly understand that these leggings work for riding, yes, but also for every other part of her daughter's active life. That realisation is what justifies the premium price point and what turns a visitor into a buyer. We believe this crossover positioning is what will unlock serious growth. Getting it right on the website is the foundation everything else is built on. The Opportunity Our current conversion rate is significantly below industry standard and we know exactly why. This isn't a traffic problem. It's a persuasion problem. The copy is generic, imagery is flat, CTAs are inconsistent, and the crossover lifestyle angle is entirely absent. A specialist who fixes every touchpoint and truly understands and champions this crossover vision could realistically deliver a major revenue increase without increasing traffic. Key Deliverables Homepage repositioning. The hero section must lead with the crossover angle immediately. A parent who has never heard of horse riding leggings should land on this page and think "these are made for my daughter." New direction: "Ride. Run. Play. Premium legwear designed for every adventure." The crossover message must be unmissable from the first scroll. Product page overhaul. Transform bullet-point feature lists into benefit-led copy that speaks to every type of active girl, not just riders. Address real parent anxieties around slipping, transparency, washability, and value. The product descriptions should make a parent feel that these leggings belong in every part of her daughter's week. Visual integration strategy. The site currently lives entirely in the stable. We need it to live everywhere. Advise on and implement lifestyle imagery covering school runs, playground, sports training, and casual wear alongside the riding context. Each product colour should anchor to a different lifestyle moment, making the crossover feel natural and intentional rather than forced. UX and CTA standardisation. Audit and fix all CTAs site-wide. Replace any confusing text with clear, consistent actions: "Add to Cart," "Buy Now," "Shop Leggings." Streamline navigation and checkout flow so nothing interrupts a parent who is ready to buy. "Beyond the Saddle" section. This is a core piece of the crossover strategy. Design and build a dedicated homepage section or collection page that speaks directly to non-equestrian active girls covering dance, athletics, casual wear, and outdoor play. This section should feel like a natural extension of the brand, not an afterthought. Messaging: "Built for busy girls. Approved by busy parents." Ad-ready site cohesion. We will be driving Instagram and Facebook traffic shortly. Every page a paid visitor lands on must consistently reflect the crossover angle. The messaging, visuals, and flow must feel seamless from ad click to checkout. Must-Have Skills Proven Shopify experience with theme customisation, Liquid templating, and performance optimisation. Strong copywriting instinct for e-commerce that is benefit-led, conversion-focused, and brand-voice consistent. A UX and CRO background where you understand why users drop off and how to fix it. Experience with lifestyle, fashion, or children's product brands. The ability to audit an existing store and deliver a structured improvement plan, not just execute tasks. Attention to detail across every touchpoint. Nice to Have Experience with Meta ad-ready landing pages, familiarity with AI image generation tools, and previous CRO case studies with measurable conversion results. Phased Timeline Week 1: Full site audit. Rewrite homepage, collection pages, all product descriptions, and standardise every CTA with the crossover angle running through all of it. Week 2: Integrate lifestyle imagery and implement the riding and activewear crossover visuals throughout the site. Week 3: Launch the "Beyond the Saddle" section, complete the final pre-ads check, and hand off. To Apply, Please Include Two or three Shopify stores you have worked on, with before and after context where possible. One example of product copy you rewrote and the impact it had. Your read on our current site and how you would bring the crossover angle to life. Your proposed approach and rough timeline. Your fixed price or hourly rate for this project. Brand Direction for Reference Jod-Z: "Performance legwear for girls who do it all." We are not competing with cheap leggings. We are the specialist brand solving slipping, transparency, poor fit, lack of pockets, and lack of style in one premium product. Our expanded world covers riding, pony club, school runs, sports, playgrounds, and everyday fashion. The parent buying psychology centres on safety, durability, value, confidence, and washability. If a parent thinks "she'll live in these" we've won.
title: Shopify Developer & CRO Specialist

description: ⚠ READ THIS BEFORE YOU APPLY We are hiring multiple candidates — but we are extremely selective about who joins. Do not apply if: - You're looking for quick, easy money with minimal effort - You need constant supervision or hand-holding to do your job - You treat this as a side hustle or backup income stream - You disappear when things get busy or difficult - You're not prepared to own your results — good or bad - You're not in this for the long term We move fast, we hold high standards, and we don't have time to manage people who aren't fully committed. If that sounds intimidating — this role is not for you. If it sounds like exactly where you thrive — keep reading. About us We are a fast-growing backend operations partner for e-commerce brands and dropshipping stores. We don't just "handle support" — we take full ownership of the backend. From customer support and disputes to logistics, systems, and reporting — we make sure brands can scale without operational chaos. Why people love working here: - We're a young, driven team that genuinely invests in the people who show up. Strong performers move up fast — we promote from within, always. - You're not a cog here; your decisions have real impact on real businesses. Expect good energy, open communication, and zero corporate nonsense. And working hands-on with 6–7 figure stores will teach you more about e-commerce operations than any course ever could. - If you're ambitious and want to build a real career in e-commerce operations — this is the place to do it. Long-term opportunities This is not a dead-end job. Managers who perform well grow into senior roles, head of department positions (support, logistics, reporting, QA), and strategic roles as we continue to scale and build out our systems. We're building something serious — the people who grow with us now will have a real stake in where this goes. What you'll be responsible for As a Manager, you will oversee the full backend operations for multiple e-commerce stores. Your role includes: - Leading and managing a team of customer support agents - Monitoring ticket quality, response times, and overall performance - Ensuring smooth daily operations across all assigned stores - Handling escalations, complex cases, and problem-solving - Managing refunds, returns, and customer issues - Overseeing chargebacks and PayPal disputes - Communicating directly with clients via Slack - Monitoring orders, shipping, and supplier communication - Identifying operational bottlenecks and improving systems - Preparing daily and weekly reports with insights and recommendations - You are not just managing tasks — you are responsible for the performance and stability of the entire backend. Who we're looking for - Takes full ownership — no excuses, no finger-pointing - Proactive and solution-oriented by default - Excellent written communication (clear, structured, professional) - Reliable and consistent — day in, day out - Comfortable leading a team and giving direct feedback - Spots problems before they escalate and comes with solutions - Fast learner who actively seeks to improve To apply Send a message that includes: - Your experience in e-commerce / customer support - Any experience managing teams - Tools you've worked with (e.g. Shopify, Gorgias, etc.) - Why you think you're a good fit for this role Applications without a proper introduction will not be considered.
title: E-commerce Customer Support Manager Needed — MULTIPLE POSITIONS OPEN

description: We are The Butterfly Effect a brand expanding into the Spanish / Latin American market and we need someone who can take our Shopify store and ship it translated, configured, and ready to sell in one project. This is a hybrid role: native-level Spanish translator + Shopify specialist. We need both in one person, because handing it off between a translator and a Shopify ops freelancer always ends in a mess. What you'd own (one project, fixed scope): 1. Shopify Markets setup - Configure Spain as a new market (currency, language, domain/subfolder strategy) - Set up Shopify Translate & Adapt — but don't lean on machine translation - Shipping zones, rates, and carrier configuration for Spain 2. Payment gateways - Activate and test the right mix for Spanish buyers (where relevant) - Configure local payment methods we'd otherwise miss - Test full checkout end-to-end in Spanish, on mobile 3. Translation (native-quality, not Google Translate) - Full theme/store UI (navigation, footer, checkout, account pages) - All product titles + descriptions (we'll tell you which collections are priority) - Policies (returns, shipping, terms, privacy) — we'll provide source - Marketing copy needs to feel like it was written by a Spanish copywriter, not translated 4. QA + handoff - Mobile + desktop in Spanish - Loom walkthrough showing what you set up and how to maintain it What we're NOT looking for: - Pure translators with no Shopify Markets experience - Shopify devs who'll outsource the translation to Google or someone else. - Anyone whose first instinct is "I'll just use Translate & Adapt's auto-translate and call it done" - Generic "I localize websites" — we want Spain-specific knowledge (Bizum exists, IVA matters, return culture is different) Who you are: - Native or near-native Spanish (Spain — we'll discuss if you're Latin American but writing for Spain) - 2+ years Shopify experience, including Shopify Markets specifically - Have launched at least one DTC store in Spain before (we'll ask for proof) - Comfortable with payment gateway setup, tax configuration, and theme translation - Strong copywriting feel — fashion / lifestyle ideal Bonus: - Have done English ⇄ Spanish localization for fashion or beauty brands - Familiar with Klaviyo (we'll need email templates ported too) - Can advise on Spanish PR, influencer or affiliate landscape (not required, but a plus) - POV on Spanish ecommerce trends in 2026 Logistics: - Fixed-price project: budget €2,500-5,000 depending on scope (we'll align after a discovery call) - Timeline: 3-4 weeks from kickoff to live - Optional retainer after launch: €30-60/hour for ongoing product translations and updates as we add SKUs - Async work — Loom updates, Notion brief, Slack for urgent To apply: 1. Send a 60-90 second Loom: introduce yourself + show ONE Shopify store you've localized for Spain. What did you set up, what was tricky, what would you do differently. 2. In 1 sentence: what's the most common mistake non-Spanish brands make when localizing for Spain? 3. Confirm: are you a native Spain Spanish speaker, or Latin American? (Both can work, but be honest.) We respond to everyone who follows the format. Skip cover letters.
title: Shopify plus expert + Spanish translator — set up Latin American market end-to-end for DTC brand

description: Description: I need an experienced schema markup developer to fix, update and expand the JSON-LD structured data on my custom PHP eCommerce website at exoticcarparts.com. This is a parts retailer specializing in Rolls-Royce and Bentley automotive parts. This is NOT a WordPress or Shopify site. It is a custom PHP CMS. You must have experience with custom PHP websites. PART 1 — URGENT FIXES 1. Remove fake aggregateRating A previous developer added fake 5-star ratings to all product schema. This violates Google's structured data policies. Please remove the aggregateRating and review fields from ALL product pages site-wide immediately. This is the highest priority task. 2. Update priceValidUntil Change all product schema priceValidUntil dates from 2025-12-31 to 2027-12-31 across all product listings site-wide. 3. Fix Organisation schema postcode In the global Organisation JSON-LD schema that appears on every page change: "postalCode": "11212" to "postalCode": "11413" 4. Fix styles.css MIME type error Currently returning text/html instead of text/css — please fix server configuration. 5. Fix Tawk.to CORS error Live chat script is being blocked by CORS policy — please update embed code to latest version. 6. Fix FancyBox duplicate initialization FancyBox is loading twice on every page — please remove duplicate script include. PART 2 — NEW SCHEMA IMPLEMENTATION 7. BreadcrumbList Schema Implement BreadcrumbList schema on all product and category pages. Pages already display breadcrumbs visually — we need the schema to match so Google shows them in search results. 8. FAQPage Schema Implement FAQPage schema on all main catalogue pages — for example: Rolls-Royce Silver Dawn parts page Rolls-Royce Cullinan parts page Bentley Continental parts page Bentley Mulsanne parts page Each page should have 3 to 5 relevant FAQ questions and answers about the parts, fitment and shipping. 9. LocalBusiness Schema Replace the current Organisation schema with a full LocalBusiness schema including: Business name: Exotic Car Parts USA Address: 182-21 150th Avenue, Springfield Gardens, Queens, NY 11413 Phone: ------------------------------------------- Email: ---------------------------------------------------- Opening hours: 7am to 8pm, 7 days a week Business type: AutoPartsStore Geographic coordinates Service area: United States 10. Improved Offer Schema on all product pages Update existing product Offer schema to include: seller field identifying Exotic Car Parts USA sku field using the part number category field: Vehicle Parts & Accessories 11. Vehicle Schema Where possible implement Vehicle schema to describe the fitment relationship between parts and the vehicles they fit — for example linking a brake pad listing to the specific Bentley or Rolls-Royce model it fits. 12. Validate all schema After all changes please run a representative sample of pages through Google's Rich Results Test and Schema.org Validator and provide a report confirming no errors remain. BEFORE APPLYING PLEASE CONFIRM: You have experience with custom PHP websites — NOT WordPress or Shopify You have experience with JSON-LD schema markup You have experience with Apache and cPanel server configuration You can complete all tasks within 5 days Deliverables: All urgent fixes completed All new schema types implemented site-wide Validation report showing clean results Brief summary of all changes made
title: Fix & Implement Advanced JSON-LD Schema Markup on Custom PHP eCommerce Website

description: eCommerce Customer Service & Community Executive - Korean and English Location : Remote Type : Full-Time Salary : RM4500 - RM5500 *Probation period: 3 months Platform Focus: Instagram & Discord, eCommerce (Shopify) About Us HARUMIO is a fast-growing eCommerce platform dedicated to anything and everything Korean. We believe in building more than just a brand; we build a community. With a strong focus on Instagram for brand discovery and Discord for community connection, we strive to deliver an engaging, human, and rapid support experience to our users. Job Summary We are looking for a POLYGLOT, SPEAK/READ/WRITE IN ENGLISH AND KOREAN LANGUAGE, who is a proactive, tech-savvy Customer Service & Community Executive to be the face of our brand on Discord and Instagram. You will not only resolve customer queries regarding orders, shipping, and returns but also cultivate a positive, engaging atmosphere on our Discord server and manage our Instagram DMs/comments. The ideal candidate is comfortable with social media, loves chatting with people, and thrives in a fast-paced digital environment. Key Responsibilities 1. Discord Community Moderation & Support (60%) Active Engagement: Monitor and engage in Discord chat channels, fostering a welcoming atmosphere for new and existing members. Ticket Support: Manage incoming support tickets using Discord integration tools (e.g., Ticket Tool) to resolve customer order issues. Moderation: Enforce community guidelines to ensure a respectful environment, managing bots, and handling potential conflicts. Events & Updates: Support in hosting community events and disseminating company announcements, product drops, or updates to the server. Feedback Loop: Relay community sentiment and product feedback from Discord to the internal team. 2. Instagram DM & Social Engagement (30%) DM Management: Respond to customer questions via Instagram DMs, providing support on order tracking, product recommendations, and returns. Comment Monitoring: Monitor and reply to public comments on posts, reels, and stories in a prompt, friendly manner. Story Engagement: Engage with user-generated content (mentions, story tags) to build brand loyalty. 3. General E-commerce Support (10%) Backend Coordination: Coordinate with warehouse/shipping teams to resolve delivery issues. Record Keeping: Update systems (Shopify) with interaction logs from social channels. Required Skills & Qualifications Min. 3 years customer service platform expertise: Proven experience using Discord (server management/moderation) and Instagram (business accounts/DMs). Communication: Excellent written communication skills, with the ability to maintain a friendly, on-brand "human" voice. Problem-Solving: Strong ability to troubleshoot problems independently and think on your feet. E-commerce Savvy: Previous experience in eCommerce support (order management) is a plus. Empathy & Patience: Ability to manage difficult customers or high-volume chats with calmness. Time Management: Highly organized with the ability to multitask across different platforms. Work Schedule Working days: Monday - Sunday Rotational 5 days/week (including weekend and public holidays) Working hours: Rotational Shift (9am - 6pm, 3pm - 12am) (9 hour shifts including break/day)
title: eCommerce Customer Service & Community Executive - Korean and English

description: Looking for an Shopify expert to help us improving Judge.me Review Request rate and update our template. description: Compensation: $1,200–$2,000/month depending on experience + performance-based bonuses tied to conversion rate / AOV improvements Growth Path: - Increased compensation based on results - Opportunity to expand into broader DTC growth role - Long-term position as we scale Shopify aggressively ---------------------------------------- About Us ---------------------------------------- Tuff Pupper is a leading dog accessories brand doing ~$650k/month on Amazon with double-digit growth. We are now building out our Shopify (DTC) channel seriously. We already have a working Shopify site. We are NOT looking for a redesign. We are looking to improve and optimize what already exists. ---------------------------------------- Role Structure ---------------------------------------- This is a part-time role with flexible hours. Expected workload: - Approximately 15–25 hours per week depending on initiatives We care about results and improvements, not time tracking. ---------------------------------------- What We Need (Important) ---------------------------------------- We want someone who can take initiative and improve our site without needing constant direction. This includes: - Identifying opportunities to increase conversion rate - Improving average order value (bundles, upsells, cross-sells) - Enhancing customer experience - Recommending and implementing the right apps/tools You should tell us: - What should be improved - Why it matters - What it will look like when done - How it will impact revenue ---------------------------------------- Role Overview ---------------------------------------- This is NOT a task-based role. You are expected to: - Identify problems and opportunities - Propose solutions - Execute improvements - Measure results ---------------------------------------- Core Responsibilities ---------------------------------------- 1. Conversion Rate Optimization (CRO) - Improve product pages (layout, structure, trust elements) - Optimize mobile experience - Reduce friction in the buying process 2. AOV Growth (Critical) - Implement bundles (e.g., harness + leash sets) - “Shop the look” / product sets - Upsells and cross-sells - Cart and post-purchase offers 3. App & Feature Implementation - Recommend and install high-impact Shopify apps - Avoid unnecessary tools - Ensure clean integration with existing site 4. Landing Pages (Email Support) - Build simple, high-converting landing pages for Klaviyo campaigns - Align with promotions and product pushes 5. Continuous Improvement - Identify new opportunities regularly - Run small tests and iterate - Focus on revenue impact, not just design ---------------------------------------- Important Constraints ---------------------------------------- - DO NOT redesign the site - DO NOT overcomplicate with too many apps - Focus on improvements, not rebuilding ---------------------------------------- Bonus (Not Required) ---------------------------------------- - Experience in pet products / pet accessories - Experience in outdoor gear, tactical gear, or athletic products - Experience working with multi-product catalogs (bundles, sets, accessories) Additional bonus: - You are a dog owner or familiar with dog products These are helpful but not required. We prioritize strong CRO thinking and ability to drive results. ---------------------------------------- What Success Looks Like ---------------------------------------- - Conversion rate improves over time - Average order value increases - Site feels cleaner, easier to use, more convincing - Clear revenue impact from changes ---------------------------------------- Important ---------------------------------------- You must be self-directed. We are not looking to manage day-to-day tasks. You are expected to: - Identify what matters - Propose actions - Execute efficiently ---------------------------------------- To Apply ---------------------------------------- Please include: 1. 2–3 Shopify stores you have improved 2. Specific results (conversion rate, AOV, revenue impact) 3. Your expected monthly rate and why 4. Answer the following: A) What are 3 specific improvements you would make to a pet accessories Shopify store? B) How would you increase AOV for a brand selling harnesses, leashes, toys, and apparel? C) What apps/tools would you consider for bundles or “shop the look” and why? D) Give one example of a CRO improvement you made and the result ---------------------------------------- Hiring Process ---------------------------------------- Shortlisted candidates may be given a small paid task: - Review our site and propose 3 improvements with expected impact
title: Shopify CRO & Growth Operator (Pet Accessories / High-Growth Brand)

description: We are looking for an experienced accessibility expert or agency to help bring our Shopify website closer to WCAG compliance standards. This is NOT a basic plugin installation project. We are looking for a team with real accessibility experience, proper testing workflow, and the ability to provide a detailed final report. Scope of Work * Full accessibility audit of our Shopify website * Identify WCAG issues across desktop and mobile * Fix accessibility issues directly on the website * Manual + automated testing * Cross-browser testing * Keyboard navigation testing * Screen reader compatibility review * Final accessibility report/documentation after completion Requirements Please apply ONLY if you: * Have proven WCAG experience * Have worked on Shopify websites before * Can provide examples or case studies * Understand accessibility best practices beyond overlays/plugins * Can complete the project within 2 weeks When applying, please include: 1. Your estimated timeline 2. Estimated total pricing 3. Your testing process/workflow 4. Examples of past WCAG projects 5. What tools you use for accessibility testing 6. Whether testing is manual, automated, or both Preferred * Experience with WCAG 2.1 AA standards * Experience working with eCommerce brands * Ability to communicate clearly and provide organized reporting
title: WCAG Accessibility Expert Needed for Shopify Website (Audit + Remediation + Final Report)

description: Overview: I’ve purchased two ecommerce / AI training programs that provide step-by-step tasks to build and launch online stores. Stores are partially provided and completed. The issue is not strategy — it’s execution. I am not able to give the consistant time to this, leaving a vauable resource unused. I’m looking for someone who can reliably and consistantly follow cource instructions and complete tasks, effectively acting as the owner to move the business forward. What You’ll Do: Keep up to date with requirements and complete tasks from the structured programs. Liasse with teachers/mentors. Follow SOPs and video instructions. Use email/slack/whatsapp to communicate. Set up accounts etc as required. Report back with what was completed Flag blockers or unclear instructions that require my assistance. Maintain consistency and momentum - this is the most important part. What This Is NOT: Not a strategy or consulting role Not “tell me what to do” Requirements: Strong attention to detail - follow instructions exactly. Consistant availability, timing is not critical. Comfortable with ecommerce platforms Comfortable working with Slack, Loom. Comfortable with AI models. Good English for written communication, and understanding instructions. Ability to work independently and constructively without constant supervision Willing to learn from the provided courses and execute Nice to Have: Previous experience building ecommerce stores Familiarity with product research tools Basic copywriting skills Access & Tools: You will be given access to: Ecommerce training programs (paid courses) Store accounts and tools Dedicated email. Access will be structured and monitored for security Hours & Commitment: Ongoing role Consistant execution required. Hours unknown at present, could be 2 / day or 10-20 /week? I do not have unlimitted funds and so a weekly limit wil be in place. Flexible hours, but must complete assigned tasks on time. Whats in it for you? You’ll gain access to very expensive paid ecommerce training programs Opportunity to grow into ongoing management role if the outcome is successful. Potential for long-term work creating and managing multiple stores with profit share options. To Apply: Summary of ecommerce/store/AI experience Example of a project where you followed a system/process Your availability (days + hours per day) Final Note: This role is about consistency and execution. I am a creator who lacks time and patience for the daily tasks. I do not require a $50/hr specialist. I require an affordable solution to keep it all ticking along. If you’re someone who can deal with the small stuff and make some progress most days (even just 1 thing), be trustable with my information and reliably communicate in a professional manner - I'm waiting to hear from you :-)
